null && !netOutBuffer.completed()) {\\n374:             pendingSaslState = saslState;\\n375:             pendingException = exception;\\n376:         } else {\\n377:             this.saslState = saslState;\\n378:             LOG.debug(\\\"Set SASL server state to {} during {}\\\", saslState, reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText());\\n379:             this.pendingSaslState = null;\\n380:             this.pendingException = null;\\n381:             if (exception != null)\\n382:                 throw exception;\\n383:         }\\n384:     }\\n385: \\n386:     private boolean flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps() throws IOException {\\n387:         boolean flushedCompletely = flushNetOutBuffer();\\n388:         if (flushedCompletely) {\\n389:             transportLayer.removeInterestOps(SelectionKey.OP_WRITE);\\n390:             if (pendingSaslState != null)\\n391:                 setSaslState(pendingSaslState, pendingException);\\n392:         } else\\n393:             transportLayer.addInterestOps(SelectionKey.OP_WRITE);\\n394:         return flushedCompletely;\\n395:     }\\n396: \\n397:     private boolean flushNetOutBuffer() throws IOException {\\n398:         if (!netOutBuffer.completed())\\n399:             netOutBuffer.writeTo(transportLayer);\\n400:         return netOutBuffer.completed();\\n401:     }\\n402: \\n403:     private InetAddress serverAddress() {\\n404:         return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getLocalAddress();\\n405:     }\\n406: \\n407:     private InetAddress clientAddress() {\\n408:         return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getInetAddress();\\n409:     }\\n410: \\n411:     private void handleSaslToken(byte[] clientToken) throws IOException {\\n412:         if (!enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders) {\\n413:             byte[] response = saslServer.evaluateResponse(clientToken);\\n414:             if (saslServer.isComplete()) {\\n415:                 reauthInfo.calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs();\\n416:                 if (reauthInfo.reauthenticating())\\n417:                     reauthInfo.ensurePrincipalUnchanged(principal());\\n418:             }\\n419:             if (response != null) {\\n420:                 netOutBuffer = ByteBufferSend.sizePrefixed(ByteBuffer.wrap(response));\\n421:                 flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\\n422:             }\\n423:         } else {\\n424:             ByteBuffer requestBuffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(clientToken);\\n425:             RequestHeader header = RequestHeader.parse(requestBuffer);\\n426:             ApiKeys apiKey = header.apiKey();\\n427:             short version = header.apiVersion();\\n428:             RequestContext requestContext = new RequestContext(header, connectionId, clientAddress(),\\n429:                     KafkaPrincipal.ANONYMOUS, listenerName, securityProtocol, ClientInformation.EMPTY, false);\\n430:             RequestAndSize requestAndSize = requestContext.parseRequest(requestBuffer);\\n431:             if (apiKey != ApiKeys.SASL_AUTHENTICATE) {\\n432:                 IllegalSaslStateException e = new IllegalSaslStateException(\\\"Unexpected Kafka request of type \\\" + apiKey + \\\" during SASL authentication.\\\");\\n433:                 buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext, requestAndSize.request.getErrorResponse(e));\\n434:                 throw e;\\n435:             }\\n436:             if (!apiKey.isVersionSupported(version)) {\\n437:                 // We cannot create an error response if the request version of SaslAuthenticate is not supported\\n438:                 // This should not normally occur since clients typically check supported versions using ApiVersionsRequest\\n439:                 throw new UnsupportedVersionException(\\\"Version \\\" + version + \\\" is not supported for apiKey \\\" + apiKey);\\n440:             }\\n441:             /*\\n442:              * The client sends multiple SASL_AUTHENTICATE requests, and the client is known\\n443:              * to support the required version if any one of them indicates it supports that\\n444:              * version.\\n445:              */\\n446:             if (!reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication)\\n447:                 reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication = version > 0;\\n448:             SaslAuthenticateRequest saslAuthenticateRequest = (SaslAuthenticateRequest) requestAndSize.request;\\n449: \\n450:             try {\\n451:                 byte[] responseToken = saslServer.evaluateResponse(\\n452:                         Utils.copyArray(saslAuthenticateRequest.data().authBytes()));\\n453:                 if (reauthInfo.reauthenticating() && saslServer.isComplete())\\n454:                     reauthInfo.ensurePrincipalUnchanged(principal());\\n455:                 // For versions with SASL_AUTHENTICATE header, send a response to SASL_AUTHENTICATE request even if token is empty.\\n456:                 byte[] responseBytes = responseToken == null ? new byte[0] : responseToken;\\n457:                 long sessionLifetimeMs = !saslServer.isComplete() ? 0L\\n458:                         : reauthInfo.calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs();\\n459:                 sendKafkaResponse(requestContext, new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\\n460:                         new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\\n461:                         .setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code())\\n462:                         .setAuthBytes(responseBytes)\\n463:                         .setSessionLifetimeMs(sessionLifetimeMs)));\\n464:             } catch (SaslAuthenticationException e) {\\n465:                 buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext,\\n466:                         new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\\n467:                                 new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\\n468:                                 .setErrorCode(Errors.SASL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.code())\\n469:                                 .setErrorMessage(e.getMessage())));\\n470:                 throw e;\\n471:             } catch (SaslException e) {\\n472:                 KerberosError kerberosError = KerberosError.fromException(e);\\n473:                 if (kerberosError != null && kerberosError.retriable()) {\\n474:                     // Handle retriable Kerberos exceptions as I/O exceptions rather than authentication exceptions\\n475:                     throw e;\\n476:                 } else {\\n477:                     // DO NOT include error message from the `SaslException` in the client response since it may\\n478:                     // contain sensitive data like the existence of the user.\\n479:                     String errorMessage = \\\"Authentication failed during \\\"\\n480:                             + reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText()\\n481:                             + \\\" due to invalid credentials with SASL mechanism \\\" + saslMechanism;\\n482:                     buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext, new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\\n483:                             new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\\n484:                             .setErrorCode(Errors.SASL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.code())\\n485:                             .setErrorMessage(errorMessage)));\\n486:                     throw new SaslAuthenticationException(errorMessage, e);\\n487:                 }\\n488:             }\\n489:         }\\n490:     }\\n491: \\n492:     private boolean handleKafkaRequest(byte[] requestBytes) throws IOException, AuthenticationException {\\n493:         boolean isKafkaRequest = false;\\n494:         String clientMechanism = null;\\n495:         try {\\n496:             ByteBuffer requestBuffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(requestBytes);\\n497:             RequestHeader header = RequestHeader.parse(requestBuffer);\\n498:             ApiKeys apiKey = header.apiKey();\\n499: \\n500:             // A valid Kafka request header was received. SASL authentication tokens are now expected only\\n501:             // following a SaslHandshakeRequest since this is not a GSSAPI client token from a Kafka 0.9.0.x client.\\n502:             if (saslState == SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST)\\n503:                 setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST);\\n504:             isKafkaRequest = true;\\n505: \\n506:             // Raise an error prior to parsing if the api cannot be handled at this layer. This avoids\\n507:             // unnecessary exposure to some of the more complex schema types.\\n508:             if (apiKey != ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS && apiKey != ApiKeys.SASL_HANDSHAKE)\\n509:                 throw new IllegalSaslStateException(\\\"Unexpected Kafka request of type \\\" + apiKey + \\\" during SASL handshake.\\\");\\n510: \\n511:             LOG.debug(\\\"Handling Kafka request {} during {}\\\", apiKey, reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText());\\n512: \\n513: \\n514:             RequestContext requestContext = new RequestContext(header, connectionId, clientAddress(),\\n515:                     KafkaPrincipal.ANONYMOUS, listenerName, securityProtocol, ClientInformation.EMPTY, false);\\n516:             RequestAndSize requestAndSize = requestContext.parseRequest(requestBuffer);\\n517:             if (apiKey == ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS)\\n518:                 handleApiVersionsRequest(requestContext, (ApiVersionsRequest) requestAndSize.request);\\n519:             else\\n520:                 clientMechanism = handleHandshakeRequest(requestContext, (SaslHandshakeRequest) requestAndSize.request);\\n521:         } catch (InvalidRequestException e) {\\n522:             if (saslState == SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST) {\\n523:                 // InvalidRequestException is thrown if the request is not in Kafka format or if the API key\\n524:                 // is invalid. For compatibility with 0.9.0.x where the first packet is a GSSAPI token\\n525:                 // starting with 0x60, revert to GSSAPI for both these exceptions.\\n526:                 if (LOG.isDebugEnabled()) {\\n527:                     StringBuilder tokenBuilder = new StringBuilder();\\n528:                     for (byte b : requestBytes) {\\n529:                         tokenBuilder.append(String.format(\\\"%02x\\\", b));\\n530:                         if (tokenBuilder.length() >= 20)\\n531:                              break;\\n532:                     }\\n533:                     LOG.debug(\\\"Received client packet of length {} starting with bytes 0x{}, process as GSSAPI packet\\\", requestBytes.length, tokenBuilder);\\n534:                 }\\n535:                 if (enabledMechanisms.contains(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM)) {\\n536:                     LOG.debug(\\\"First client packet is not a SASL mechanism request, using default mechanism GSSAPI\\\");\\n537:                     clientMechanism = SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM;\\n538:                 } else\\n539:                     throw new UnsupportedSaslMechanismException(\\\"Exception handling first SASL packet from client, GSSAPI is not supported by server\\\", e);\\n540:             } else\\n541:                 throw e;\\n542:         }\\n543:         if (clientMechanism != null && (!reauthInfo.reauthenticating()\\n544:                 || reauthInfo.saslMechanismUnchanged(clientMechanism))) {\\n545:             createSaslServer(clientMechanism);\\n546:             setSaslState(SaslState.AUTHENTICATE);\\n547:         }\\n548:         return isKafkaRequest;\\n549:     }\\n550: \\n551:     private String handleHandshakeRequest(RequestContext context, SaslHandshakeRequest handshakeRequest) throws IOException, UnsupportedSaslMechanismException {\\n552:         String clientMechanism = handshakeRequest.data().mechanism();\\n553:         short version = context.header.apiVersion();\\n554:         if (version >= 1)\\n555:             this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(true);\\n556:         if (enabledMechanisms.contains(clientMechanism)) {\\n557:             LOG.debug(\\\"Using SASL mechanism '{}' provided by client\\\", clientMechanism);\\n558:             sendKafkaResponse(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\\n559:                     new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\\n560:             return clientMechanism;\\n561:         } else {\\n562:             LOG.debug(\\\"SASL mechanism '{}' requested by client is not supported\\\", clientMechanism);\\n563:             buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\\n564:                     new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.UNSUPPORTED_SASL_MECHANISM.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\\n565:             throw new UnsupportedSaslMechanismException(\\\"Unsupported SASL mechanism \\\" + clientMechanism);\\n566:         }\\n567:     }\\n568: \\n569:     // Visible to override for testing\\n570:     protected void enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(boolean flag) {\\n571:         this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders = flag;\\n572:     }\\n573: \\n574:     private void handleApiVersionsRequest(RequestContext context, ApiVersionsRequest apiVersionsRequest) throws IOException {\\n575:         if (saslState != SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST)\\n576:             throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"Unexpected ApiVersions request received during SASL authentication state \\\" + saslState);\\n577: \\n578:         if (apiVersionsRequest.hasUnsupportedRequestVersion())\\n579:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.UNSUPPORTED_VERSION.exception()));\\n580:         else if (!apiVersionsRequest.isValid())\\n581:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.INVALID_REQUEST.exception()));\\n582:         else {\\n583:             metadataRegistry.registerClientInformation(new ClientInformation(apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareName(),\\n584:                 apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareVersion()));\\n585:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionSupplier.get());\\n586:             setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_REQUEST);\\n587:         }\\n588:     }\\n589: \\n590:     /**\\n591:      * Build a {@link Send} response on {@link #authenticate()} failure. The actual response is sent out when\\n592:      * {@link #sendAuthenticationFailureResponse()} is called.\\n593:      */\\n594:     private void buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) {\\n595:         authenticationFailureSend = context.buildResponseSend(response);\\n596:     }\\n597: \\n598:     /**\\n599:      * Send any authentication failure response that may have been previously built.\\n600:      */\\n601:     private void sendAuthenticationFailureResponse() throws IOException {\\n602:         if (authenticationFailureSend == null)\\n603:             return;\\n604:         sendKafkaResponse(authenticationFailureSend);\\n605:         authenticationFailureSend = null;\\n606:     }\\n607: \\n608:     private void sendKafkaResponse(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) throws IOException {\\n609:         sendKafkaResponse(context.buildResponseSend(response));\\n610:     }\\n611: \\n612:     private void sendKafkaResponse(Send send) throws IOException {\\n613:         netOutBuffer = send;\\n614:         flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\\n615:     }\\n616: \\n617:     /**\\n618:      * Information related to re-authentication\\n619:      */\\n620:     private class ReauthInfo {\\n621:         public String previousSaslMechanism;\\n622:         public KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal;\\n623:         public long reauthenticationBeginNanos;\\n624:         public Long sessionExpirationTimeNanos;\\n625:         public boolean connectedClientSupportsReauthentication;\\n626:         public long authenticationEndNanos;\\n627:         public String badMechanismErrorMessage;\\n628: \\n629:         public void reauthenticating(String previousSaslMechanism, KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal,\\n630:                 long reauthenticationBeginNanos) {\\n631:             this.previousSaslMechanism = Objects.requireNonNull(previousSaslMechanism);\\n632:             this.previousKafkaPrincipal = Objects.requireNonNull(previousKafkaPrincipal);\\n633:             this.reauthenticationBeginNanos = reauthenticationBeginNanos;\\n634:         }\\n635: \\n636:         public boolean reauthenticating() {\\n637:             return previousSaslMechanism != null;\\n638:         }\\n639: \\n640:         public String authenticationOrReauthenticationText() {\\n641:             return reauthenticating() ? \\\"re-authentication\\\" : \\\"authentication\\\";\\n642:         }\\n643: \\n644:         public void ensurePrincipalUnchanged(KafkaPrincipal reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal) throws SaslAuthenticationException {\\n645:             if (!previousKafkaPrincipal.equals(reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal)) {\\n646:                 throw new SaslAuthenticationException(String.format(\\n647:                         \\\"Cannot change principals during re-authentication from %s.%s: %s.%s\\\",\\n648:                         previousKafkaPrincipal.getPrincipalType(), previousKafkaPrincipal.getName(),\\n649:                         reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal.getPrincipalType(), reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal.getName()));\\n650:             }\\n651:         }\\n652: \\n653:         /*\\n654:          * We define the REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM state because the failed re-authentication\\n655:          * metric does not get updated if we send back an error immediately upon the\\n656:          * start of re-authentication.\\n657:          */\\n658:         public boolean saslMechanismUnchanged(String clientMechanism) {\\n659:             if (previousSaslMechanism.equals(clientMechanism))\\n660:                 return true;\\n661:             badMechanismErrorMessage = String.format(\\n662:                     \\\"SASL mechanism '%s' requested by client is not supported for re-authentication of mechanism '%s'\\\",\\n663:                     clientMechanism, previousSaslMechanism);\\n664:             LOG.debug(badMechanismErrorMessage);\\n665:             setSaslState(SaslState.REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM);\\n666:             return false;\\n667:         }\\n668: \\n669:         private long calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs() {\\n670:             long retvalSessionLifetimeMs = 0L;\\n671:             long authenticationEndMs = time.milliseconds();\\n672:             authenticationEndNanos = time.nanoseconds();\\n673:             Long credentialExpirationMs = (Long) saslServer\\n674:                     .getNegotiatedProperty(SaslInternalConfigs.CREDENTIAL_LIFETIME_MS_SASL_NEGOTIATED_PROPERTY_KEY);\\n675:             Long connectionsMaxReauthMs = connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism.get(saslMechanism);\\n676:             if (credentialExpirationMs != null || connectionsMaxReauthMs != null) {\\n677:                 if (credentialExpirationMs == null)\\n678:                     retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(connectionsMaxReauthMs);\\n679:                 else if (connectionsMaxReauthMs == null)\\n680:                     retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs);\\n681:                 else\\n682:                     retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(\\n683:                             Math.min(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs, connectionsMaxReauthMs));\\n684:                 if (retvalSessionLifetimeMs > 0L)\\n685:                     sessionExpirationTimeNanos = authenticationEndNanos + 1000 * 1000 * retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\\n686:             }\\n687:             if (credentialExpirationMs != null) {\\n688:                 if (sessionExpirationTimeNanos != null)\\n689:                     LOG.debug(\\n690:                             \\\"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, credential expiration={} ({} ms); session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\\\",\\n691:                             connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(credentialExpirationMs),\\n692:                             credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs,\\n693:                             new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs), retvalSessionLifetimeMs,\\n694:                             retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\\n695:                 else\\n696:                     LOG.debug(\\n697:                             \\\"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, credential expiration={} ({} ms); no session expiration, sending 0 ms to client\\\",\\n698:                             connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(credentialExpirationMs),\\n699:                             credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs);\\n700:             } else {\\n701:                 if (sessionExpirationTimeNanos != null)\\n702:                     LOG.debug(\\n703:                             \\\"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\\\",\\n704:                             connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs),\\n705:                             retvalSessionLifetimeMs, retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\\n706:                 else\\n707:                     LOG.debug(\\n708:                             \\\"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; no session expiration, sending 0 ms to client\\\",\\n709:                             connectionsMaxReauthMs);\\n710:             }\\n711:             return retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\\n712:         }\\n713: \\n714:         public Long reauthenticationLatencyMs() {\\n715:             if (!reauthenticating())\\n716:                 return null;\\n717:             // record at least 1 ms if there is some latency\\n718:             long latencyNanos = authenticationEndNanos - reauthenticationBeginNanos;\\n719:             return latencyNanos == 0L ? 0L : Math.max(1L, Math.round(latencyNanos / 1000.0 / 1000.0));\\n720:         }\\n721: \\n722:         private long zeroIfNegative(long value) {\\n723:             return Math.max(0L, value);\\n724:         }        \\n725:     }\\n726: }\\n727: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sourcegraph-testing/kafka@a61b998c5df7ca9baccc5d966cb1e95108cbdee9/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n6:  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8:  *\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10:  *\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: package org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator;\n18: \n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SaslConfigs;\n21: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.internals.BrokerSecurityConfigs;\n22: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException;\n23: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.IllegalSaslStateException;\n24: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidRequestException;\n25: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.SaslAuthenticationException;\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedSaslMechanismException;\n27: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException;\n28: import org.apache.kafka.common.message.SaslAuthenticateResponseData;\n29: import org.apache.kafka.common.message.SaslHandshakeResponseData;\n30: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.Authenticator;\n31: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ByteBufferSend;\n32: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ChannelBuilders;\n33: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ChannelMetadataRegistry;\n34: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ClientInformation;\n35: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ListenerName;\n36: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.NetworkReceive;\n37: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ReauthenticationContext;\n38: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.Send;\n39: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.SslTransportLayer;\n40: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransportLayer;\n41: import org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.ApiKeys;\n42: import org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.Errors;\n43: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.AbstractResponse;\n44: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.ApiVersionsRequest;\n45: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.ApiVersionsResponse;\n46: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.RequestAndSize;\n47: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.RequestContext;\n48: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.RequestHeader;\n49: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslAuthenticateRequest;\n50: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslAuthenticateResponse;\n51: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslHandshakeRequest;\n52: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslHandshakeResponse;\n53: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.AuthenticateCallbackHandler;\n54: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipal;\n55: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipalBuilder;\n56: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipalSerde;\n57: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.SaslAuthenticationContext;\n58: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.SecurityProtocol;\n59: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.kerberos.KerberosError;\n60: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.kerberos.KerberosName;\n61: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.kerberos.KerberosShortNamer;\n62: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.ScramLoginModule;\n63: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals.ScramMechanism;\n64: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\n65: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n66: import org.slf4j.Logger;\n67: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n68: \n69: import javax.net.ssl.SSLSession;\n70: import javax.security.auth.Subject;\n71: import javax.security.sasl.Sasl;\n72: import javax.security.sasl.SaslException;\n73: import javax.security.sasl.SaslServer;\n74: import java.io.Closeable;\n75: import java.io.IOException;\n76: import java.net.InetAddress;\n77: import java.nio.ByteBuffer;\n78: import java.nio.channels.SelectionKey;\n79: import java.security.PrivilegedActionException;\n80: import java.security.PrivilegedExceptionAction;\n81: import java.util.ArrayList;\n82: import java.util.Date;\n83: import java.util.HashSet;\n84: import java.util.List;\n85: import java.util.Map;\n86: import java.util.Objects;\n87: import java.util.Optional;\n88: import java.util.function.Supplier;\n89: \n90: public class SaslServerAuthenticator implements Authenticator {\n91:     // GSSAPI limits requests to 64K, but we allow a bit extra for custom SASL mechanisms\n92:     static final int MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE = 524288;\n93:     private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(SaslServerAuthenticator.class);\n94: \n95:     /**\n96:      * The internal state transitions for initial authentication of a channel on the\n97:      * server side are declared in order, starting with {@link #INITIAL_REQUEST} and\n98:      * ending in either {@link #COMPLETE} or {@link #FAILED}.\n99:      * <p>\n100:      * Re-authentication of a channel on the server side starts with the state\n101:      * {@link #REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE}. It may then flow to\n102:      * {@link #REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM} before a transition to {@link #FAILED} if\n103:      * re-authentication is attempted with a mechanism different than the original\n104:      * one; otherwise it joins the authentication flow at the {@link #AUTHENTICATE}\n105:      * state and likewise ends at either {@link #COMPLETE} or {@link #FAILED}.\n106:      */\n107:     private enum SaslState {\n108:         INITIAL_REQUEST,               // May be GSSAPI token, SaslHandshake or ApiVersions for authentication\n109:         HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST, // May be SaslHandshake or ApiVersions\n110:         HANDSHAKE_REQUEST,             // After an ApiVersions request, next request must be SaslHandshake\n111:         AUTHENTICATE,                  // Authentication tokens (SaslHandshake v1 and above indicate SaslAuthenticate headers)\n112:         COMPLETE,                      // Authentication completed successfully\n113:         FAILED,                        // Authentication failed\n114:         REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE,      // Initial state for re-authentication, processes SASL handshake request\n115:         REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM,          // When re-authentication requested with wrong mechanism, generate exception\n116:     }\n117: \n118:     private final SecurityProtocol securityProtocol;\n119:     private final ListenerName listenerName;\n120:     private final String connectionId;\n121:     private final Map<String, Subject> subjects;\n122:     private final TransportLayer transportLayer;\n123:     private final List<String> enabledMechanisms;\n124:     private final Map<String, ?> configs;\n125:     private final KafkaPrincipalBuilder principalBuilder;\n126:     private final Map<String, AuthenticateCallbackHandler> callbackHandlers;\n127:     private final Map<String, Long> connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism;\n128:     private final Time time;\n129:     private final ReauthInfo reauthInfo;\n130:     private final ChannelMetadataRegistry metadataRegistry;\n131:     private final Supplier<ApiVersionsResponse> apiVersionSupplier;\n132: \n133:     // Current SASL state\n134:     private SaslState saslState = SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST;\n135:     // Next SASL state to be set when outgoing writes associated with the current SASL state complete\n136:     private SaslState pendingSaslState = null;\n137:     // Exception that will be thrown by `authenticate()` when SaslState is set to FAILED after outbound writes complete\n138:     private AuthenticationException pendingException = null;\n139:     private SaslServer saslServer;\n140:     private String saslMechanism;\n141: \n142:     // buffers used in `authenticate`\n143:     private NetworkReceive netInBuffer;\n144:     private Send netOutBuffer;\n145:     private Send authenticationFailureSend = null;\n146:     // flag indicating if sasl tokens are sent as Kafka SaslAuthenticate request/responses\n147:     private boolean enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders;\n148: \n149:     public SaslServerAuthenticator(Map<String, ?> configs,\n150:                                    Map<String, AuthenticateCallbackHandler> callbackHandlers,\n151:                                    String connectionId,\n152:                                    Map<String, Subject> subjects,\n153:                                    KerberosShortNamer kerberosNameParser,\n154:                                    ListenerName listenerName,\n155:                                    SecurityProtocol securityProtocol,\n156:                                    TransportLayer transportLayer,\n157:                                    Map<String, Long> connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism,\n158:                                    ChannelMetadataRegistry metadataRegistry,\n159:                                    Time time,\n160:                                    Supplier<ApiVersionsResponse> apiVersionSupplier) {\n161:         this.callbackHandlers = callbackHandlers;\n162:         this.connectionId = connectionId;\n163:         this.subjects = subjects;\n164:         this.listenerName = listenerName;\n165:         this.securityProtocol = securityProtocol;\n166:         this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders = false;\n167:         this.transportLayer = transportLayer;\n168:         this.connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism = connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism;\n169:         this.time = time;\n170:         this.reauthInfo = new ReauthInfo();\n171:         this.metadataRegistry = metadataRegistry;\n172:         this.apiVersionSupplier = apiVersionSupplier;\n173: \n174:         this.configs = configs;\n175:         @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n176:         List<String> enabledMechanisms = (List<String>) this.configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG);\n177:         if (enabledMechanisms == null || enabledMechanisms.isEmpty())\n178:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"No SASL mechanisms are enabled\");\n179:         this.enabledMechanisms = new ArrayList<>(new HashSet<>(enabledMechanisms));\n180:         for (String mechanism : this.enabledMechanisms) {\n181:             if (!callbackHandlers.containsKey(mechanism))\n182:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Callback handler not specified for SASL mechanism \" + mechanism);\n183:             if (!subjects.containsKey(mechanism))\n184:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Subject cannot be null for SASL mechanism \" + mechanism);\n185:             LOG.trace(\"{} for mechanism={}: {}\", BrokerSecurityConfigs.CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS, mechanism,\n186:                     connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism.get(mechanism));\n187:         }\n188: \n189:         // Note that the old principal builder does not support SASL, so we do not need to pass the\n190:         // authenticator or the transport layer\n191:         this.principalBuilder = ChannelBuilders.createPrincipalBuilder(configs, kerberosNameParser, null);\n192:     }\n193: \n194:     private void createSaslServer(String mechanism) throws IOException {\n195:         this.saslMechanism = mechanism;\n196:         Subject subject = subjects.get(mechanism);\n197:         final AuthenticateCallbackHandler callbackHandler = callbackHandlers.get(mechanism);\n198:         if (mechanism.equals(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM)) {\n199:             saslServer = createSaslKerberosServer(callbackHandler, configs, subject);\n200:         } else {\n201:             try {\n202:                 saslServer = Subject.doAs(subject, (PrivilegedExceptionAction<SaslServer>) () ->\n203:                     Sasl.createSaslServer(saslMechanism, \"kafka\", serverAddress().getHostName(), configs, callbackHandler));\n204:                 if (saslServer == null) {\n205:                     throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication with server mechanism \" + saslMechanism);\n206:                 }\n207:             } catch (PrivilegedActionException e) {\n208:                 throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication with server mechanism \" + saslMechanism, e.getCause());\n209:             }\n210:         }\n211:     }\n212: \n213:     private SaslServer createSaslKerberosServer(final AuthenticateCallbackHandler saslServerCallbackHandler, final Map<String, ?> configs, Subject subject) throws IOException {\n214:         // server is using a JAAS-authenticated subject: determine service principal name and hostname from kafka server's subject.\n215:         final String servicePrincipal = SaslClientAuthenticator.firstPrincipal(subject);\n216:         KerberosName kerberosName;\n217:         try {\n218:             kerberosName = KerberosName.parse(servicePrincipal);\n219:         } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\n220:             throw new KafkaException(\"Principal has name with unexpected format \" + servicePrincipal);\n221:         }\n222:         final String servicePrincipalName = kerberosName.serviceName();\n223:         final String serviceHostname = kerberosName.hostName();\n224: \n225:         LOG.debug(\"Creating SaslServer for {} with mechanism {}\", kerberosName, saslMechanism);\n226: \n227:         try {\n228:             return Subject.doAs(subject, (PrivilegedExceptionAction<SaslServer>) () ->\n229:                     Sasl.createSaslServer(saslMechanism, servicePrincipalName, serviceHostname, configs, saslServerCallbackHandler));\n230:         } catch (PrivilegedActionException e) {\n231:             throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication\", e.getCause());\n232:         }\n233:     }\n234: \n235:     /**\n236:      * Evaluates client responses via `SaslServer.evaluateResponse` and returns the issued challenge to the client until\n237:      * authentication succeeds or fails.\n238:      *\n239:      * The messages are sent and received as size delimited bytes that consists of a 4 byte network-ordered size N\n240:      * followed by N bytes representing the opaque payload.\n241:      */\n242:     @SuppressWarnings(\"fallthrough\")\n243:     @Override\n244:     public void authenticate() throws IOException {\n245:         if (saslState != SaslState.REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE) {\n246:             if (netOutBuffer != null && !flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps())\n247:                 return;\n248: \n249:             if (saslServer != null && saslServer.isComplete()) {\n250:                 setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\n251:                 return;\n252:             }\n253: \n254:             // allocate on heap (as opposed to any socket server memory pool)\n255:             if (netInBuffer == null) netInBuffer = new NetworkReceive(MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE, connectionId);\n256: \n257:             netInBuffer.readFrom(transportLayer);\n258:             if (!netInBuffer.complete())\n259:                 return;\n260:             netInBuffer.payload().rewind();\n261:         }\n262:         byte[] clientToken = new byte[netInBuffer.payload().remaining()];\n263:         netInBuffer.payload().get(clientToken, 0, clientToken.length);\n264:         netInBuffer = null; // reset the networkReceive as we read all the data.\n265:         try {\n266:             switch (saslState) {\n267:                 case REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE:\n268:                 case HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST:\n269:                 case HANDSHAKE_REQUEST:\n270:                     handleKafkaRequest(clientToken);\n271:                     break;\n272:                 case REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM:\n273:                     throw new SaslAuthenticationException(reauthInfo.badMechanismErrorMessage);\n274:                 case INITIAL_REQUEST:\n275:                     if (handleKafkaRequest(clientToken))\n276:                         break;\n277:                     // For default GSSAPI, fall through to authenticate using the client token as the first GSSAPI packet.\n278:                     // This is required for interoperability with 0.9.0.x clients which do not send handshake request\n279:                 case AUTHENTICATE:\n280:                     handleSaslToken(clientToken);\n281:                     // When the authentication exchange is complete and no more tokens are expected from the client,\n282:                     // update SASL state. Current SASL state will be updated when outgoing writes to the client complete.\n283:                     if (saslServer.isComplete())\n284:                         setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\n285:                     break;\n286:                 default:\n287:                     break;\n288:             }\n289:         } catch (AuthenticationException e) {\n290:             // Exception will be propagated after response is sent to client\n291:             setSaslState(SaslState.FAILED, e);\n292:         } catch (Exception e) {\n293:             // In the case of IOExceptions and other unexpected exceptions, fail immediately\n294:             saslState = SaslState.FAILED;\n295:             LOG.debug(\"Failed during {}: {}\", reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText(), e.getMessage());\n296:             throw e;\n297:         }\n298:     }\n299: \n300:     @Override\n301:     public KafkaPrincipal principal() {\n302:         Optional<SSLSession> sslSession = transportLayer instanceof SslTransportLayer ?\n303:                 Optional.of(((SslTransportLayer) transportLayer).sslSession()) : Optional.empty();\n304:         SaslAuthenticationContext context = new SaslAuthenticationContext(saslServer, securityProtocol,\n305:                 clientAddress(), listenerName.value(), sslSession);\n306:         KafkaPrincipal principal = principalBuilder.build(context);\n307:         if (ScramMechanism.isScram(saslMechanism) && Boolean.parseBoolean((String) saslServer.getNegotiatedProperty(ScramLoginModule.TOKEN_AUTH_CONFIG))) {\n308:             principal.tokenAuthenticated(true);\n309:         }\n310:         return principal;\n311:     }\n312: \n313:     @Override\n314:     public Optional<KafkaPrincipalSerde> principalSerde() {\n315:         return principalBuilder instanceof KafkaPrincipalSerde ? Optional.of((KafkaPrincipalSerde) principalBuilder) : Optional.empty();\n316:     }\n317: \n318:     @Override\n319:     public boolean complete() {\n320:         return saslState == SaslState.COMPLETE;\n321:     }\n322: \n323:     @Override\n324:     public void handleAuthenticationFailure() throws IOException {\n325:         sendAuthenticationFailureResponse();\n326:     }\n327: \n328:     @Override\n329:     public void close() throws IOException {\n330:         if (principalBuilder instanceof Closeable)\n331:             Utils.closeQuietly((Closeable) principalBuilder, \"principal builder\");\n332:         if (saslServer != null)\n333:             saslServer.dispose();\n334:     }\n335: \n336:     @Override\n337:     public void reauthenticate(ReauthenticationContext reauthenticationContext) throws IOException {\n338:         NetworkReceive saslHandshakeReceive = reauthenticationContext.networkReceive();\n339:         if (saslHandshakeReceive == null)\n340:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n341:                     \"Invalid saslHandshakeReceive in server-side re-authentication context: null\");\n342:         SaslServerAuthenticator previousSaslServerAuthenticator = (SaslServerAuthenticator) reauthenticationContext.previousAuthenticator();\n343:         reauthInfo.reauthenticating(previousSaslServerAuthenticator.saslMechanism,\n344:                 previousSaslServerAuthenticator.principal(), reauthenticationContext.reauthenticationBeginNanos());\n345:         previousSaslServerAuthenticator.close();\n346:         netInBuffer = saslHandshakeReceive;\n347:         LOG.debug(\"Beginning re-authentication: {}\", this);\n348:         netInBuffer.payload().rewind();\n349:         setSaslState(SaslState.REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE);\n350:         authenticate();\n351:     }\n352: \n353:     @Override\n354:     public Long serverSessionExpirationTimeNanos() {\n355:         return reauthInfo.sessionExpirationTimeNanos;\n356:     }\n357: \n358:     @Override\n359:     public Long reauthenticationLatencyMs() {\n360:         return reauthInfo.reauthenticationLatencyMs();\n361:     }\n362: \n363:     @Override\n364:     public boolean connectedClientSupportsReauthentication() {\n365:         return reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication;\n366:     }\n367: \n368:     private void setSaslState(SaslState saslState) {\n369:         setSaslState(saslState, null);\n370:     }\n371: \n372:     private void setSaslState(SaslState saslState, AuthenticationException exception) {\n373:         if (netOutBuffer != null && !netOutBuffer.completed()) {\n374:             pendingSaslState = saslState;\n375:             pendingException = exception;\n376:         } else {\n377:             this.saslState = saslState;\n378:             LOG.debug(\"Set SASL server state to {} during {}\", saslState, reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText());\n379:             this.pendingSaslState = null;\n380:             this.pendingException = null;\n381:             if (exception != null)\n382:                 throw exception;\n383:         }\n384:     }\n385: \n386:     private boolean flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps() throws IOException {\n387:         boolean flushedCompletely = flushNetOutBuffer();\n388:         if (flushedCompletely) {\n389:             transportLayer.removeInterestOps(SelectionKey.OP_WRITE);\n390:             if (pendingSaslState != null)\n391:                 setSaslState(pendingSaslState, pendingException);\n392:         } else\n393:             transportLayer.addInterestOps(SelectionKey.OP_WRITE);\n394:         return flushedCompletely;\n395:     }\n396: \n397:     private boolean flushNetOutBuffer() throws IOException {\n398:         if (!netOutBuffer.completed())\n399:             netOutBuffer.writeTo(transportLayer);\n400:         return netOutBuffer.completed();\n401:     }\n402: \n403:     private InetAddress serverAddress() {\n404:         return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getLocalAddress();\n405:     }\n406: \n407:     private InetAddress clientAddress() {\n408:         return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getInetAddress();\n409:     }\n410: \n411:     private void handleSaslToken(byte[] clientToken) throws IOException {\n412:         if (!enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders) {\n413:             byte[] response = saslServer.evaluateResponse(clientToken);\n414:             if (saslServer.isComplete()) {\n415:                 reauthInfo.calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs();\n416:                 if (reauthInfo.reauthenticating())\n417:                     reauthInfo.ensurePrincipalUnchanged(principal());\n418:             }\n419:             if (response != null) {\n420:                 netOutBuffer = ByteBufferSend.sizePrefixed(ByteBuffer.wrap(response));\n421:                 flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\n422:             }\n423:         } else {\n424:             ByteBuffer requestBuffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(clientToken);\n425:             RequestHeader header = RequestHeader.parse(requestBuffer);\n426:             ApiKeys apiKey = header.apiKey();\n427:             short version = header.apiVersion();\n428:             RequestContext requestContext = new RequestContext(header, connectionId, clientAddress(),\n429:                     KafkaPrincipal.ANONYMOUS, listenerName, securityProtocol, ClientInformation.EMPTY, false);\n430:             RequestAndSize requestAndSize = requestContext.parseRequest(requestBuffer);\n431:             if (apiKey != ApiKeys.SASL_AUTHENTICATE) {\n432:                 IllegalSaslStateException e = new IllegalSaslStateException(\"Unexpected Kafka request of type \" + apiKey + \" during SASL authentication.\");\n433:                 buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext, requestAndSize.request.getErrorResponse(e));\n434:                 throw e;\n435:             }\n436:             if (!apiKey.isVersionSupported(version)) {\n437:                 // We cannot create an error response if the request version of SaslAuthenticate is not supported\n438:                 // This should not normally occur since clients typically check supported versions using ApiVersionsRequest\n439:                 throw new UnsupportedVersionException(\"Version \" + version + \" is not supported for apiKey \" + apiKey);\n440:             }\n441:             /*\n442:              * The client sends multiple SASL_AUTHENTICATE requests, and the client is known\n443:              * to support the required version if any one of them indicates it supports that\n444:              * version.\n445:              */\n446:             if (!reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication)\n447:                 reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication = version > 0;\n448:             SaslAuthenticateRequest saslAuthenticateRequest = (SaslAuthenticateRequest) requestAndSize.request;\n449: \n450:             try {\n451:                 byte[] responseToken = saslServer.evaluateResponse(\n452:                         Utils.copyArray(saslAuthenticateRequest.data().authBytes()));\n453:                 if (reauthInfo.reauthenticating() && saslServer.isComplete())\n454:                     reauthInfo.ensurePrincipalUnchanged(principal());\n455:                 // For versions with SASL_AUTHENTICATE header, send a response to SASL_AUTHENTICATE request even if token is empty.\n456:                 byte[] responseBytes = responseToken == null ? new byte[0] : responseToken;\n457:                 long sessionLifetimeMs = !saslServer.isComplete() ? 0L\n458:                         : reauthInfo.calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs();\n459:                 sendKafkaResponse(requestContext, new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n460:                         new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n461:                         .setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code())\n462:                         .setAuthBytes(responseBytes)\n463:                         .setSessionLifetimeMs(sessionLifetimeMs)));\n464:             } catch (SaslAuthenticationException e) {\n465:                 buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext,\n466:                         new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n467:                                 new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n468:                                 .setErrorCode(Errors.SASL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.code())\n469:                                 .setErrorMessage(e.getMessage())));\n470:                 throw e;\n471:             } catch (SaslException e) {\n472:                 KerberosError kerberosError = KerberosError.fromException(e);\n473:                 if (kerberosError != null && kerberosError.retriable()) {\n474:                     // Handle retriable Kerberos exceptions as I/O exceptions rather than authentication exceptions\n475:                     throw e;\n476:                 } else {\n477:                     // DO NOT include error message from the `SaslException` in the client response since it may\n478:                     // contain sensitive data like the existence of the user.\n479:                     String errorMessage = \"Authentication failed during \"\n480:                             + reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText()\n481:                             + \" due to invalid credentials with SASL mechanism \" + saslMechanism;\n482:                     buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext, new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n483:                             new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n484:                             .setErrorCode(Errors.SASL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.code())\n485:                             .setErrorMessage(errorMessage)));\n486:                     throw new SaslAuthenticationException(errorMessage, e);\n487:                 }\n488:             }\n489:         }\n490:     }\n491: \n492:     private boolean handleKafkaRequest(byte[] requestBytes) throws IOException, AuthenticationException {\n493:         boolean isKafkaRequest = false;\n494:         String clientMechanism = null;\n495:         try {\n496:             ByteBuffer requestBuffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(requestBytes);\n497:             RequestHeader header = RequestHeader.parse(requestBuffer);\n498:             ApiKeys apiKey = header.apiKey();\n499: \n500:             // A valid Kafka request header was received. SASL authentication tokens are now expected only\n501:             // following a SaslHandshakeRequest since this is not a GSSAPI client token from a Kafka 0.9.0.x client.\n502:             if (saslState == SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST)\n503:                 setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST);\n504:             isKafkaRequest = true;\n505: \n506:             // Raise an error prior to parsing if the api cannot be handled at this layer. This avoids\n507:             // unnecessary exposure to some of the more complex schema types.\n508:             if (apiKey != ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS && apiKey != ApiKeys.SASL_HANDSHAKE)\n509:                 throw new IllegalSaslStateException(\"Unexpected Kafka request of type \" + apiKey + \" during SASL handshake.\");\n510: \n511:             LOG.debug(\"Handling Kafka request {} during {}\", apiKey, reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText());\n512: \n513: \n514:             RequestContext requestContext = new RequestContext(header, connectionId, clientAddress(),\n515:                     KafkaPrincipal.ANONYMOUS, listenerName, securityProtocol, ClientInformation.EMPTY, false);\n516:             RequestAndSize requestAndSize = requestContext.parseRequest(requestBuffer);\n517:             if (apiKey == ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS)\n518:                 handleApiVersionsRequest(requestContext, (ApiVersionsRequest) requestAndSize.request);\n519:             else\n520:                 clientMechanism = handleHandshakeRequest(requestContext, (SaslHandshakeRequest) requestAndSize.request);\n521:         } catch (InvalidRequestException e) {\n522:             if (saslState == SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST) {\n523:                 // InvalidRequestException is thrown if the request is not in Kafka format or if the API key\n524:                 // is invalid. For compatibility with 0.9.0.x where the first packet is a GSSAPI token\n525:                 // starting with 0x60, revert to GSSAPI for both these exceptions.\n526:                 if (LOG.isDebugEnabled()) {\n527:                     StringBuilder tokenBuilder = new StringBuilder();\n528:                     for (byte b : requestBytes) {\n529:                         tokenBuilder.append(String.format(\"%02x\", b));\n530:                         if (tokenBuilder.length() >= 20)\n531:                              break;\n532:                     }\n533:                     LOG.debug(\"Received client packet of length {} starting with bytes 0x{}, process as GSSAPI packet\", requestBytes.length, tokenBuilder);\n534:                 }\n535:                 if (enabledMechanisms.contains(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM)) {\n536:                     LOG.debug(\"First client packet is not a SASL mechanism request, using default mechanism GSSAPI\");\n537:                     clientMechanism = SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM;\n538:                 } else\n539:                     throw new UnsupportedSaslMechanismException(\"Exception handling first SASL packet from client, GSSAPI is not supported by server\", e);\n540:             } else\n541:                 throw e;\n542:         }\n543:         if (clientMechanism != null && (!reauthInfo.reauthenticating()\n544:                 || reauthInfo.saslMechanismUnchanged(clientMechanism))) {\n545:             createSaslServer(clientMechanism);\n546:             setSaslState(SaslState.AUTHENTICATE);\n547:         }\n548:         return isKafkaRequest;\n549:     }\n550: \n551:     private String handleHandshakeRequest(RequestContext context, SaslHandshakeRequest handshakeRequest) throws IOException, UnsupportedSaslMechanismException {\n552:         String clientMechanism = handshakeRequest.data().mechanism();\n553:         short version = context.header.apiVersion();\n554:         if (version >= 1)\n555:             this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(true);\n556:         if (enabledMechanisms.contains(clientMechanism)) {\n557:             LOG.debug(\"Using SASL mechanism '{}' provided by client\", clientMechanism);\n558:             sendKafkaResponse(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\n559:                     new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\n560:             return clientMechanism;\n561:         } else {\n562:             LOG.debug(\"SASL mechanism '{}' requested by client is not supported\", clientMechanism);\n563:             buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\n564:                     new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.UNSUPPORTED_SASL_MECHANISM.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\n565:             throw new UnsupportedSaslMechanismException(\"Unsupported SASL mechanism \" + clientMechanism);\n566:         }\n567:     }\n568: \n569:     // Visible to override for testing\n570:     protected void enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(boolean flag) {\n571:         this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders = flag;\n572:     }\n573: \n574:     private void handleApiVersionsRequest(RequestContext context, ApiVersionsRequest apiVersionsRequest) throws IOException {\n575:         if (saslState != SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST)\n576:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Unexpected ApiVersions request received during SASL authentication state \" + saslState);\n577: \n578:         if (apiVersionsRequest.hasUnsupportedRequestVersion())\n579:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.UNSUPPORTED_VERSION.exception()));\n580:         else if (!apiVersionsRequest.isValid())\n581:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.INVALID_REQUEST.exception()));\n582:         else {\n583:             metadataRegistry.registerClientInformation(new ClientInformation(apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareName(),\n584:                 apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareVersion()));\n585:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionSupplier.get());\n586:             setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_REQUEST);\n587:         }\n588:     }\n589: \n590:     /**\n591:      * Build a {@link Send} response on {@link #authenticate()} failure. The actual response is sent out when\n592:      * {@link #sendAuthenticationFailureResponse()} is called.\n593:      */\n594:     private void buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) {\n595:         authenticationFailureSend = context.buildResponseSend(response);\n596:     }\n597: \n598:     /**\n599:      * Send any authentication failure response that may have been previously built.\n600:      */\n601:     private void sendAuthenticationFailureResponse() throws IOException {\n602:         if (authenticationFailureSend == null)\n603:             return;\n604:         sendKafkaResponse(authenticationFailureSend);\n605:         authenticationFailureSend = null;\n606:     }\n607: \n608:     private void sendKafkaResponse(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) throws IOException {\n609:         sendKafkaResponse(context.buildResponseSend(response));\n610:     }\n611: \n612:     private void sendKafkaResponse(Send send) throws IOException {\n613:         netOutBuffer = send;\n614:         flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\n615:     }\n616: \n617:     /**\n618:      * Information related to re-authentication\n619:      */\n620:     private class ReauthInfo {\n621:         public String previousSaslMechanism;\n622:         public KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal;\n623:         public long reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n624:         public Long sessionExpirationTimeNanos;\n625:         public boolean connectedClientSupportsReauthentication;\n626:         public long authenticationEndNanos;\n627:         public String badMechanismErrorMessage;\n628: \n629:         public void reauthenticating(String previousSaslMechanism, KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal,\n630:                 long reauthenticationBeginNanos) {\n631:             this.previousSaslMechanism = Objects.requireNonNull(previousSaslMechanism);\n632:             this.previousKafkaPrincipal = Objects.requireNonNull(previousKafkaPrincipal);\n633:             this.reauthenticationBeginNanos = reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n634:         }\n635: \n636:         public boolean reauthenticating() {\n637:             return previousSaslMechanism != null;\n638:         }\n639: \n640:         public String authenticationOrReauthenticationText() {\n641:             return reauthenticating() ? \"re-authentication\" : \"authentication\";\n642:         }\n643: \n644:         public void ensurePrincipalUnchanged(KafkaPrincipal reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal) throws SaslAuthenticationException {\n645:             if (!previousKafkaPrincipal.equals(reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal)) {\n646:                 throw new SaslAuthenticationException(String.format(\n647:                         \"Cannot change principals during re-authentication from %s.%s: %s.%s\",\n648:                         previousKafkaPrincipal.getPrincipalType(), previousKafkaPrincipal.getName(),\n649:                         reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal.getPrincipalType(), reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal.getName()));\n650:             }\n651:         }\n652: \n653:         /*\n654:          * We define the REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM state because the failed re-authentication\n655:          * metric does not get updated if we send back an error immediately upon the\n656:          * start of re-authentication.\n657:          */\n658:         public boolean saslMechanismUnchanged(String clientMechanism) {\n659:             if (previousSaslMechanism.equals(clientMechanism))\n660:                 return true;\n661:             badMechanismErrorMessage = String.format(\n662:                     \"SASL mechanism '%s' requested by client is not supported for re-authentication of mechanism '%s'\",\n663:                     clientMechanism, previousSaslMechanism);\n664:             LOG.debug(badMechanismErrorMessage);\n665:             setSaslState(SaslState.REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM);\n666:             return false;\n667:         }\n668: \n669:         private long calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs() {\n670:             long retvalSessionLifetimeMs = 0L;\n671:             long authenticationEndMs = time.milliseconds();\n672:             authenticationEndNanos = time.nanoseconds();\n673:             Long credentialExpirationMs = (Long) saslServer\n674:                     .getNegotiatedProperty(SaslInternalConfigs.CREDENTIAL_LIFETIME_MS_SASL_NEGOTIATED_PROPERTY_KEY);\n675:             Long connectionsMaxReauthMs = connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism.get(saslMechanism);\n676:             if (credentialExpirationMs != null || connectionsMaxReauthMs != null) {\n677:                 if (credentialExpirationMs == null)\n678:                     retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(connectionsMaxReauthMs);\n679:                 else if (connectionsMaxReauthMs == null)\n680:                     retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs);\n681:                 else\n682:                     retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(\n683:                             Math.min(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs, connectionsMaxReauthMs));\n684:                 if (retvalSessionLifetimeMs > 0L)\n685:                     sessionExpirationTimeNanos = authenticationEndNanos + 1000 * 1000 * retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\n686:             }\n687:             if (credentialExpirationMs != null) {\n688:                 if (sessionExpirationTimeNanos != null)\n689:                     LOG.debug(\n690:                             \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, credential expiration={} ({} ms); session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\",\n691:                             connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(credentialExpirationMs),\n692:                             credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs,\n693:                             new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs), retvalSessionLifetimeMs,\n694:                             retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\n695:                 else\n696:                     LOG.debug(\n697:                             \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, credential expiration={} ({} ms); no session expiration, sending 0 ms to client\",\n698:                             connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(credentialExpirationMs),\n699:                             credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs);\n700:             } else {\n701:                 if (sessionExpirationTimeNanos != null)\n702:                     LOG.debug(\n703:                             \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\",\n704:                             connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs),\n705:                             retvalSessionLifetimeMs, retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\n706:                 else\n707:                     LOG.debug(\n708:                             \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; no session expiration, sending 0 ms to client\",\n709:                             connectionsMaxReauthMs);\n710:             }\n711:             return retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\n712:         }\n713: \n714:         public Long reauthenticationLatencyMs() {\n715:             if (!reauthenticating())\n716:                 return null;\n717:             // record at least 1 ms if there is some latency\n718:             long latencyNanos = authenticationEndNanos - reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n719:             return latencyNanos == 0L ? 0L : Math.max(1L, Math.round(latencyNanos / 1000.0 / 1000.0));\n720:         }\n721: \n722:         private long zeroIfNegative(long value) {\n723:             return Math.max(0L, value);\n724:         }        \n725:     }\n726: }\n727: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sourcegraph-testing/kafka@a61b998c5df7ca9baccc5d966cb1e95108cbdee9/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements.
