License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.config.SaslConfigs;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.config.internals.BrokerSecurityConfigs;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.IllegalSaslStateException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidRequestException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.SaslAuthenticationException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedSaslMechanismException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SaslAuthenticateResponseData;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SaslHandshakeResponseData;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.InvalidReceiveException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.Authenticator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.ByteBufferSend;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.ChannelBuilders;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.ChannelMetadataRegistry;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.ClientInformation;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.ListenerName;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.NetworkReceive;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.ReauthenticationContext;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.Send;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.SslTransportLayer;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransportLayer;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.ApiKeys;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.Errors;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.AbstractResponse;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.ApiVersionsRequest;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.ApiVersionsResponse;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.RequestAndSize;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.RequestContext;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.RequestHeader;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslAuthenticateRequest;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslAuthenticateResponse;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslHandshakeRequest;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslHandshakeResponse;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.AuthenticateCallbackHandler;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipal;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipalBuilder;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipalSerde;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.SaslAuthenticationContext;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.SecurityProtocol;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.kerberos.KerberosError;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.kerberos.KerberosName;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.kerberos.KerberosShortNamer;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.ScramLoginModule;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals.ScramMechanism;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\nimport org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n\nimport javax.net.ssl.SSLSession;\nimport javax.security.auth.Subject;\nimport javax.security.sasl.Sasl;\nimport javax.security.sasl.SaslException;\nimport javax.security.sasl.SaslServer;\nimport java.io.Closeable;\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.net.InetAddress;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.nio.channels.SelectionKey;\nimport java.security.PrivilegedActionException;\nimport java.security.PrivilegedExceptionAction;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Date;\nimport java.util.HashSet;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Objects;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.function.Supplier;\n\npublic class SaslServerAuthenticator implements Authenticator {\n    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(SaslServerAuthenticator.class);\n\n    /**\n     * The internal state transitions for initial authentication of a channel on the\n     * server side are declared in order, starting with {@link #INITIAL_REQUEST} and\n     * ending in either {@link #COMPLETE} or {@link #FAILED}.\n     * <p>\n     * Re-authentication of a channel on the server side starts with the state\n     * {@link #REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE}. It may then flow to\n     * {@link #REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM} before a transition to {@link #FAILED} if\n     * re-authentication is attempted with a mechanism different than the original\n     * one; otherwise it joins the authentication flow at the {@link #AUTHENTICATE}\n     * state and likewise ends at either {@link #COMPLETE} or {@link #FAILED}.\n     */\n    private enum SaslState {\n        INITIAL_REQUEST,               // May be GSSAPI token, SaslHandshake or ApiVersions for authentication\n        HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST, // May be SaslHandshake or ApiVersions\n        HANDSHAKE_REQUEST,             // After an ApiVersions request, next request must be SaslHandshake\n        AUTHENTICATE,                  // Authentication tokens (SaslHandshake v1 and above indicate SaslAuthenticate headers)\n        COMPLETE,                      // Authentication completed successfully\n        FAILED,                        // Authentication failed\n        REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE,      // Initial state for re-authentication, processes SASL handshake request\n        REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM,          // When re-authentication requested with wrong mechanism, generate exception\n    }\n\n    private final SecurityProtocol securityProtocol;\n    private final ListenerName listenerName;\n    private final String connectionId;\n    private final Map<String, Subject> subjects;\n    private final TransportLayer transportLayer;\n    private final List<String> enabledMechanisms;\n    private final Map<String, ?> configs;\n    private final KafkaPrincipalBuilder principalBuilder;\n    private final Map<String, AuthenticateCallbackHandler> callbackHandlers;\n    private final Map<String, Long> connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism;\n    private final Time time;\n    private final ReauthInfo reauthInfo;\n    private final ChannelMetadataRegistry metadataRegistry;\n    private final Supplier<ApiVersionsResponse> apiVersionSupplier;\n\n    // Current SASL state\n    private SaslState saslState = SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST;\n    // Next SASL state to be set when outgoing writes associated with the current SASL state complete\n    private SaslState pendingSaslState = null;\n    // Exception that will be thrown by `authenticate()` when SaslState is set to FAILED after outbound writes complete\n    private AuthenticationException pendingException = null;\n    private SaslServer saslServer;\n    private String saslMechanism;\n\n    // buffers used in `authenticate`\n    private Integer saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize;\n    private NetworkReceive netInBuffer;\n    private Send netOutBuffer;\n    private Send authenticationFailureSend = null;\n    // flag indicating if sasl tokens are sent as Kafka SaslAuthenticate request/responses\n    private boolean enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders;\n\n    public SaslServerAuthenticator(Map<String, ?> configs,\n                                   Map<String, AuthenticateCallbackHandler> callbackHandlers,\n                                   String connectionId,\n                                   Map<String, Subject> subjects,\n                                   KerberosShortNamer kerberosNameParser,\n                                   ListenerName listenerName,\n                                   SecurityProtocol securityProtocol,\n                                   TransportLayer transportLayer,\n                                   Map<String, Long> connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism,\n                                   ChannelMetadataRegistry metadataRegistry,\n                                   Time time,\n                                   Supplier<ApiVersionsResponse> apiVersionSupplier) {\n        this.callbackHandlers = callbackHandlers;\n        this.connectionId = connectionId;\n        this.subjects = subjects;\n        this.listenerName = listenerName;\n        this.securityProtocol = securityProtocol;\n        this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders = false;\n        this.transportLayer = transportLayer;\n        this.connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism = connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism;\n        this.time = time;\n        this.reauthInfo = new ReauthInfo();\n        this.metadataRegistry = metadataRegistry;\n        this.apiVersionSupplier = apiVersionSupplier;\n\n        this.configs = configs;\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        List<String> enabledMechanisms = (List<String>) this.configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG);\n        if (enabledMechanisms == null || enabledMechanisms.isEmpty())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"No SASL mechanisms are enabled\");\n        this.enabledMechanisms = new ArrayList<>(new HashSet<>(enabledMechanisms));\n        for (String mechanism : this.enabledMechanisms) {\n            if (!callbackHandlers.containsKey(mechanism))\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Callback handler not specified for SASL mechanism \" + mechanism);\n            if (!subjects.containsKey(mechanism))\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Subject cannot be null for SASL mechanism \" + mechanism);\n            LOG.trace(\"{} for mechanism={}: {}\", BrokerSecurityConfigs.CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_CONFIG, mechanism,\n                    connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism.get(mechanism));\n        }\n\n        // Note that the old principal builder does not support SASL, so we do not need to pass the\n        // authenticator or the transport layer\n        this.principalBuilder = ChannelBuilders.createPrincipalBuilder(configs, kerberosNameParser, null);\n\n        saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize = (Integer) configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE_CONFIG);\n        if (saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize == null)\n            saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize = BrokerSecurityConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE;\n    }\n\n    private void createSaslServer(String mechanism) throws IOException {\n        this.saslMechanism = mechanism;\n        Subject subject = subjects.get(mechanism);\n        final AuthenticateCallbackHandler callbackHandler = callbackHandlers.get(mechanism);\n        if (mechanism.equals(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM)) {\n            saslServer = createSaslKerberosServer(callbackHandler, configs, subject);\n        } else {\n            try {\n                saslServer = Subject.doAs(subject, (PrivilegedExceptionAction<SaslServer>) () ->\n                    Sasl.createSaslServer(saslMechanism, \"kafka\", serverAddress().getHostName(), configs, callbackHandler));\n                if (saslServer == null) {\n                    throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication with server mechanism \" + saslMechanism);\n                }\n            } catch (PrivilegedActionException e) {\n                throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication with server mechanism \" + saslMechanism, e.getCause());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private SaslServer createSaslKerberosServer(final AuthenticateCallbackHandler saslServerCallbackHandler, final Map<String, ?> configs, Subject subject) throws IOException {\n        // server is using a JAAS-authenticated subject: determine service principal name and hostname from kafka server's subject.\n        final String servicePrincipal = SaslClientAuthenticator.firstPrincipal(subject);\n        KerberosName kerberosName;\n        try {\n            kerberosName = KerberosName.parse(servicePrincipal);\n        } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\n            throw new KafkaException(\"Principal has name with unexpected format \" + servicePrincipal);\n        }\n        final String servicePrincipalName = kerberosName.serviceName();\n        final String serviceHostname = kerberosName.hostName();\n\n        LOG.debug(\"Creating SaslServer for {} with mechanism {}\", kerberosName, saslMechanism);\n\n        try {\n            return Subject.doAs(subject, (PrivilegedExceptionAction<SaslServer>) () ->\n                    Sasl.createSaslServer(saslMechanism, servicePrincipalName, serviceHostname, configs, saslServerCallbackHandler));\n        } catch (PrivilegedActionException e) {\n            throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication\", e.getCause());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Evaluates client responses via `SaslServer.evaluateResponse` and returns the issued challenge to the client until\n     * authentication succeeds or fails.\n     *\n     * The messages are sent and received as size delimited bytes that consists of a 4 byte network-ordered size N\n     * followed by N bytes representing the opaque payload.\n     */\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"fallthrough\")\n    @Override\n    public void authenticate() throws IOException {\n        if (saslState != SaslState.REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE) {\n            if (netOutBuffer != null && !flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps())\n                return;\n\n            if (saslServer != null && saslServer.isComplete()) {\n                setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // allocate on heap (as opposed to any socket server memory pool)\n            if (netInBuffer == null) netInBuffer = new NetworkReceive(saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize, connectionId);\n\n            try {\n                netInBuffer.readFrom(transportLayer);\n            } catch (InvalidReceiveException e) {\n                throw new SaslAuthenticationException(\"Failing SASL authentication due to invalid receive size\", e);\n            }\n            if (!netInBuffer.complete())\n                return;\n            netInBuffer.payload().rewind();\n        }\n        byte[] clientToken = new byte[netInBuffer.payload().remaining()];\n        netInBuffer.payload().get(clientToken, 0, clientToken.length);\n        netInBuffer = null; // reset the networkReceive as we read all the data.\n        try {\n            switch (saslState) {\n                case REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE:\n                case HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST:\n                case HANDSHAKE_REQUEST:\n                    handleKafkaRequest(clientToken);\n                    break;\n                case REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM:\n                    throw new SaslAuthenticationException(reauthInfo.badMechanismErrorMessage);\n                case INITIAL_REQUEST:\n                    if (handleKafkaRequest(clientToken))\n                        break;\n                    // For default GSSAPI, fall through to authenticate using the client token as the first GSSAPI packet.\n                    // This is required for interoperability with 0.9.0.x clients which do not send handshake request\n                case AUTHENTICATE:\n                    handleSaslToken(clientToken);\n                    // When the authentication exchange is complete and no more tokens are expected from the client,\n                    // update SASL state. Current SASL state will be updated when outgoing writes to the client complete.\n                    if (saslServer.isComplete())\n                        setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\n                    break;\n                default:\n                    break;\n            }\n        } catch (AuthenticationException e) {\n            // Exception will be propagated after response is sent to client\n            setSaslState(SaslState.FAILED, e);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            // In the case of IOExceptions and other unexpected exceptions, fail immediately\n            saslState = SaslState.FAILED;\n            LOG.debug(\"Failed during {}: {}\", reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText(), e.getMessage());\n            throw e;\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KafkaPrincipal principal() {\n        Optional<SSLSession> sslSession = transportLayer instanceof SslTransportLayer ?\n                Optional.of(((SslTransportLayer) transportLayer).sslSession()) : Optional.empty();\n        SaslAuthenticationContext context = new SaslAuthenticationContext(saslServer, securityProtocol,\n                clientAddress(), listenerName.value(), sslSession);\n        KafkaPrincipal principal = principalBuilder.build(context);\n        if (ScramMechanism.isScram(saslMechanism) && Boolean.parseBoolean((String) saslServer.getNegotiatedProperty(ScramLoginModule.TOKEN_AUTH_CONFIG))) {\n            principal.tokenAuthenticated(true);\n        }\n        return principal;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Optional<KafkaPrincipalSerde> principalSerde() {\n        return principalBuilder instanceof KafkaPrincipalSerde ? Optional.of((KafkaPrincipalSerde) principalBuilder) : Optional.empty();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean complete() {\n        return saslState == SaslState.COMPLETE;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void handleAuthenticationFailure() throws IOException {\n        sendAuthenticationFailureResponse();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws IOException {\n        if (principalBuilder instanceof Closeable)\n            Utils.closeQuietly((Closeable) principalBuilder, \"principal builder\");\n        if (saslServer != null)\n            saslServer.dispose();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void reauthenticate(ReauthenticationContext reauthenticationContext) throws IOException {\n        NetworkReceive saslHandshakeReceive = reauthenticationContext.networkReceive();\n        if (saslHandshakeReceive == null)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n                    \"Invalid saslHandshakeReceive in server-side re-authentication context: null\");\n        SaslServerAuthenticator previousSaslServerAuthenticator = (SaslServerAuthenticator) reauthenticationContext.previousAuthenticator();\n        reauthInfo.reauthenticating(previousSaslServerAuthenticator.saslMechanism,\n                previousSaslServerAuthenticator.principal(), reauthenticationContext.reauthenticationBeginNanos());\n        previousSaslServerAuthenticator.close();\n        netInBuffer = saslHandshakeReceive;\n        LOG.debug(\"Beginning re-authentication: {}\", this);\n        netInBuffer.payload().rewind();\n        setSaslState(SaslState.REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE);\n        authenticate();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Long serverSessionExpirationTimeNanos() {\n        return reauthInfo.sessionExpirationTimeNanos;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Long reauthenticationLatencyMs() {\n        return reauthInfo.reauthenticationLatencyMs();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean connectedClientSupportsReauthentication() {\n        return reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication;\n    }\n\n    private void setSaslState(SaslState saslState) {\n        setSaslState(saslState, null);\n    }\n\n    private void setSaslState(SaslState saslState, AuthenticationException exception) {\n        if (netOutBuffer != null && !netOutBuffer.completed()) {\n            pendingSaslState = saslState;\n            pendingException = exception;\n        } else {\n            this.saslState = saslState;\n            LOG.debug(\"Set SASL server state to {} during {}\", saslState, reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText());\n            this.pendingSaslState = null;\n            this.pendingException = null;\n            if (exception != null)\n                throw exception;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private boolean flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps() throws IOException {\n        boolean flushedCompletely = flushNetOutBuffer();\n        if (flushedCompletely) {\n            transportLayer.removeInterestOps(SelectionKey.OP_WRITE);\n            if (pendingSaslState != null)\n                setSaslState(pendingSaslState, pendingException);\n        } else\n            transportLayer.addInterestOps(SelectionKey.OP_WRITE);\n        return flushedCompletely;\n    }\n\n    private boolean flushNetOutBuffer() throws IOException {\n        if (!netOutBuffer.completed())\n            netOutBuffer.writeTo(transportLayer);\n        return netOutBuffer.completed();\n    }\n\n    private InetAddress serverAddress() {\n        return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getLocalAddress();\n    }\n\n    private InetAddress clientAddress() {\n        return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getInetAddress();\n    }\n\n    private int clientPort() {\n        return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getPort();\n    }\n\n    private void handleSaslToken(byte[] clientToken) throws IOException {\n        if (!enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders) {\n            byte[] response = saslServer.evaluateResponse(clientToken);\n            if (saslServer.isComplete()) {\n                reauthInfo.calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs();\n                if (reauthInfo.reauthenticating())\n                    reauthInfo.ensurePrincipalUnchanged(principal());\n            }\n            if (response != null) {\n                netOutBuffer = ByteBufferSend.sizePrefixed(ByteBuffer.wrap(response));\n                flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\n            }\n        } else {\n            ByteBuffer requestBuffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(clientToken);\n            RequestHeader header = RequestHeader.parse(requestBuffer);\n            ApiKeys apiKey = header.apiKey();\n            short version = header.apiVersion();\n            RequestContext requestContext = new RequestContext(header, connectionId, clientAddress(), Optional.of(clientPort()),\n                    KafkaPrincipal.ANONYMOUS, listenerName, securityProtocol, ClientInformation.EMPTY, false);\n            RequestAndSize requestAndSize = requestContext.parseRequest(requestBuffer);\n            if (apiKey != ApiKeys.SASL_AUTHENTICATE) {\n                IllegalSaslStateException e = new IllegalSaslStateException(\"Unexpected Kafka request of type \" + apiKey + \" during SASL authentication.\");\n                buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext, requestAndSize.request.getErrorResponse(e));\n                throw e;\n            }\n            if (!apiKey.isVersionSupported(version)) {\n                // We cannot create an error response if the request version of SaslAuthenticate is not supported\n                // This should not normally occur since clients typically check supported versions using ApiVersionsRequest\n                throw new UnsupportedVersionException(\"Version \" + version + \" is not supported for apiKey \" + apiKey);\n            }\n            /*\n             * The client sends multiple SASL_AUTHENTICATE requests, and the client is known\n             * to support the required version if any one of them indicates it supports that\n             * version.\n             */\n            if (!reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication)\n                reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication = version > 0;\n            SaslAuthenticateRequest saslAuthenticateRequest = (SaslAuthenticateRequest) requestAndSize.request;\n\n            try {\n                byte[] responseToken = saslServer.evaluateResponse(\n                        Utils.copyArray(saslAuthenticateRequest.data().authBytes()));\n                if (reauthInfo.reauthenticating() && saslServer.isComplete())\n                    reauthInfo.ensurePrincipalUnchanged(principal());\n                // For versions with SASL_AUTHENTICATE header, send a response to SASL_AUTHENTICATE request even if token is empty.\n                byte[] responseBytes = responseToken == null ? new byte[0] : responseToken;\n                long sessionLifetimeMs = !saslServer.isComplete() ? 0L\n                        : reauthInfo.calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs();\n                sendKafkaResponse(requestContext, new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n                        new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n                        .setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code())\n                        .setAuthBytes(responseBytes)\n                        .setSessionLifetimeMs(sessionLifetimeMs)));\n            } catch (SaslAuthenticationException e) {\n                buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext,\n                        new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n                                new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n                                .setErrorCode(Errors.SASL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.code())\n                                .setErrorMessage(e.getMessage())));\n                throw e;\n            } catch (SaslException e) {\n                KerberosError kerberosError = KerberosError.fromException(e);\n                if (kerberosError != null && kerberosError.retriable()) {\n                    // Handle retriable Kerberos exceptions as I/O exceptions rather than authentication exceptions\n                    throw e;\n                } else {\n                    // DO NOT include error message from the `SaslException` in the client response since it may\n                    // contain sensitive data like the existence of the user.\n                    String errorMessage = \"Authentication failed during \"\n                            + reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText()\n                            + \" due to invalid credentials with SASL mechanism \" + saslMechanism;\n                    buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext, new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n                            new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n                            .setErrorCode(Errors.SASL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.code())\n                            .setErrorMessage(errorMessage)));\n                    throw new SaslAuthenticationException(errorMessage, e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private boolean handleKafkaRequest(byte[] requestBytes) throws IOException, AuthenticationException {\n        boolean isKafkaRequest = false;\n        String clientMechanism = null;\n        try {\n            ByteBuffer requestBuffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(requestBytes);\n            RequestHeader header = RequestHeader.parse(requestBuffer);\n            ApiKeys apiKey = header.apiKey();\n\n            // A valid Kafka request header was received. SASL authentication tokens are now expected only\n            // following a SaslHandshakeRequest since this is not a GSSAPI client token from a Kafka 0.9.0.x client.\n            if (saslState == SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST)\n                setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST);\n            isKafkaRequest = true;\n\n            // Raise an error prior to parsing if the api cannot be handled at this layer. This avoids\n            // unnecessary exposure to some of the more complex schema types.\n            if (apiKey != ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS && apiKey != ApiKeys.SASL_HANDSHAKE)\n                throw new IllegalSaslStateException(\"Unexpected Kafka request of type \" + apiKey + \" during SASL handshake.\");\n\n            LOG.debug(\"Handling Kafka request {} during {}\", apiKey, reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText());\n\n\n            RequestContext requestContext = new RequestContext(header, connectionId, clientAddress(), Optional.of(clientPort()),\n                    KafkaPrincipal.ANONYMOUS, listenerName, securityProtocol, ClientInformation.EMPTY, false);\n            RequestAndSize requestAndSize = requestContext.parseRequest(requestBuffer);\n            if (apiKey == ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS)\n                handleApiVersionsRequest(requestContext, (ApiVersionsRequest) requestAndSize.request);\n            else\n                clientMechanism = handleHandshakeRequest(requestContext, (SaslHandshakeRequest) requestAndSize.request);\n        } catch (InvalidRequestException e) {\n            if (saslState == SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST) {\n                // InvalidRequestException is thrown if the request is not in Kafka format or if the API key\n                // is invalid. For compatibility with 0.9.0.x where the first packet is a GSSAPI token\n                // starting with 0x60, revert to GSSAPI for both these exceptions.\n                if (LOG.isDebugEnabled()) {\n                    StringBuilder tokenBuilder = new StringBuilder();\n                    for (byte b : requestBytes) {\n                        tokenBuilder.append(String.format(\"%02x\", b));\n                        if (tokenBuilder.length() >= 20)\n                             break;\n                    }\n                    LOG.debug(\"Received client packet of length {} starting with bytes 0x{}, process as GSSAPI packet\", requestBytes.length, tokenBuilder);\n                }\n                if (enabledMechanisms.contains(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM)) {\n                    LOG.debug(\"First client packet is not a SASL mechanism request, using default mechanism GSSAPI\");\n                    clientMechanism = SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM;\n                } else\n                    throw new UnsupportedSaslMechanismException(\"Exception handling first SASL packet from client, GSSAPI is not supported by server\", e);\n            } else\n                throw e;\n        }\n        if (clientMechanism != null && (!reauthInfo.reauthenticating()\n                || reauthInfo.saslMechanismUnchanged(clientMechanism))) {\n            createSaslServer(clientMechanism);\n            setSaslState(SaslState.AUTHENTICATE);\n        }\n        return isKafkaRequest;\n    }\n\n    private String handleHandshakeRequest(RequestContext context, SaslHandshakeRequest handshakeRequest) throws IOException, UnsupportedSaslMechanismException {\n        String clientMechanism = handshakeRequest.data().mechanism();\n        short version = context.header.apiVersion();\n        if (version >= 1)\n            this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(true);\n        if (enabledMechanisms.contains(clientMechanism)) {\n            LOG.debug(\"Using SASL mechanism '{}' provided by client\", clientMechanism);\n            sendKafkaResponse(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\n                    new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\n            return clientMechanism;\n        } else {\n            LOG.debug(\"SASL mechanism '{}' requested by client is not supported\", clientMechanism);\n            buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\n                    new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.UNSUPPORTED_SASL_MECHANISM.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\n            throw new UnsupportedSaslMechanismException(\"Unsupported SASL mechanism \" + clientMechanism);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Visible to override for testing\n    protected void enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(boolean flag) {\n        this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders = flag;\n    }\n\n    private void handleApiVersionsRequest(RequestContext context, ApiVersionsRequest apiVersionsRequest) throws IOException {\n        if (saslState != SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST)\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Unexpected ApiVersions request received during SASL authentication state \" + saslState);\n\n        if (apiVersionsRequest.hasUnsupportedRequestVersion())\n            sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.UNSUPPORTED_VERSION.exception()));\n        else if (!apiVersionsRequest.isValid())\n            sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.INVALID_REQUEST.exception()));\n        else {\n            metadataRegistry.registerClientInformation(new ClientInformation(apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareName(),\n                apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareVersion()));\n            sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionSupplier.get());\n            setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_REQUEST);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Build a {@link Send} response on {@link #authenticate()} failure. The actual response is sent out when\n     * {@link #sendAuthenticationFailureResponse()} is called.\n     */\n    private void buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) {\n        authenticationFailureSend = context.buildResponseSend(response);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Send any authentication failure response that may have been previously built.\n     */\n    private void sendAuthenticationFailureResponse() throws IOException {\n        if (authenticationFailureSend == null)\n            return;\n        sendKafkaResponse(authenticationFailureSend);\n        authenticationFailureSend = null;\n    }\n\n    private void sendKafkaResponse(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) throws IOException {\n        sendKafkaResponse(context.buildResponseSend(response));\n    }\n\n    private void sendKafkaResponse(Send send) throws IOException {\n        netOutBuffer = send;\n        flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Information related to re-authentication\n     */\n    private class ReauthInfo {\n        public String previousSaslMechanism;\n        public KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal;\n        public long reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n        public Long sessionExpirationTimeNanos;\n        public boolean connectedClientSupportsReauthentication;\n        public long authenticationEndNanos;\n        public String badMechanismErrorMessage;\n\n        public void reauthenticating(String previousSaslMechanism, KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal,\n                long reauthenticationBeginNanos) {\n            this.previousSaslMechanism = Objects.requireNonNull(previousSaslMechanism);\n            this.previousKafkaPrincipal = Objects.requireNonNull(previousKafkaPrincipal);\n            this.reauthenticationBeginNanos = reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n        }\n\n        public boolean reauthenticating() {\n            return previousSaslMechanism != null;\n        }\n\n        public String authenticationOrReauthenticationText() {\n            return reauthenticating() ? \"re-authentication\" : \"authentication\";\n        }\n\n        public void ensurePrincipalUnchanged(KafkaPrincipal reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal) throws SaslAuthenticationException {\n            if (!previousKafkaPrincipal.equals(reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal)) {\n                throw new SaslAuthenticationException(String.format(\n                        \"Cannot change principals during re-authentication from %s.%s: %s.%s\",\n                        previousKafkaPrincipal.getPrincipalType(), previousKafkaPrincipal.getName(),\n                        reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal.getPrincipalType(), reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal.getName()));\n            }\n        }\n\n        /*\n         * We define the REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM state because the failed re-authentication\n         * metric does not get updated if we send back an error immediately upon the\n         * start of re-authentication.\n         */\n        public boolean saslMechanismUnchanged(String clientMechanism) {\n            if (previousSaslMechanism.equals(clientMechanism))\n                return true;\n            badMechanismErrorMessage = String.format(\n                    \"SASL mechanism '%s' requested by client is not supported for re-authentication of mechanism '%s'\",\n                    clientMechanism, previousSaslMechanism);\n            LOG.debug(badMechanismErrorMessage);\n            setSaslState(SaslState.REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM);\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        private long calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs() {\n            long retvalSessionLifetimeMs = 0L;\n            long authenticationEndMs = time.milliseconds();\n            authenticationEndNanos = time.nanoseconds();\n            Long credentialExpirationMs = (Long) saslServer\n                    .getNegotiatedProperty(SaslInternalConfigs.CREDENTIAL_LIFETIME_MS_SASL_NEGOTIATED_PROPERTY_KEY);\n            Long connectionsMaxReauthMs = connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism.get(saslMechanism);\n            boolean maxReauthSet = connectionsMaxReauthMs != null && connectionsMaxReauthMs > 0;\n\n            if (credentialExpirationMs != null || maxReauthSet) {\n                if (credentialExpirationMs == null)\n                    retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(connectionsMaxReauthMs);\n                else if (!maxReauthSet)\n                    retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs);\n                else\n                    retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(Math.min(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs, connectionsMaxReauthMs));\n\n                sessionExpirationTimeNanos = authenticationEndNanos + 1000 * 1000 * retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\n            }\n\n            if (credentialExpirationMs != null) {\n                LOG.debug(\n                        \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, credential expiration={} ({} ms); session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\",\n                        connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(credentialExpirationMs),\n                        credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs,\n                        new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs), retvalSessionLifetimeMs,\n                        retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\n            } else {\n                if (sessionExpirationTimeNanos != null)\n                    LOG.debug(\n                            \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\",\n                            connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs),\n                            retvalSessionLifetimeMs, retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\n                else\n                    LOG.debug(\n                            \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; no session expiration, sending 0 ms to client\",\n                            connectionsMaxReauthMs);\n            }\n            return retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\n        }\n\n        public Long reauthenticationLatencyMs() {\n            if (!reauthenticating())\n                return null;\n            // record at least 1 ms if there is some latency\n            long latencyNanos = authenticationEndNanos - reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n            return latencyNanos == 0L ? 0L : Math.max(1L, Math.round(latencyNanos / 1000.0 / 1000.0));\n        }\n\n        private long zeroIfNegative(long value) {\n            return Math.max(0L, value);\n        }        \n    }\n}\n",

    "content": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage kafka.security.authorizer\n\nimport java.{lang, util}\nimport java.util.concurrent.{CompletableFuture, CompletionStage}\nimport com.typesafe.scalalogging.Logger\nimport kafka.server.{KafkaConfig, KafkaServer}\nimport kafka.utils._\nimport kafka.utils.Implicits._\nimport kafka.zk._\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.Endpoint\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.acl._\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.acl.AclOperation._\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.acl.AclPermissionType.{ALLOW, DENY}\nimport org.apache.kafka.security.authorizer.AclEntry.RESOURCE_SEPARATOR\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.{ApiException, InvalidRequestException, UnsupportedVersionException}\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.ApiKeys\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.resource._\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipal\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.{SecurityUtils, Time}\nimport org.apache.kafka.security.authorizer.AclEntry\nimport org.apache.kafka.server.authorizer.AclDeleteResult.AclBindingDeleteResult\nimport org.apache.kafka.server.authorizer._\nimport org.apache.kafka.server.config.ReplicationConfigs\nimport org.apache.kafka.server.common.MetadataVersion.IBP_2_0_IV1\nimport org.apache.kafka.server.config.ZkConfigs\nimport org.apache.zookeeper.client.ZKClientConfig\n\nimport scala.annotation.nowarn\nimport scala.collection.mutable.ArrayBuffer\nimport scala.collection.{Seq, immutable, mutable}\nimport scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\nimport scala.util.{Failure, Random, Success, Try}\n\nobject AclAuthorizer {\n  // Optional override zookeeper cluster configuration where acls will be stored. If not specified,\n  // acls will be stored in the same zookeeper where all other kafka broker metadata is stored.\n  val configPrefix: String = \"authorizer.\"\n  private val ZkUrlProp = s\"${configPrefix}zookeeper.url\"\n  private val ZkConnectionTimeOutProp = s\"${configPrefix}zookeeper.connection.timeout.ms\"\n  private val ZkSessionTimeOutProp = s\"${configPrefix}zookeeper.session.timeout.ms\"\n  private val ZkMaxInFlightRequests = s\"${configPrefix}zookeeper.max.in.flight.requests\"\n\n  // Semi-colon separated list of users that will be treated as super users and will have access to all the resources\n  // for all actions from all hosts, defaults to no super users.\n  val SuperUsersProp: String = \"super.users\"\n  // If set to true when no acls are found for a resource, authorizer allows access to everyone. Defaults to false.\n  val AllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFoundProp: String = \"allow.everyone.if.no.acl.found\"\n\n  case class VersionedAcls(acls: Set[AclEntry], zkVersion: Int) {\n    def exists: Boolean = zkVersion != ZkVersion.UnknownVersion\n  }\n\n  private class AclSeqs(seqs: Seq[AclEntry]*) {\n    def find(p: AclEntry => Boolean): Option[AclEntry] = {\n      // Lazily iterate through the inner `Seq` elements and stop as soon as we find a match\n      val it = seqs.iterator.flatMap(_.find(p))\n      if (it.hasNext) Some(it.next())\n      else None\n    }\n\n    def isEmpty: Boolean = !seqs.exists(_.nonEmpty)\n  }\n\n  val NoAcls: VersionedAcls = VersionedAcls(Set.empty, ZkVersion.UnknownVersion)\n  val WildcardHost: String = \"*\"\n\n  // Orders by resource type, then resource pattern type and finally reverse ordering by name.\n  class ResourceOrdering extends Ordering[ResourcePattern] {\n\n    def compare(a: ResourcePattern, b: ResourcePattern): Int = {\n      val rt = a.resourceType.compareTo(b.resourceType)\n      if (rt != 0)\n        rt\n      else {\n        val rnt = a.patternType.compareTo(b.patternType)\n        if (rnt != 0)\n          rnt\n        else\n          (a.name compare b.name) * -1\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  private[authorizer] def zkClientConfigFromKafkaConfigAndMap(kafkaConfig: KafkaConfig, configMap: mutable.Map[String, _<:Any]): ZKClientConfig = {\n    val zkSslClientEnable = configMap.get(AclAuthorizer.configPrefix + ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CLIENT_ENABLE_CONFIG).\n      map(_.toString.trim).getOrElse(kafkaConfig.zkSslClientEnable.toString).toBoolean\n    if (!zkSslClientEnable)\n      new ZKClientConfig\n    else {\n      // start with the base config from the Kafka configuration\n      // be sure to force creation since the zkSslClientEnable property in the kafkaConfig could be false\n      val zkClientConfig = KafkaServer.zkClientConfigFromKafkaConfig(kafkaConfig, forceZkSslClientEnable = true)\n      // add in any prefixed overlays\n      ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CONFIG_TO_SYSTEM_PROPERTY_MAP.asScala.forKeyValue { (kafkaProp, sysProp) =>\n        configMap.get(AclAuthorizer.configPrefix + kafkaProp).foreach { prefixedValue =>\n          zkClientConfig.setProperty(sysProp,\n            if (kafkaProp == ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_ENDPOINT_IDENTIFICATION_ALGORITHM_CONFIG)\n              (prefixedValue.toString.trim.toUpperCase == \"HTTPS\").toString\n            else\n              prefixedValue.toString.trim)\n        }\n      }\n      zkClientConfig\n    }\n  }\n\n  private def validateAclBinding(aclBinding: AclBinding): Unit = {\n    if (aclBinding.isUnknown)\n      throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"ACL binding contains unknown elements\")\n    if (aclBinding.pattern().name().contains(\"/\"))\n      throw new IllegalArgumentException(s\"ACL binding contains invalid resource name: ${aclBinding.pattern().name()}\")\n  }\n\n  def loadAllAcls(\n    zkClient: KafkaZkClient,\n    logger: Logging,\n    aclConsumer: (ResourcePattern, VersionedAcls) => Unit\n  ): Unit = {\n    ZkAclStore.stores.foreach { store =>\n      val resourceTypes = zkClient.getResourceTypes(store.patternType)\n      for (rType <- resourceTypes) {\n        val resourceType = Try(SecurityUtils.resourceType(rType))\n        resourceType match {\n          case Success(resourceTypeObj) =>\n            val resourceNames = zkClient.getResourceNames(store.patternType, resourceTypeObj)\n            for (resourceName <- resourceNames) {\n              val resource = new ResourcePattern(resourceTypeObj, resourceName, store.patternType)\n              val versionedAcls = getAclsFromZk(zkClient, resource)\n              aclConsumer.apply(resource, versionedAcls)\n            }\n          case Failure(_) => logger.warn(s\"Ignoring unknown ResourceType: $rType\")\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  private def getAclsFromZk(zkClient: KafkaZkClient, resource: ResourcePattern): VersionedAcls = {\n    zkClient.getVersionedAclsForResource(resource)\n  }\n}\n\nclass AclAuthorizer extends Authorizer with Logging {\n  import kafka.security.authorizer.AclAuthorizer._\n\n  private[security] val authorizerLogger = Logger(\"kafka.authorizer.logger\")\n  private var superUsers = Set.empty[KafkaPrincipal]\n  private var shouldAllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFound = false\n  private var zkClient: KafkaZkClient = _\n  private var aclChangeListeners: Iterable[AclChangeSubscription] = Iterable.empty\n  private var extendedAclSupport: Boolean = _\n\n  @volatile\n  private var aclCache = new scala.collection.immutable.TreeMap[ResourcePattern, VersionedAcls]()(new ResourceOrdering)\n\n  @volatile\n  private var resourceCache = new scala.collection.immutable.HashMap[ResourceTypeKey,\n    scala.collection.immutable.HashSet[String]]()\n\n  private val lock = new Object()\n\n  // The maximum number of times we should try to update the resource acls in zookeeper before failing;\n  // This should never occur, but is a safeguard just in case.\n  protected[security] var maxUpdateRetries = 10\n\n  private val retryBackoffMs = 100\n  private val retryBackoffJitterMs = 50\n\n  /**\n   * Guaranteed to be called before any authorize call is made.\n   */\n  override def configure(javaConfigs: util.Map[String, _]): Unit = {\n    val configs = javaConfigs.asScala\n    val props = new java.util.Properties()\n    configs.forKeyValue { (key, value) => props.put(key, value.toString.trim) }\n\n    superUsers = configs.get(AclAuthorizer.SuperUsersProp).collect {\n      case str: String if str.nonEmpty => str.split(\";\").map(s => SecurityUtils.parseKafkaPrincipal(s.trim)).toSet\n    }.getOrElse(Set.empty[KafkaPrincipal])\n\n    shouldAllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFound = configs.get(AclAuthorizer.AllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFoundProp).exists(_.toString.trim.toBoolean)\n\n    // Use `KafkaConfig` in order to get the default ZK config values if not present in `javaConfigs`. Note that this\n    // means that `KafkaConfig.zkConnect` must always be set by the user (even if `AclAuthorizer.ZkUrlProp` is also\n    // set).\n    val kafkaConfig = KafkaConfig.fromProps(props, doLog = false)\n    val zkUrl = configs.get(AclAuthorizer.ZkUrlProp).map(_.toString.trim).getOrElse(kafkaConfig.zkConnect)\n    val zkConnectionTimeoutMs = configs.get(AclAuthorizer.ZkConnectionTimeOutProp).map(_.toString.trim.toInt).getOrElse(kafkaConfig.zkConnectionTimeoutMs)\n    val zkSessionTimeOutMs = configs.get(AclAuthorizer.ZkSessionTimeOutProp).map(_.toString.trim.toInt).getOrElse(kafkaConfig.zkSessionTimeoutMs)\n    val zkMaxInFlightRequests = configs.get(AclAuthorizer.ZkMaxInFlightRequests).map(_.toString.trim.toInt).getOrElse(kafkaConfig.zkMaxInFlightRequests)\n\n    val zkClientConfig = AclAuthorizer.zkClientConfigFromKafkaConfigAndMap(kafkaConfig, configs)\n    val time = Time.SYSTEM\n    // createChrootIfNecessary=true is necessary in case we are running in a KRaft cluster\n    // because such a cluster will not create any chroot path in ZooKeeper (it doesn't connect to ZooKeeper)\n    zkClient = KafkaZkClient(zkUrl, kafkaConfig.zkEnableSecureAcls, zkSessionTimeOutMs, zkConnectionTimeoutMs,\n      zkMaxInFlightRequests, time, name = \"ACL authorizer\", zkClientConfig = zkClientConfig,\n      metricGroup = \"kafka.security\", metricType = \"AclAuthorizer\", createChrootIfNecessary = true, enableEntityConfigControllerCheck = false)\n    zkClient.createAclPaths()\n\n    extendedAclSupport = kafkaConfig.interBrokerProtocolVersion.isAtLeast(IBP_2_0_IV1)\n\n    // Start change listeners first and then populate the cache so that there is no timing window\n    // between loading cache and processing change notifications.\n    startZkChangeListeners()\n    loadCache()\n  }\n\n  override def start(serverInfo: AuthorizerServerInfo): util.Map[Endpoint, _ <: CompletionStage[Void]] = {\n    serverInfo.endpoints.asScala.map { endpoint =>\n      endpoint -> CompletableFuture.completedFuture[Void](null)\n    }.toMap.asJava\n  }\n\n  override def authorize(requestContext: AuthorizableRequestContext, actions: util.List[Action]): util.List[AuthorizationResult] = {\n    actions.asScala.map(action => authorizeAction(requestContext, action)).asJava\n  }\n\n  override def createAcls(requestContext: AuthorizableRequestContext,\n                          aclBindings: util.List[AclBinding]): util.List[_ <: CompletionStage[AclCreateResult]] = {\n    val results = new Array[AclCreateResult](aclBindings.size)\n    val aclsToCreate = aclBindings.asScala.zipWithIndex.filter { case (aclBinding, i) =>\n      try {\n        if (!extendedAclSupport && aclBinding.pattern.patternType == PatternType.PREFIXED) {\n          throw new UnsupportedVersionException(s\"Adding ACLs on prefixed resource patterns requires \" +\n            s\"${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG} of $IBP_2_0_IV1 or greater\")\n        }\n        validateAclBinding(aclBinding)\n        true\n      } catch {\n        case e: Throwable =>\n          results(i) = new AclCreateResult(new InvalidRequestException(\"Failed to create ACL\", apiException(e)))\n          false\n      }\n    }.groupBy(_._1.pattern)\n\n    if (aclsToCreate.nonEmpty) {\n      lock synchronized {\n        aclsToCreate.forKeyValue { (resource, aclsWithIndex) =>\n          try {\n            updateResourceAcls(resource) { currentAcls =>\n              val newAcls = aclsWithIndex.map { case (acl, _) => new AclEntry(acl.entry) }\n              currentAcls ++ newAcls\n            }\n            aclsWithIndex.foreach { case (_, index) => results(index) = AclCreateResult.SUCCESS }\n          } catch {\n            case e: Throwable =>\n              aclsWithIndex.foreach { case (_, index) => results(index) = new AclCreateResult(apiException(e)) }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    results.toBuffer.map(CompletableFuture.completedFuture[AclCreateResult]).asJava\n  }\n\n  /**\n   *\n   * <b>Concurrent updates:</b>\n   * <ul>\n   *   <li>If ACLs are created using [[kafka.security.authorizer.AclAuthorizer#createAcls]] while a delete is in\n   *   progress, these ACLs may or may not be considered for deletion depending on the order of updates.\n   *   The returned [[org.apache.kafka.server.authorizer.AclDeleteResult]] indicates which ACLs were deleted.</li>\n   *   <li>If the provided filters use resource pattern type\n   *   [[org.apache.kafka.common.resource.PatternType#MATCH]] that needs to filter all resources to determine\n   *   matching ACLs, only ACLs that have already been propagated to the broker processing the ACL update will be\n   *   deleted. This may not include some ACLs that were persisted, but not yet propagated to all brokers. The\n   *   returned [[org.apache.kafka.server.authorizer.AclDeleteResult]] indicates which ACLs were deleted.</li>\n   *   <li>If the provided filters use other resource pattern types that perform a direct match, all matching ACLs\n   *   from previously completed [[kafka.security.authorizer.AclAuthorizer#createAcls]] are guaranteed to be deleted.</li>\n   * </ul>\n   */\n  override def deleteAcls(requestContext: AuthorizableRequestContext,\n                          aclBindingFilters: util.List[AclBindingFilter]): util.List[_ <: CompletionStage[AclDeleteResult]] = {\n    val deletedBindings = new mutable.HashMap[AclBinding, Int]()\n    val deleteExceptions = new mutable.HashMap[AclBinding, ApiException]()\n    val filters = aclBindingFilters.asScala.zipWithIndex\n    lock synchronized {\n      // Find all potentially matching resource patterns from the provided filters and ACL cache and apply the filters\n      val resources = aclCache.keys ++ filters.map(_._1.patternFilter).filter(_.matchesAtMostOne).flatMap(filterToResources)\n      val resourcesToUpdate = resources.map { resource =>\n        val matchingFilters = filters.filter { case (filter, _) =>\n          filter.patternFilter.matches(resource)\n        }\n        resource -> matchingFilters\n      }.toMap.filter(_._2.nonEmpty)\n\n      resourcesToUpdate.forKeyValue { (resource, matchingFilters) =>\n        val resourceBindingsBeingDeleted = new mutable.HashMap[AclBinding, Int]()\n        try {\n          updateResourceAcls(resource) { currentAcls =>\n            val aclsToRemove = currentAcls.filter { acl =>\n              matchingFilters.exists { case (filter, index) =>\n                val matches = filter.entryFilter.matches(acl)\n                if (matches) {\n                  val binding = new AclBinding(resource, acl)\n                  deletedBindings.getOrElseUpdate(binding, index)\n                  resourceBindingsBeingDeleted.getOrElseUpdate(binding, index)\n                }\n                matches\n              }\n            }\n            currentAcls -- aclsToRemove\n          }\n        } catch {\n          case e: Exception =>\n            resourceBindingsBeingDeleted.keys.foreach { binding =>\n              deleteExceptions.getOrElseUpdate(binding, apiException(e))\n            }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    val deletedResult = deletedBindings.groupBy(_._2).map { case (k, bindings) =>\n      k -> bindings.keys.map { binding => new AclBindingDeleteResult(binding, deleteExceptions.get(binding).orNull) }\n    }\n    (0 until aclBindingFilters.size).map { i =>\n      new AclDeleteResult(deletedResult.getOrElse(i, Set.empty[AclBindingDeleteResult]).toSet.asJava)\n    }.map(CompletableFuture.completedFuture[AclDeleteResult]).asJava\n  }\n\n  override def acls(filter: AclBindingFilter): lang.Iterable[AclBinding] = {\n    val aclBindings = new util.ArrayList[AclBinding]()\n    aclCache.forKeyValue { case (resource, versionedAcls) =>\n      versionedAcls.acls.foreach { acl =>\n        val binding = new AclBinding(resource, acl.ace)\n        if (filter.matches(binding))\n          aclBindings.add(binding)\n      }\n    }\n    aclBindings\n  }\n\n  override def close(): Unit = {\n    aclChangeListeners.foreach(listener => listener.close())\n    if (zkClient != null) zkClient.close()\n  }\n\n  override def authorizeByResourceType(requestContext: AuthorizableRequestContext,\n                                       op: AclOperation,\n                                       resourceType: ResourceType): AuthorizationResult = {\n    SecurityUtils.authorizeByResourceTypeCheckArgs(op, resourceType)\n\n    val principal = new KafkaPrincipal(\n      requestContext.principal().getPrincipalType,\n      requestContext.principal().getName)\n\n    if (isSuperUser(principal))\n      return AuthorizationResult.ALLOWED\n\n    val resourceSnapshot = resourceCache\n    val principalStr = principal.toString\n    val host = requestContext.clientAddress().getHostAddress\n    val action = new Action(op, new ResourcePattern(resourceType, \"NONE\", PatternType.UNKNOWN), 0, true, true)\n\n    val denyLiterals = matchingResources(\n      resourceSnapshot, principalStr, host, op, AclPermissionType.DENY, resourceType, PatternType.LITERAL)\n\n    if (denyAll(denyLiterals)) {\n      logAuditMessage(requestContext, action, authorized = false)\n      return AuthorizationResult.DENIED\n    }\n\n    if (shouldAllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFound) {\n      logAuditMessage(requestContext, action, authorized = true)\n      return AuthorizationResult.ALLOWED\n    }\n\n    val denyPrefixes = matchingResources(\n      resourceSnapshot, principalStr, host, op, AclPermissionType.DENY, resourceType, PatternType.PREFIXED)\n\n    if (denyLiterals.isEmpty && denyPrefixes.isEmpty) {\n      if (hasMatchingResources(resourceSnapshot, principalStr, host, op, AclPermissionType.ALLOW, resourceType, PatternType.PREFIXED)\n          || hasMatchingResources(resourceSnapshot, principalStr, host, op, AclPermissionType.ALLOW, resourceType, PatternType.LITERAL)) {\n        logAuditMessage(requestContext, action, authorized = true)\n        return AuthorizationResult.ALLOWED\n      } else {\n        logAuditMessage(requestContext, action, authorized = false)\n        return AuthorizationResult.DENIED\n      }\n    }\n\n    val allowLiterals = matchingResources(\n      resourceSnapshot, principalStr, host, op, AclPermissionType.ALLOW, resourceType, PatternType.LITERAL)\n    val allowPrefixes = matchingResources(\n      resourceSnapshot, principalStr, host, op, AclPermissionType.ALLOW, resourceType, PatternType.PREFIXED)\n\n    if (allowAny(allowLiterals, allowPrefixes, denyLiterals, denyPrefixes)) {\n      logAuditMessage(requestContext, action, authorized = true)\n      return AuthorizationResult.ALLOWED\n    }\n\n    logAuditMessage(requestContext, action, authorized = false)\n    AuthorizationResult.DENIED\n  }\n\n  private def matchingResources(resourceSnapshot: immutable.Map[ResourceTypeKey, immutable.Set[String]],\n                                principal: String, host: String, op: AclOperation, permission: AclPermissionType,\n                                resourceType: ResourceType, patternType: PatternType): ArrayBuffer[Set[String]] = {\n    val matched = ArrayBuffer[immutable.Set[String]]()\n    for (p <- Set(principal, AclEntry.WILDCARD_PRINCIPAL_STRING);\n         h <- Set(host, AclEntry.WILDCARD_HOST);\n         o <- Set(op, AclOperation.ALL)) {\n      val resourceTypeKey = ResourceTypeKey(\n        new AccessControlEntry(p, h, o, permission), resourceType, patternType)\n      resourceSnapshot.get(resourceTypeKey) match {\n        case Some(resources) => matched += resources\n        case None =>\n      }\n    }\n    matched\n  }\n\n  private def hasMatchingResources(resourceSnapshot: immutable.Map[ResourceTypeKey, immutable.Set[String]],\n                                   principal: String, host: String, op: AclOperation, permission: AclPermissionType,\n                                   resourceType: ResourceType, patternType: PatternType): Boolean = {\n    for (p <- Set(principal, AclEntry.WILDCARD_PRINCIPAL_STRING);\n         h <- Set(host, AclEntry.WILDCARD_HOST);\n         o <- Set(op, AclOperation.ALL)) {\n          val resourceTypeKey = ResourceTypeKey(\n            new AccessControlEntry(p, h, o, permission), resourceType, patternType)\n          if (resourceSnapshot.contains(resourceTypeKey))\n            return true\n    }\n    false\n  }\n\n  private def denyAll(denyLiterals: ArrayBuffer[immutable.Set[String]]): Boolean =\n    denyLiterals.exists(_.contains(ResourcePattern.WILDCARD_RESOURCE))\n\n\n  private def allowAny(allowLiterals: ArrayBuffer[immutable.Set[String]], allowPrefixes: ArrayBuffer[immutable.Set[String]],\n                       denyLiterals: ArrayBuffer[immutable.Set[String]], denyPrefixes: ArrayBuffer[immutable.Set[String]]): Boolean = {\n    (allowPrefixes.exists(_.exists(prefix => allowPrefix(prefix, denyPrefixes)))\n      || allowLiterals.exists(_.exists(literal => allowLiteral(literal, denyLiterals, denyPrefixes))))\n  }\n\n  private def allowLiteral(literalName: String, denyLiterals: ArrayBuffer[immutable.Set[String]],\n                           denyPrefixes: ArrayBuffer[immutable.Set[String]]): Boolean = {\n    literalName match {\n      case ResourcePattern.WILDCARD_RESOURCE => true\n      case _ => !denyLiterals.exists(_.contains(literalName)) && !hasDominantPrefixedDeny(literalName, denyPrefixes)\n    }\n  }\n\n  private def allowPrefix(prefixName: String,\n                          denyPrefixes: ArrayBuffer[immutable.Set[String]]): Boolean = {\n    !hasDominantPrefixedDeny(prefixName, denyPrefixes)\n  }\n\n  private def hasDominantPrefixedDeny(resourceName: String, denyPrefixes: ArrayBuffer[immutable.Set[String]]): Boolean = {\n    val sb = new StringBuilder\n    for (ch <- resourceName.toCharArray) {\n      sb.append(ch)\n      if (denyPrefixes.exists(p => p.contains(sb.toString()))) {\n        return true\n      }\n    }\n    false\n  }\n\n  private def authorizeAction(requestContext: AuthorizableRequestContext, action: Action): AuthorizationResult = {\n    val resource = action.resourcePattern\n    if (resource.patternType != PatternType.LITERAL) {\n      throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Only literal resources are supported. Got: \" + resource.patternType)\n    }\n\n    // ensure we compare identical classes\n    val sessionPrincipal = requestContext.principal\n    val principal = if (classOf[KafkaPrincipal] != sessionPrincipal.getClass)\n      new KafkaPrincipal(sessionPrincipal.getPrincipalType, sessionPrincipal.getName)\n    else\n      sessionPrincipal\n\n    val host = requestContext.clientAddress.getHostAddress\n    val operation = action.operation\n\n    def isEmptyAclAndAuthorized(acls: AclSeqs): Boolean = {\n      if (acls.isEmpty) {\n        // No ACLs found for this resource, permission is determined by value of config allow.everyone.if.no.acl.found\n        authorizerLogger.debug(s\"No acl found for resource $resource, authorized = $shouldAllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFound\")\n        shouldAllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFound\n      } else false\n    }\n\n    def denyAclExists(acls: AclSeqs): Boolean = {\n      // Check if there are any Deny ACLs which would forbid this operation.\n      matchingAclExists(operation, resource, principal, host, DENY, acls)\n    }\n\n    def allowAclExists(acls: AclSeqs): Boolean = {\n      // Check if there are any Allow ACLs which would allow this operation.\n      // Allowing read, write, delete, or alter implies allowing describe.\n      // See #{org.apache.kafka.common.acl.AclOperation} for more details about ACL inheritance.\n      val allowOps = operation match {\n        case DESCRIBE => Set[AclOperation](DESCRIBE, READ, WRITE, DELETE, ALTER)\n        case DESCRIBE_CONFIGS => Set[AclOperation](DESCRIBE_CONFIGS, ALTER_CONFIGS)\n        case _ => Set[AclOperation](operation)\n      }\n      allowOps.exists(operation => matchingAclExists(operation, resource, principal, host, ALLOW, acls))\n    }\n\n    def aclsAllowAccess = {\n      // we allow an operation if no acls are found and user has configured to allow all users\n      // when no acls are found or if no deny acls are found and at least one allow acls matches.\n      val acls = matchingAcls(resource.resourceType, resource.name)\n      isEmptyAclAndAuthorized(acls) || (!denyAclExists(acls) && allowAclExists(acls))\n    }\n\n    // Evaluate if operation is allowed\n    val authorized = isSuperUser(principal) || aclsAllowAccess\n\n    logAuditMessage(requestContext, action, authorized)\n    if (authorized) AuthorizationResult.ALLOWED else AuthorizationResult.DENIED\n  }\n\n  private def isSuperUser(principal: KafkaPrincipal): Boolean = {\n    if (superUsers.contains(principal)) {\n      authorizerLogger.debug(s\"principal = $principal is a super user, allowing operation without checking acls.\")\n      true\n    } else false\n  }\n\n  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n  private def matchingAcls(resourceType: ResourceType, resourceName: String): AclSeqs = {\n    // this code is performance sensitive, make sure to run AclAuthorizerBenchmark after any changes\n\n    // save aclCache reference to a local val to get a consistent view of the cache during acl updates.\n    val aclCacheSnapshot = aclCache\n    val wildcard = aclCacheSnapshot.get(new ResourcePattern(resourceType, ResourcePattern.WILDCARD_RESOURCE, PatternType.LITERAL))\n      .map(_.acls.toBuffer)\n      .getOrElse(mutable.Buffer.empty)\n\n    val literal = aclCacheSnapshot.get(new ResourcePattern(resourceType, resourceName, PatternType.LITERAL))\n      .map(_.acls.toBuffer)\n      .getOrElse(mutable.Buffer.empty)\n\n    val prefixed = new ArrayBuffer[AclEntry]\n    aclCacheSnapshot\n      .from(new ResourcePattern(resourceType, resourceName, PatternType.PREFIXED))\n      .to(new ResourcePattern(resourceType, resourceName.take(1), PatternType.PREFIXED))\n      .forKeyValue { (resource, acls) =>\n        if (resourceName.startsWith(resource.name)) prefixed ++= acls.acls\n      }\n\n    new AclSeqs(prefixed, wildcard, literal)\n  }\n\n  private def matchingAclExists(operation: AclOperation,\n                                resource: ResourcePattern,\n                                principal: KafkaPrincipal,\n                                host: String,\n                                permissionType: AclPermissionType,\n                                acls: AclSeqs): Boolean = {\n    acls.find { acl =>\n      acl.permissionType == permissionType &&\n        (acl.kafkaPrincipal == principal || acl.kafkaPrincipal == AclEntry.WILDCARD_PRINCIPAL) &&\n        (operation == acl.operation || acl.operation == AclOperation.ALL) &&\n        (acl.host == host || acl.host == AclEntry.WILDCARD_HOST)\n    }.exists { acl =>\n      authorizerLogger.debug(s\"operation = $operation on resource = $resource from host = $host is $permissionType based on acl = $acl\")\n      true\n    }\n  }\n\n  private def loadCache(): Unit = {\n    lock synchronized  {\n      loadAllAcls(zkClient, this, updateCache)\n    }\n  }\n\n  private[authorizer] def startZkChangeListeners(): Unit = {\n    aclChangeListeners = ZkAclChangeStore.stores\n      .map(store => store.createListener(AclChangedNotificationHandler, zkClient))\n  }\n\n  private def filterToResources(filter: ResourcePatternFilter): Set[ResourcePattern] = {\n    filter.patternType match {\n      case PatternType.LITERAL | PatternType.PREFIXED =>\n        Set(new ResourcePattern(filter.resourceType, filter.name, filter.patternType))\n      case PatternType.ANY =>\n        Set(new ResourcePattern(filter.resourceType, filter.name, PatternType.LITERAL),\n          new ResourcePattern(filter.resourceType, filter.name, PatternType.PREFIXED))\n      case _ => throw new IllegalArgumentException(s\"Cannot determine matching resources for patternType $filter\")\n    }\n  }\n\n  private def logAuditMessage(requestContext: AuthorizableRequestContext, action: Action, authorized: Boolean): Unit = {\n    def logMessage: String = {\n      val principal = requestContext.principal\n      val operation = SecurityUtils.operationName(action.operation)\n      val host = requestContext.clientAddress.getHostAddress\n      val resourceType = SecurityUtils.resourceTypeName(action.resourcePattern.resourceType)\n      val resource = s\"$resourceType$RESOURCE_SEPARATOR${action.resourcePattern.patternType}$RESOURCE_SEPARATOR${action.resourcePattern.name}\"\n      val authResult = if (authorized) \"Allowed\" else \"Denied\"\n      val apiKey = if (ApiKeys.hasId(requestContext.requestType)) ApiKeys.forId(requestContext.requestType).name else requestContext.requestType\n      val refCount = action.resourceReferenceCount\n\n      s\"Principal = $principal is $authResult Operation = $operation \" +\n        s\"from host = $host on resource = $resource for request = $apiKey with resourceRefCount = $refCount\"\n    }\n\n    if (authorized) {\n      // logIfAllowed is true if access is granted to the resource as a result of this authorization.\n      // In this case, log at debug level. If false, no access is actually granted, the result is used\n      // only to determine authorized operations. So log only at trace level.\n      if (action.logIfAllowed)\n        authorizerLogger.debug(logMessage)\n      else\n        authorizerLogger.trace(logMessage)\n    } else {\n      // logIfDenied is true if access to the resource was explicitly requested. Since this is an attempt\n      // to access unauthorized resources, log at info level. If false, this is either a request to determine\n      // authorized operations or a filter (e.g for regex subscriptions) to filter out authorized resources.\n      // In this case, log only at trace level.\n      if (action.logIfDenied)\n        authorizerLogger.info(logMessage)\n      else\n        authorizerLogger.trace(logMessage)\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n    * Safely updates the resources ACLs by ensuring reads and writes respect the expected zookeeper version.\n    * Continues to retry until it successfully updates zookeeper.\n    *\n    * Returns a boolean indicating if the content of the ACLs was actually changed.\n    *\n    * @param resource the resource to change ACLs for\n    * @param getNewAcls function to transform existing acls to new ACLs\n    * @return boolean indicating if a change was made\n    */\n  private def updateResourceAcls(resource: ResourcePattern)(getNewAcls: Set[AclEntry] => Set[AclEntry]): Boolean = {\n    var currentVersionedAcls =\n      if (aclCache.contains(resource))\n        getAclsFromCache(resource)\n      else\n        getAclsFromZk(zkClient, resource)\n    var newVersionedAcls: VersionedAcls = null\n    var writeComplete = false\n    var retries = 0\n    while (!writeComplete && retries <= maxUpdateRetries) {\n      val newAcls = getNewAcls(currentVersionedAcls.acls)\n      val (updateSucceeded, updateVersion) =\n        if (newAcls.nonEmpty) {\n          if (currentVersionedAcls.exists)\n            zkClient.conditionalSetAclsForResource(resource, newAcls, currentVersionedAcls.zkVersion)\n          else\n            zkClient.createAclsForResourceIfNotExists(resource, newAcls)\n        } else {\n          trace(s\"Deleting path for $resource because it had no ACLs remaining\")\n          (zkClient.conditionalDelete(resource, currentVersionedAcls.zkVersion), 0)\n        }\n\n      if (!updateSucceeded) {\n        trace(s\"Failed to update ACLs for $resource. Used version ${currentVersionedAcls.zkVersion}. Reading data and retrying update.\")\n        Thread.sleep(backoffTime)\n        currentVersionedAcls = getAclsFromZk(zkClient, resource)\n        retries += 1\n      } else {\n        newVersionedAcls = VersionedAcls(newAcls, updateVersion)\n        writeComplete = updateSucceeded\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (!writeComplete)\n      throw new IllegalStateException(s\"Failed to update ACLs for $resource after trying a maximum of $maxUpdateRetries times\")\n\n    if (newVersionedAcls.acls != currentVersionedAcls.acls) {\n      info(s\"Updated ACLs for $resource with new version ${newVersionedAcls.zkVersion}\")\n      debug(s\"Updated ACLs for $resource to $newVersionedAcls\")\n      updateCache(resource, newVersionedAcls)\n      updateAclChangedFlag(resource)\n      true\n    } else {\n      debug(s\"Updated ACLs for $resource, no change was made\")\n      updateCache(resource, newVersionedAcls) // Even if no change, update the version\n      false\n    }\n  }\n\n  private def getAclsFromCache(resource: ResourcePattern): VersionedAcls = {\n    aclCache.getOrElse(resource, throw new IllegalArgumentException(s\"ACLs do not exist in the cache for resource $resource\"))\n  }\n\n\n  // Visible for benchmark\n  def updateCache(resource: ResourcePattern, versionedAcls: VersionedAcls): Unit = {\n    val currentAces: Set[AccessControlEntry] = aclCache.get(resource).map(_.acls.map(_.ace)).getOrElse(Set.empty)\n    val newAces: Set[AccessControlEntry] = versionedAcls.acls.map(aclEntry => aclEntry.ace)\n    val acesToAdd = newAces.diff(currentAces)\n    val acesToRemove = currentAces.diff(newAces)\n\n    acesToAdd.foreach { ace =>\n      val resourceTypeKey = ResourceTypeKey(ace, resource.resourceType(), resource.patternType())\n      resourceCache.get(resourceTypeKey) match {\n        case Some(resources) => resourceCache += (resourceTypeKey -> (resources + resource.name()))\n        case None => resourceCache += (resourceTypeKey -> immutable.HashSet(resource.name()))\n      }\n    }\n    acesToRemove.foreach { ace =>\n      val resourceTypeKey = ResourceTypeKey(ace, resource.resourceType(), resource.patternType())\n      resourceCache.get(resourceTypeKey) match {\n        case Some(resources) =>\n          val newResources = resources - resource.name()\n          if (newResources.isEmpty) {\n            resourceCache -= resourceTypeKey\n          } else {\n            resourceCache += (resourceTypeKey -> newResources)\n          }\n        case None =>\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (versionedAcls.acls.nonEmpty) {\n      aclCache = aclCache.updated(resource, versionedAcls)\n    } else {\n      aclCache -= resource\n    }\n  }\n\n  private def updateAclChangedFlag(resource: ResourcePattern): Unit = {\n    zkClient.createAclChangeNotification(resource)\n  }\n\n  private def backoffTime = {\n    retryBackoffMs + Random.nextInt(retryBackoffJitterMs)\n  }\n\n  private def apiException(e: Throwable): ApiException = {\n    e match {\n      case e1: ApiException => e1\n      case e1 => new ApiException(e1)\n    }\n  }\n\n  private[authorizer] def processAclChangeNotification(resource: ResourcePattern): Unit = {\n    lock synchronized {\n      val versionedAcls = getAclsFromZk(zkClient, resource)\n      info(s\"Processing Acl change notification for $resource, versionedAcls : ${versionedAcls.acls}, zkVersion : ${versionedAcls.zkVersion}\")\n      updateCache(resource, versionedAcls)\n    }\n  }\n\n  private object AclChangedNotificationHandler extends AclChangeNotificationHandler {\n    override def processNotification(resource: ResourcePattern): Unit = {\n      processAclChangeNotification(resource)\n    }\n  }\n\n  private case class ResourceTypeKey(ace: AccessControlEntry,\n                                     resourceType: ResourceType,\n                                     patternType: PatternType)\n}\n",
    "filePath": "/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/c0783729-683c-4871-bc60-9d07e9b6d1de/tool-results/b9d7492.txt",
    "numLines": 768,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 768
  },
  "type": "text"
}
