distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: package org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator;\n18: \n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SaslConfigs;\n21: import org.apache.kafka.common.config.internals.BrokerSecurityConfigs;\n22: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException;\n23: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.IllegalSaslStateException;\n24: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidRequestException;\n25: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.SaslAuthenticationException;\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedSaslMechanismException;\n27: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException;\n28: import org.apache.kafka.common.internals.SecurityManagerCompatibility;\n29: import org.apache.kafka.common.message.SaslAuthenticateResponseData;\n30: import org.apache.kafka.common.message.SaslHandshakeResponseData;\n31: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.Authenticator;\n32: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ByteBufferSend;\n33: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ChannelBuilders;\n34: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ChannelMetadataRegistry;\n35: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ClientInformation;\n36: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.InvalidReceiveException;\n37: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ListenerName;\n38: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.NetworkReceive;\n39: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ReauthenticationContext;\n40: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.Send;\n41: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.SslTransportLayer;\n42: import org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransportLayer;\n43: import org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.ApiKeys;\n44: import org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.Errors;\n45: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.AbstractResponse;\n46: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.ApiVersionsRequest;\n47: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.ApiVersionsResponse;\n48: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.RequestAndSize;\n49: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.RequestContext;\n50: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.RequestHeader;\n51: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslAuthenticateRequest;\n52: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslAuthenticateResponse;\n53: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslHandshakeRequest;\n54: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.SaslHandshakeResponse;\n55: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.AuthenticateCallbackHandler;\n56: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipal;\n57: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipalBuilder;\n58: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipalSerde;\n59: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.SaslAuthenticationContext;\n60: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.SecurityProtocol;\n61: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.kerberos.KerberosError;\n62: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.kerberos.KerberosName;\n63: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.kerberos.KerberosShortNamer;\n64: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.ScramLoginModule;\n65: import org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals.ScramMechanism;\n66: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\n67: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n68: \n69: import org.slf4j.Logger;\n70: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n71: \n72: import java.io.Closeable;\n73: import java.io.IOException;\n74: import java.net.InetAddress;\n75: import java.nio.ByteBuffer;\n76: import java.nio.channels.SelectionKey;\n77: import java.util.ArrayList;\n78: import java.util.Date;\n79: import java.util.HashSet;\n80: import java.util.List;\n81: import java.util.Map;\n82: import java.util.Objects;\n83: import java.util.Optional;\n84: import java.util.concurrent.CompletionException;\n85: import java.util.function.Function;\n86: \n87: import javax.net.ssl.SSLSession;\n88: import javax.security.auth.Subject;\n89: import javax.security.sasl.Sasl;\n90: import javax.security.sasl.SaslException;\n91: import javax.security.sasl.SaslServer;\n92: \n93: public class SaslServerAuthenticator implements Authenticator {\n94:     private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(SaslServerAuthenticator.class);\n95: \n96:     /**\n97:      * The internal state transitions for initial authentication of a channel on the\n98:      * server side are declared in order, starting with {@link #INITIAL_REQUEST} and\n99:      * ending in either {@link #COMPLETE} or {@link #FAILED}.\n100:      * <p>\n101:      * Re-authentication of a channel on the server side starts with the state\n102:      * {@link #REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE}. It may then flow to\n103:      * {@link #REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM} before a transition to {@link #FAILED} if\n104:      * re-authentication is attempted with a mechanism different than the original\n105:      * one; otherwise it joins the authentication flow at the {@link #AUTHENTICATE}\n106:      * state and likewise ends at either {@link #COMPLETE} or {@link #FAILED}.\n107:      */\n108:     private enum SaslState {\n109:         INITIAL_REQUEST,               // May be SaslHandshake or ApiVersions for authentication\n110:         HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST, // May be SaslHandshake or ApiVersions\n111:         HANDSHAKE_REQUEST,             // After an ApiVersions request, next request must be SaslHandshake\n112:         AUTHENTICATE,                  // Authentication tokens (SaslHandshake v1 and above indicate SaslAuthenticate headers)\n113:         COMPLETE,                      // Authentication completed successfully\n114:         FAILED,                        // Authentication failed\n115:         REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE,      // Initial state for re-authentication, processes SASL handshake request\n116:         REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM,          // When re-authentication requested with wrong mechanism, generate exception\n117:     }\n118: \n119:     private final SecurityProtocol securityProtocol;\n120:     private final ListenerName listenerName;\n121:     private final String connectionId;\n122:     private final Map<String, Subject> subjects;\n123:     private final TransportLayer transportLayer;\n124:     private final List<String> enabledMechanisms;\n125:     private final Map<String, ?> configs;\n126:     private final KafkaPrincipalBuilder principalBuilder;\n127:     private final Map<String, AuthenticateCallbackHandler> callbackHandlers;\n128:     private final Map<String, Long> connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism;\n129:     private final Time time;\n130:     private final ReauthInfo reauthInfo;\n131:     private final ChannelMetadataRegistry metadataRegistry;\n132:     private final Function<Short, ApiVersionsResponse> apiVersionSupplier;\n133: \n134:     // Current SASL state\n135:     private SaslState saslState = SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST;\n136:     // Next SASL state to be set when outgoing writes associated with the current SASL state complete\n137:     private SaslState pendingSaslState = null;\n138:     // Exception that will be thrown by `authenticate()` when SaslState is set to FAILED after outbound writes complete\n139:     private AuthenticationException pendingException = null;\n140:     private SaslServer saslServer;\n141:     private String saslMechanism;\n142: \n143:     // buffers used in `authenticate`\n144:     private Integer saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize;\n145:     private NetworkReceive netInBuffer;\n146:     private Send netOutBuffer;\n147:     private Send authenticationFailureSend = null;\n148:     // flag indicating if sasl tokens are sent as Kafka SaslAuthenticate request/responses\n149:     private boolean enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders;\n150: \n151:     public SaslServerAuthenticator(Map<String, ?> configs,\n152:                                    Map<String, AuthenticateCallbackHandler> callbackHandlers,\n153:                                    String connectionId,\n154:                                    Map<String, Subject> subjects,\n155:                                    KerberosShortNamer kerberosNameParser,\n156:                                    ListenerName listenerName,\n157:                                    SecurityProtocol securityProtocol,\n158:                                    TransportLayer transportLayer,\n159:                                    Map<String, Long> connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism,\n160:                                    ChannelMetadataRegistry metadataRegistry,\n161:                                    Time time,\n162:                                    Function<Short, ApiVersionsResponse> apiVersionSupplier) {\n163:         this.callbackHandlers = callbackHandlers;\n164:         this.connectionId = connectionId;\n165:         this.subjects = subjects;\n166:         this.listenerName = listenerName;\n167:         this.securityProtocol = securityProtocol;\n168:         this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders = false;\n169:         this.transportLayer = transportLayer;\n170:         this.connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism = connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism;\n171:         this.time = time;\n172:         this.reauthInfo = new ReauthInfo();\n173:         this.metadataRegistry = metadataRegistry;\n174:         this.apiVersionSupplier = apiVersionSupplier;\n175: \n176:         this.configs = configs;\n177:         @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n178:         List<String> enabledMechanisms = (List<String>) this.configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG);\n179:         if (enabledMechanisms == null || enabledMechanisms.isEmpty())\n180:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"No SASL mechanisms are enabled\");\n181:         this.enabledMechanisms = new ArrayList<>(new HashSet<>(enabledMechanisms));\n182:         for (String mechanism : this.enabledMechanisms) {\n183:             if (!callbackHandlers.containsKey(mechanism))\n184:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Callback handler not specified for SASL mechanism \" + mechanism);\n185:             if (!subjects.containsKey(mechanism))\n186:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Subject cannot be null for SASL mechanism \" + mechanism);\n187:             LOG.trace(\"{} for mechanism={}: {}\", BrokerSecurityConfigs.CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_CONFIG, mechanism,\n188:                     connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism.get(mechanism));\n189:         }\n190: \n191:         // Note that the old principal builder does not support SASL, so we do not need to pass the\n192:         // authenticator or the transport layer\n193:         this.principalBuilder = ChannelBuilders.createPrincipalBuilder(configs, kerberosNameParser, null);\n194: \n195:         saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize = (Integer) configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE_CONFIG);\n196:         if (saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize == null)\n197:             saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize = BrokerSecurityConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE;\n198:     }\n199: \n200:     private void createSaslServer(String mechanism) throws IOException {\n201:         this.saslMechanism = mechanism;\n202:         Subject subject = subjects.get(mechanism);\n203:         final AuthenticateCallbackHandler callbackHandler = callbackHandlers.get(mechanism);\n204:         if (mechanism.equals(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM)) {\n205:             saslServer = createSaslKerberosServer(callbackHandler, configs, subject);\n206:         } else {\n207:             try {\n208:                 saslServer = SecurityManagerCompatibility.get().callAs(subject, () ->\n209:                     Sasl.createSaslServer(saslMechanism, \"kafka\", serverAddress().getHostName(), configs, callbackHandler));\n210:                 if (saslServer == null) {\n211:                     throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication with server mechanism \" + saslMechanism);\n212:                 }\n213:             } catch (CompletionException e) {\n214:                 throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication with server mechanism \" + saslMechanism, e.getCause());\n215:             }\n216:         }\n217:     }\n218: \n219:     private SaslServer createSaslKerberosServer(final AuthenticateCallbackHandler saslServerCallbackHandler, final Map<String, ?> configs, Subject subject) throws IOException {\n220:         // server is using a JAAS-authenticated subject: determine service principal name and hostname from kafka server's subject.\n221:         final String servicePrincipal = SaslClientAuthenticator.firstPrincipal(subject);\n222:         KerberosName kerberosName;\n223:         try {\n224:             kerberosName = KerberosName.parse(servicePrincipal);\n225:         } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\n226:             throw new KafkaException(\"Principal has name with unexpected format \" + servicePrincipal);\n227:         }\n228:         final String servicePrincipalName = kerberosName.serviceName();\n229:         final String serviceHostname = kerberosName.hostName();\n230: \n231:         LOG.debug(\"Creating SaslServer for {} with mechanism {}\", kerberosName, saslMechanism);\n232: \n233:         try {\n234:             return SecurityManagerCompatibility.get().callAs(subject, () ->\n235:                     Sasl.createSaslServer(saslMechanism, servicePrincipalName, serviceHostname, configs, saslServerCallbackHandler));\n236:         } catch (CompletionException e) {\n237:             throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication\", e.getCause());\n238:         }\n239:     }\n240: \n241:     /**\n242:      * Evaluates client responses via `SaslServer.evaluateResponse` and returns the issued challenge to the client until\n243:      * authentication succeeds or fails.\n244:      *\n245:      * The messages are sent and received as size delimited bytes that consists of a 4 byte network-ordered size N\n246:      * followed by N bytes representing the opaque payload.\n247:      */\n248:     @SuppressWarnings(\"fallthrough\")\n249:     @Override\n250:     public void authenticate() throws IOException {\n251:         if (saslState != SaslState.REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE) {\n252:             if (netOutBuffer != null && !flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps())\n253:                 return;\n254: \n255:             if (saslServer != null && saslServer.isComplete()) {\n256:                 setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\n257:                 return;\n258:             }\n259: \n260:             // allocate on heap (as opposed to any socket server memory pool)\n261:             if (netInBuffer == null) netInBuffer = new NetworkReceive(saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize, connectionId);\n262: \n263:             try {\n264:                 netInBuffer.readFrom(transportLayer);\n265:             } catch (InvalidReceiveException e) {\n266:                 throw new SaslAuthenticationException(\"Failing SASL authentication due to invalid receive size\", e);\n267:             }\n268:             if (!netInBuffer.complete())\n269:                 return;\n270:             netInBuffer.payload().rewind();\n271:         }\n272:         byte[] clientToken = new byte[netInBuffer.payload().remaining()];\n273:         netInBuffer.payload().get(clientToken, 0, clientToken.length);\n274:         netInBuffer = null; // reset the networkReceive as we read all the data.\n275:         try {\n276:             switch (saslState) {\n277:                 case REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE:\n278:                 case HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST:\n279:                 case HANDSHAKE_REQUEST:\n280:                 case INITIAL_REQUEST:\n281:                     handleKafkaRequest(clientToken);\n282:                     break;\n283:                 case REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM:\n284:                     throw new SaslAuthenticationException(reauthInfo.badMechanismErrorMessage);\n285:                 case AUTHENTICATE:\n286:                     handleSaslToken(clientToken);\n287:                     // When the authentication exchange is complete and no more tokens are expected from the client,\n288:                     // update SASL state. Current SASL state will be updated when outgoing writes to the client complete.\n289:                     if (saslServer.isComplete())\n290:                         setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\n291:                     break;\n292:                 default:\n293:                     break;\n294:             }\n295:         } catch (AuthenticationException e) {\n296:             // Exception will be propagated after response is sent to client\n297:             setSaslState(SaslState.FAILED, e);\n298:         } catch (Exception e) {\n299:             // In the case of IOExceptions and other unexpected exceptions, fail immediately\n300:             saslState = SaslState.FAILED;\n301:             LOG.debug(\"Failed during {}: {}\", reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText(), e.getMessage());\n302:             throw e;\n303:         }\n304:     }\n305: \n306:     @Override\n307:     public KafkaPrincipal principal() {\n308:         Optional<SSLSession> sslSession = transportLayer instanceof SslTransportLayer ?\n309:                 Optional.of(((SslTransportLayer) transportLayer).sslSession()) : Optional.empty();\n310:         SaslAuthenticationContext context = new SaslAuthenticationContext(saslServer, securityProtocol,\n311:                 clientAddress(), listenerName.value(), sslSession);\n312:         KafkaPrincipal principal = principalBuilder.build(context);\n313:         if (ScramMechanism.isScram(saslMechanism) && Boolean.parseBoolean((String) saslServer.getNegotiatedProperty(ScramLoginModule.TOKEN_AUTH_CONFIG))) {\n314:             principal.tokenAuthenticated(true);\n315:         }\n316:         return principal;\n317:     }\n318: \n319:     @Override\n320:     public Optional<KafkaPrincipalSerde> principalSerde() {\n321:         return principalBuilder instanceof KafkaPrincipalSerde ? Optional.of((KafkaPrincipalSerde) principalBuilder) : Optional.empty();\n322:     }\n323: \n324:     @Override\n325:     public boolean complete() {\n326:         return saslState == SaslState.COMPLETE;\n327:     }\n328: \n329:     @Override\n330:     public void handleAuthenticationFailure() throws IOException {\n331:         sendAuthenticationFailureResponse();\n332:     }\n333: \n334:     @Override\n335:     public void close() throws IOException {\n336:         if (principalBuilder instanceof Closeable)\n337:             Utils.closeQuietly((Closeable) principalBuilder, \"principal builder\");\n338:         if (saslServer != null)\n339:             saslServer.dispose();\n340:     }\n341: \n342:     @Override\n343:     public void reauthenticate(ReauthenticationContext reauthenticationContext) throws IOException {\n344:         NetworkReceive saslHandshakeReceive = reauthenticationContext.networkReceive();\n345:         if (saslHandshakeReceive == null)\n346:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n347:                     \"Invalid saslHandshakeReceive in server-side re-authentication context: null\");\n348:         SaslServerAuthenticator previousSaslServerAuthenticator = (SaslServerAuthenticator) reauthenticationContext.previousAuthenticator();\n349:         reauthInfo.reauthenticating(previousSaslServerAuthenticator.saslMechanism,\n350:                 previousSaslServerAuthenticator.principal(), reauthenticationContext.reauthenticationBeginNanos());\n351:         previousSaslServerAuthenticator.close();\n352:         netInBuffer = saslHandshakeReceive;\n353:         LOG.debug(\"Beginning re-authentication: {}\", this);\n354:         netInBuffer.payload().rewind();\n355:         setSaslState(SaslState.REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE);\n356:         authenticate();\n357:     }\n358: \n359:     @Override\n360:     public Long serverSessionExpirationTimeNanos() {\n361:         return reauthInfo.sessionExpirationTimeNanos;\n362:     }\n363: \n364:     @Override\n365:     public Long reauthenticationLatencyMs() {\n366:         return reauthInfo.reauthenticationLatencyMs();\n367:     }\n368: \n369:     @Override\n370:     public boolean connectedClientSupportsReauthentication() {\n371:         return reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication;\n372:     }\n373: \n374:     private void setSaslState(SaslState saslState) {\n375:         setSaslState(saslState, null);\n376:     }\n377: \n378:     private void setSaslState(SaslState saslState, AuthenticationException exception) {\n379:         if (netOutBuffer != null && !netOutBuffer.completed()) {\n380:             pendingSaslState = saslState;\n381:             pendingException = exception;\n382:         } else {\n383:             this.saslState = saslState;\n384:             LOG.debug(\"Set SASL server state to {} during {}\", saslState, reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText());\n385:             this.pendingSaslState = null;\n386:             this.pendingException = null;\n387:             if (exception != null)\n388:                 throw exception;\n389:         }\n390:     }\n391: \n392:     private boolean flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps() throws IOException {\n393:         boolean flushedCompletely = flushNetOutBuffer();\n394:         if (flushedCompletely) {\n395:             transportLayer.removeInterestOps(SelectionKey.OP_WRITE);\n396:             if (pendingSaslState != null)\n397:                 setSaslState(pendingSaslState, pendingException);\n398:         } else\n399:             transportLayer.addInterestOps(SelectionKey.OP_WRITE);\n400:         return flushedCompletely;\n401:     }\n402: \n403:     private boolean flushNetOutBuffer() throws IOException {\n404:         if (!netOutBuffer.completed())\n405:             netOutBuffer.writeTo(transportLayer);\n406:         return netOutBuffer.completed();\n407:     }\n408: \n409:     private InetAddress serverAddress() {\n410:         return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getLocalAddress();\n411:     }\n412: \n413:     private InetAddress clientAddress() {\n414:         return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getInetAddress();\n415:     }\n416: \n417:     private int clientPort() {\n418:         return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getPort();\n419:     }\n420: \n421:     private void handleSaslToken(byte[] clientToken) throws IOException {\n422:         if (!enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders) {\n423:             byte[] response = saslServer.evaluateResponse(clientToken);\n424:             if (saslServer.isComplete()) {\n425:                 reauthInfo.calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs();\n426:                 if (reauthInfo.reauthenticating())\n427:                     reauthInfo.ensurePrincipalUnchanged(principal());\n428:             }\n429:             if (response != null) {\n430:                 netOutBuffer = ByteBufferSend.sizePrefixed(ByteBuffer.wrap(response));\n431:                 flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\n432:             }\n433:         } else {\n434:             ByteBuffer requestBuffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(clientToken);\n435:             RequestHeader header = RequestHeader.parse(requestBuffer);\n436:             ApiKeys apiKey = header.apiKey();\n437:             RequestContext requestContext = new RequestContext(header, connectionId, clientAddress(), Optional.of(clientPort()),\n438:                     KafkaPrincipal.ANONYMOUS, listenerName, securityProtocol, ClientInformation.EMPTY, false);\n439:             RequestAndSize requestAndSize = requestContext.parseRequest(requestBuffer);\n440:             if (apiKey != ApiKeys.SASL_AUTHENTICATE) {\n441:                 IllegalSaslStateException e = new IllegalSaslStateException(\"Unexpected Kafka request of type \" + apiKey + \" during SASL authentication.\");\n442:                 buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext, requestAndSize.request.getErrorResponse(e));\n443:                 throw e;\n444:             }\n445:             short version = header.apiVersion();\n446:             if (!header.isApiVersionSupported()) {\n447:                 // We cannot create an error response if the request version of SaslAuthenticate is not supported\n448:                 // This should not normally occur since clients typically check supported versions using ApiVersionsRequest\n449:                 throw new UnsupportedVersionException(\"Version \" + version + \" is not supported for apiKey \" + apiKey);\n450:             }\n451:             /*\n452:              * The client sends multiple SASL_AUTHENTICATE requests, and the client is known\n453:              * to support the required version if any one of them indicates it supports that\n454:              * version.\n455:              */\n456:             if (!reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication)\n457:                 reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication = version > 0;\n458:             SaslAuthenticateRequest saslAuthenticateRequest = (SaslAuthenticateRequest) requestAndSize.request;\n459: \n460:             try {\n461:                 byte[] responseToken = saslServer.evaluateResponse(\n462:                         Utils.copyArray(saslAuthenticateRequest.data().authBytes()));\n463:                 if (reauthInfo.reauthenticating() && saslServer.isComplete())\n464:                     reauthInfo.ensurePrincipalUnchanged(principal());\n465:                 // For versions with SASL_AUTHENTICATE header, send a response to SASL_AUTHENTICATE request even if token is empty.\n466:                 byte[] responseBytes = responseToken == null ? new byte[0] : responseToken;\n467:                 long sessionLifetimeMs = !saslServer.isComplete() ? 0L\n468:                         : reauthInfo.calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs();\n469:                 sendKafkaResponse(requestContext, new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n470:                         new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n471:                         .setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code())\n472:                         .setAuthBytes(responseBytes)\n473:                         .setSessionLifetimeMs(sessionLifetimeMs)));\n474:             } catch (SaslAuthenticationException e) {\n475:                 buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext,\n476:                         new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n477:                                 new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n478:                                 .setErrorCode(Errors.SASL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.code())\n479:                                 .setErrorMessage(e.getMessage())));\n480:                 throw e;\n481:             } catch (SaslException e) {\n482:                 KerberosError kerberosError = KerberosError.fromException(e);\n483:                 if (kerberosError != null && kerberosError.retriable()) {\n484:                     // Handle retriable Kerberos exceptions as I/O exceptions rather than authentication exceptions\n485:                     throw e;\n486:                 } else {\n487:                     // DO NOT include error message from the `SaslException` in the client response since it may\n488:                     // contain sensitive data like the existence of the user.\n489:                     String errorMessage = \"Authentication failed during \"\n490:                             + reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText()\n491:                             + \" due to invalid credentials with SASL mechanism \" + saslMechanism;\n492:                     buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext, new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n493:                             new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n494:                             .setErrorCode(Errors.SASL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.code())\n495:                             .setErrorMessage(errorMessage)));\n496:                     throw new SaslAuthenticationException(errorMessage, e);\n497:                 }\n498:             }\n499:         }\n500:     }\n501: \n502:     /**\n503:      * @throws InvalidRequestException if the request is not in Kafka format or if the API key is invalid. Clients\n504:      * that support SASL without support for KIP-43 (e.g. Kafka Clients 0.9.x) are in the former bucket - the first\n505:      * packet such clients send is a GSSAPI token starting with 0x60.\n506:      */\n507:     private void handleKafkaRequest(byte[] requestBytes) throws IOException, AuthenticationException {\n508:         try {\n509:             ByteBuffer requestBuffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(requestBytes);\n510:             RequestHeader header = RequestHeader.parse(requestBuffer);\n511:             ApiKeys apiKey = header.apiKey();\n512: \n513:             // Raise an error prior to parsing if the api cannot be handled at this layer. This avoids\n514:             // unnecessary exposure to some of the more complex schema types.\n515:             if (apiKey != ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS && apiKey != ApiKeys.SASL_HANDSHAKE)\n516:                 throw new InvalidRequestException(\"Unexpected Kafka request of type \" + apiKey + \" during SASL handshake.\");\n517: \n518:             LOG.debug(\"Handling Kafka request {} during {}\", apiKey, reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText());\n519: \n520:             RequestContext requestContext = new RequestContext(header, connectionId, clientAddress(), Optional.of(clientPort()),\n521:                     KafkaPrincipal.ANONYMOUS, listenerName, securityProtocol, ClientInformation.EMPTY, false);\n522:             RequestAndSize requestAndSize = requestContext.parseRequest(requestBuffer);\n523: \n524:             // A valid Kafka request was received, we can now update the sasl state\n525:             if (saslState == SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST)\n526:                 setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST);\n527: \n528:             if (apiKey == ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS)\n529:                 handleApiVersionsRequest(requestContext, (ApiVersionsRequest) requestAndSize.request);\n530:             else {\n531:                 String clientMechanism = handleHandshakeRequest(requestContext, (SaslHandshakeRequest) requestAndSize.request);\n532:                 if (!reauthInfo.reauthenticating() || reauthInfo.saslMechanismUnchanged(clientMechanism)) {\n533:                     createSaslServer(clientMechanism);\n534:                     setSaslState(SaslState.AUTHENTICATE);\n535:                 }\n536:             }\n537:         } catch (InvalidRequestException e) {\n538:             if (saslState == SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST) {\n539:                 // InvalidRequestException is thrown if the request is not in Kafka format or if the API key is invalid.\n540:                 // If it's the initial request, this could be an ancient client (see method documentation for more details),\n541:                 // a client configured with the wrong security protocol or a non kafka-client altogether (eg http client).\n542:                 throw new InvalidRequestException(\"Invalid request, potential reasons: kafka client configured with the \" +\n543:                     \"wrong security protocol, it does not support KIP-43 or it is not a kafka client.\", e);\n544:             }\n545:             throw e;\n546:         }\n547:     }\n548: \n549:     private String handleHandshakeRequest(RequestContext context, SaslHandshakeRequest handshakeRequest) throws IOException, UnsupportedSaslMechanismException {\n550:         String clientMechanism = handshakeRequest.data().mechanism();\n551:         short version = context.header.apiVersion();\n552:         if (version >= 1)\n553:             this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(true);\n554:         if (enabledMechanisms.contains(clientMechanism)) {\n555:             LOG.debug(\"Using SASL mechanism '{}' provided by client\", clientMechanism);\n556:             sendKafkaResponse(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\n557:                     new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\n558:             return clientMechanism;\n559:         } else {\n560:             LOG.debug(\"SASL mechanism '{}' requested by client is not supported\", clientMechanism);\n561:             buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\n562:                     new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.UNSUPPORTED_SASL_MECHANISM.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\n563:             throw new UnsupportedSaslMechanismException(\"Unsupported SASL mechanism \" + clientMechanism);\n564:         }\n565:     }\n566: \n567:     // Visible to override for testing\n568:     protected void enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(boolean flag) {\n569:         this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders = flag;\n570:     }\n571: \n572:     private void handleApiVersionsRequest(RequestContext context, ApiVersionsRequest apiVersionsRequest) throws IOException {\n573:         if (saslState != SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST)\n574:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Unexpected ApiVersions request received during SASL authentication state \" + saslState);\n575: \n576:         if (apiVersionsRequest.hasUnsupportedRequestVersion())\n577:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.UNSUPPORTED_VERSION.exception()));\n578:         else if (!apiVersionsRequest.isValid())\n579:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.INVALID_REQUEST.exception()));\n580:         else {\n581:             metadataRegistry.registerClientInformation(new ClientInformation(apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareName(),\n582:                 apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareVersion()));\n583:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionSupplier.apply(apiVersionsRequest.version()));\n584:             setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_REQUEST);\n585:         }\n586:     }\n587: \n588:     /**\n589:      * Build a {@link Send} response on {@link #authenticate()} failure. The actual response is sent out when\n590:      * {@link #sendAuthenticationFailureResponse()} is called.\n591:      */\n592:     private void buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) {\n593:         authenticationFailureSend = context.buildResponseSend(response);\n594:     }\n595: \n596:     /**\n597:      * Send any authentication failure response that may have been previously built.\n598:      */\n599:     private void sendAuthenticationFailureResponse() throws IOException {\n600:         if (authenticationFailureSend == null)\n601:             return;\n602:         sendKafkaResponse(authenticationFailureSend);\n603:         authenticationFailureSend = null;\n604:     }\n605: \n606:     private void sendKafkaResponse(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) throws IOException {\n607:         sendKafkaResponse(context.buildResponseSend(response));\n608:     }\n609: \n610:     private void sendKafkaResponse(Send send) throws IOException {\n611:         netOutBuffer = send;\n612:         flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\n613:     }\n614: \n615:     /**\n616:      * Information related to re-authentication\n617:      */\n618:     private class ReauthInfo {\n619:         public String previousSaslMechanism;\n620:         public KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal;\n621:         public long reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n622:         public Long sessionExpirationTimeNanos;\n623:         public boolean connectedClientSupportsReauthentication;\n624:         public long authenticationEndNanos;\n625:         public String badMechanismErrorMessage;\n626: \n627:         public void reauthenticating(String previousSaslMechanism, KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal,\n628:                 long reauthenticationBeginNanos) {\n629:             this.previousSaslMechanism = Objects.requireNonNull(previousSaslMechanism);\n630:             this.previousKafkaPrincipal = Objects.requireNonNull(previousKafkaPrincipal);\n631:             this.reauthenticationBeginNanos = reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n632:         }\n633: \n634:         public boolean reauthenticating() {\n635:             return previousSaslMechanism != null;\n636:         }\n637: \n638:         public String authenticationOrReauthenticationText() {\n639:             return reauthenticating() ? \"re-authentication\" : \"authentication\";\n640:         }\n641: \n642:         public void ensurePrincipalUnchanged(KafkaPrincipal reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal) throws SaslAuthenticationException {\n643:             if (!previousKafkaPrincipal.equals(reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal)) {\n644:                 throw new SaslAuthenticationException(String.format(\n645:                         \"Cannot change principals during re-authentication from %s.%s: %s.%s\",\n646:                         previousKafkaPrincipal.getPrincipalType(), previousKafkaPrincipal.getName(),\n647:                         reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal.getPrincipalType(), reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal.getName()));\n648:             }\n649:         }\n650: \n651:         /*\n652:          * We define the REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM state because the failed re-authentication\n653:          * metric does not get updated if we send back an error immediately upon the\n654:          * start of re-authentication.\n655:          */\n656:         public boolean saslMechanismUnchanged(String clientMechanism) {\n657:             if (previousSaslMechanism.equals(clientMechanism))\n658:                 return true;\n659:             badMechanismErrorMessage = String.format(\n660:                     \"SASL mechanism '%s' requested by client is not supported for re-authentication of mechanism '%s'\",\n661:                     clientMechanism, previousSaslMechanism);\n662:             LOG.debug(badMechanismErrorMessage);\n663:             setSaslState(SaslState.REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM);\n664:             return false;\n665:         }\n666: \n667:         private long calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs() {\n668:             long retvalSessionLifetimeMs = 0L;\n669:             long authenticationEndMs = time.milliseconds();\n670:             authenticationEndNanos = time.nanoseconds();\n671:             Long credentialExpirationMs = (Long) saslServer\n672:                     .getNegotiatedProperty(SaslInternalConfigs.CREDENTIAL_LIFETIME_MS_SASL_NEGOTIATED_PROPERTY_KEY);\n673:             Long connectionsMaxReauthMs = connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism.get(saslMechanism);\n674:             boolean maxReauthSet = connectionsMaxReauthMs != null && connectionsMaxReauthMs > 0;\n675: \n676:             if (credentialExpirationMs != null || maxReauthSet) {\n677:                 if (credentialExpirationMs == null)\n678:                     retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(connectionsMaxReauthMs);\n679:                 else if (!maxReauthSet)\n680:                     retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs);\n681:                 else\n682:                     retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(Math.min(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs, connectionsMaxReauthMs));\n683: \n684:                 sessionExpirationTimeNanos = authenticationEndNanos + 1000 * 1000 * retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\n685:             }\n686: \n687:             if (credentialExpirationMs != null) {\n688:                 LOG.debug(\n689:                         \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, credential expiration={} ({} ms); session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\",\n690:                         connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(credentialExpirationMs),\n691:                         credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs,\n692:                         new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs), retvalSessionLifetimeMs,\n693:                         retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\n694:             } else {\n695:                 if (sessionExpirationTimeNanos != null)\n696:                     LOG.debug(\n697:                             \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\",\n698:                             connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs),\n699:                             retvalSessionLifetimeMs, retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\n700:                 else\n701:                     LOG.debug(\n702:                             \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; no session expiration, sending 0 ms to client\",\n703:                             connectionsMaxReauthMs);\n704:             }\n705:             return retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\n706:         }\n707: \n708:         public Long reauthenticationLatencyMs() {\n709:             if (!reauthenticating())\n710:                 return null;\n711:             // record at least 1 ms if there is some latency\n712:             long latencyNanos = authenticationEndNanos - reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n713:             return latencyNanos == 0L ? 0L : Math.max(1L, Math.round(latencyNanos / 1000.0 / 1000.0));\n714:         }\n715: \n716:         private long zeroIfNegative(long value) {\n717:             return Math.max(0L, value);\n718:         }        \n719:     }\n720: }\n721: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--be816b82@7bb066906e7979c9d10cdc924186dfe3f49a9428/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java"
  }

License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals;\n\n\nimport java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;\nimport java.util.HashMap;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.SaslAuthenticationException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator.CredentialCache;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.ScramCredential;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.token.delegation.internals.DelegationTokenCache;\n\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\nimport static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertThrows;\nimport static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertTrue;\n\npublic class ScramSaslServerTest {\n\n    private static final String USER_A = \"userA\";\n    private static final String USER_B = \"userB\";\n\n    private ScramFormatter formatter;\n    private ScramSaslServer saslServer;\n\n    @BeforeEach\n    public void setUp() throws Exception {\n        ScramMechanism mechanism = ScramMechanism.SCRAM_SHA_256;\n        formatter  = new ScramFormatter(mechanism);\n        CredentialCache.Cache<ScramCredential> credentialCache = new CredentialCache().createCache(mechanism.mechanismName(), ScramCredential.class);\n        credentialCache.put(USER_A, formatter.generateCredential(\"passwordA\", 4096));\n        credentialCache.put(USER_B, formatter.generateCredential(\"passwordB\", 4096));\n        ScramServerCallbackHandler callbackHandler = new ScramServerCallbackHandler(credentialCache, new DelegationTokenCache(ScramMechanism.mechanismNames()));\n        saslServer = new ScramSaslServer(mechanism, new HashMap<>(), callbackHandler);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void noAuthorizationIdSpecified() throws Exception {\n        byte[] nextChallenge = saslServer.evaluateResponse(clientFirstMessage(USER_A, null));\n        assertTrue(nextChallenge.length > 0, \"Next challenge is empty\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void authorizationIdEqualsAuthenticationId() throws Exception {\n        byte[] nextChallenge = saslServer.evaluateResponse(clientFirstMessage(USER_A, USER_A));\n        assertTrue(nextChallenge.length > 0, \"Next challenge is empty\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void authorizationIdNotEqualsAuthenticationId() {\n        assertThrows(SaslAuthenticationException.class, () -> saslServer.evaluateResponse(clientFirstMessage(USER_A, USER_B)));\n    }\n\n    private byte[] clientFirstMessage(String userName, String authorizationId) {\n        String nonce = formatter.secureRandomString();\n        String authorizationField = authorizationId != null ? \"a=\" + authorizationId : \"\";\n        String firstMessage = String.format(\"n,%s,n=%s,r=%s\", authorizationField, userName, nonce);\n        return firstMessage.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);\n    }\n}"
}

static org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals.ScramMechanism.SCRAM_SHA_256;\nimport static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertEquals;\nimport static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertThrows;\nimport static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.fail;\nimport static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.verify;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\n\npublic class SaslServerAuthenticatorTest {\n\n    private final String clientId = \"clientId\";\n    \n    @Test\n    public void testOversizeRequest() throws IOException {\n        TransportLayer transportLayer = mock(TransportLayer.class);\n        Map<String, ?> configs = Collections.singletonMap(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG,\n                Collections.singletonList(SCRAM_SHA_256.mechanismName()));\n        SaslServerAuthenticator authenticator = setupAuthenticator(configs, transportLayer,\n                SCRAM_SHA_256.mechanismName(), new DefaultChannelMetadataRegistry());\n\n        when(transportLayer.read(any(ByteBuffer.class))).then(invocation -> {\n            invocation.<ByteBuffer>getArgument(0).putInt(BrokerSecurityConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE + 1);\n            return 4;\n        });\n        assertThrows(SaslAuthenticationException.class, authenticator::authenticate);\n        verify(transportLayer).read(any(ByteBuffer.class));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testUnexpectedRequestType() throws IOException {\n        TransportLayer transportLayer = mock(TransportLayer.class);\n        Map<String, ?> configs = Collections.singletonMap(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG,\n                Collections.singletonList(SCRAM_SHA_256.mechanismName()));\n        SaslServerAuthenticator authenticator = setupAuthenticator(configs, transportLayer,\n                SCRAM_SHA_256.mechanismName(), new DefaultChannelMetadataRegistry());\n\n        RequestHeader header = new RequestHeader(ApiKeys.METADATA, (short) 0, clientId, 13243);\n        ByteBuffer headerBuffer = RequestTestUtils.serializeRequestHeader(header);\n\n        when(transportLayer.read(any(ByteBuffer.class))).then(invocation -> {\n            invocation.<ByteBuffer>getArgument(0).putInt(headerBuffer.remaining());\n            return 4;\n        }).then(invocation -> {\n            // serialize only the request header. the authenticator should not parse beyond this\n            invocation.<ByteBuffer>getArgument(0).put(headerBuffer.duplicate());\n            return headerBuffer.remaining();\n        });\n\n        try {\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n            fail(\"Expected authenticate() to raise an exception\");\n        } catch (IllegalSaslStateException e) {\n            // expected exception\n        }\n\n        verify(transportLayer, times(2)).read(any(ByteBuffer.class));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testOldestApiVersionsRequest() throws IOException {\n        testApiVersionsRequest(ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS.oldestVersion(),\n                ClientInformation.UNKNOWN_NAME_OR_VERSION, ClientInformation.UNKNOWN_NAME_OR_VERSION);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testLatestApiVersionsRequest() throws IOException {\n        testApiVersionsRequest(ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS.latestVersion(),\n                \"apache-kafka-java\", AppInfoParser.getVersion());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testSessionExpiresAtTokenExpiryDespiteNoReauthIsSet() throws IOException {\n        String mechanism = OAuthBearerLoginModule.OAUTHBEARER_MECHANISM;\n        Duration tokenExpirationDuration = Duration.ofSeconds(1);\n        SaslServer saslServer = mock(SaslServer.class);\n\n        MockTime time = new MockTime();\n        try (\n                MockedStatic<?> ignored = mockSaslServer(saslServer, mechanism, time, tokenExpirationDuration);\n                MockedStatic<?> ignored2 = mockKafkaPrincipal(\"[principal-type]\", \"[principal-name\");\n                TransportLayer transportLayer = mockTransportLayer()\n        ) {\n\n            SaslServerAuthenticator authenticator = getSaslServerAuthenticatorForOAuth(mechanism, transportLayer, time, 0L);\n\n            mockRequest(saslHandshakeRequest(mechanism), transportLayer);\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n\n            when(saslServer.isComplete()).thenReturn(false).thenReturn(true);\n            mockRequest(saslAuthenticateRequest(), transportLayer);\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n\n            long atTokenExpiryNanos = time.nanoseconds() + tokenExpirationDuration.toNanos();\n            assertEquals(atTokenExpiryNanos, authenticator.serverSessionExpirationTimeNanos());\n\n            ByteBuffer secondResponseSent = getResponses(transportLayer).get(1);\n            consumeSizeAndHeader(secondResponseSent);\n            SaslAuthenticateResponse response = SaslAuthenticateResponse.parse(secondResponseSent, (short) 2);\n            assertEquals(tokenExpirationDuration.toMillis(), response.sessionLifetimeMs());\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testSessionExpiresAtMaxReauthTime() throws IOException {\n        String mechanism = OAuthBearerLoginModule.OAUTHBEARER_MECHANISM;\n        SaslServer saslServer = mock(SaslServer.class);\n        MockTime time = new MockTime(0, 1, 1000);\n        long maxReauthMs = 100L;\n        Duration tokenExpiryGreaterThanMaxReauth = Duration.ofMillis(maxReauthMs).multipliedBy(10);\n\n        try (\n                MockedStatic<?> ignored = mockSaslServer(saslServer, mechanism, time, tokenExpiryGreaterThanMaxReauth);\n                MockedStatic<?> ignored2 = mockKafkaPrincipal(\"[principal-type]\", \"[principal-name\");\n                TransportLayer transportLayer = mockTransportLayer()\n        ) {\n\n            SaslServerAuthenticator authenticator = getSaslServerAuthenticatorForOAuth(mechanism, transportLayer, time, maxReauthMs);\n\n            mockRequest(saslHandshakeRequest(mechanism), transportLayer);\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n\n            when(saslServer.isComplete()).thenReturn(false).thenReturn(true);\n            mockRequest(saslAuthenticateRequest(), transportLayer);\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n\n            long atMaxReauthNanos = time.nanoseconds() + Duration.ofMillis(maxReauthMs).toNanos();\n            assertEquals(atMaxReauthNanos, authenticator.serverSessionExpirationTimeNanos());\n\n            ByteBuffer secondResponseSent = getResponses(transportLayer).get(1);\n            consumeSizeAndHeader(secondResponseSent);\n            SaslAuthenticateResponse response = SaslAuthenticateResponse.parse(secondResponseSent, (short) 2);\n            assertEquals(maxReauthMs, response.sessionLifetimeMs());\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testSessionExpiresAtTokenExpiry() throws IOException {\n        String mechanism = OAuthBearerLoginModule.OAUTHBEARER_MECHANISM;\n        SaslServer saslServer = mock(SaslServer.class);\n        MockTime time = new MockTime(0, 1, 1000);\n        Duration tokenExpiryShorterThanMaxReauth = Duration.ofSeconds(2);\n        long maxReauthMs = tokenExpiryShorterThanMaxReauth.multipliedBy(2).toMillis();\n\n        try (\n                MockedStatic<?> ignored = mockSaslServer(saslServer, mechanism, time, tokenExpiryShorterThanMaxReauth);\n                MockedStatic<?> ignored2 = mockKafkaPrincipal(\"[principal-type]\", \"[principal-name\");\n                TransportLayer transportLayer = mockTransportLayer()\n        ) {\n\n            SaslServerAuthenticator authenticator = getSaslServerAuthenticatorForOAuth(mechanism, transportLayer, time, maxReauthMs);\n\n            mockRequest(saslHandshakeRequest(mechanism), transportLayer);\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n\n            when(saslServer.isComplete()).thenReturn(false).thenReturn(true);\n            mockRequest(saslAuthenticateRequest(), transportLayer);\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n\n            long atTokenExpiryNanos = time.nanoseconds() + tokenExpiryShorterThanMaxReauth.toNanos();\n            assertEquals(atTokenExpiryNanos, authenticator.serverSessionExpirationTimeNanos());\n\n            ByteBuffer secondResponseSent = getResponses(transportLayer).get(1);\n            consumeSizeAndHeader(secondResponseSent);\n            SaslAuthenticateResponse response = SaslAuthenticateResponse.parse(secondResponseSent, (short) 2);\n            assertEquals(tokenExpiryShorterThanMaxReauth.toMillis(), response.sessionLifetimeMs());\n        }\n    }\n\n    private SaslServerAuthenticator getSaslServerAuthenticatorForOAuth(String mechanism, TransportLayer transportLayer, Time time, Long maxReauth) {\n        Map<String, ?> configs = Collections.singletonMap(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG,\n                Collections.singletonList(mechanism));\n        ChannelMetadataRegistry metadataRegistry = new DefaultChannelMetadataRegistry();\n\n        return setupAuthenticator(configs, transportLayer, mechanism, metadataRegistry, time, maxReauth);\n    }\n\n    private MockedStatic<?> mockSaslServer(SaslServer saslServer, String mechanism, Time time, Duration tokenExpirationDuration) throws SaslException {\n        when(saslServer.getMechanismName()).thenReturn(mechanism);\n        when(saslServer.evaluateResponse(any())).thenReturn(new byte[]{});\n        long millisToExpiration = tokenExpirationDuration.toMillis();\n        when(saslServer.getNegotiatedProperty(eq(SaslInternalConfigs.CREDENTIAL_LIFETIME_MS_SASL_NEGOTIATED_PROPERTY_KEY)))\n                .thenReturn(time.milliseconds() + millisToExpiration);\n        return Mockito.mockStatic(Sasl.class, (Answer<SaslServer>) invocation -> saslServer);\n    }\n\n    private MockedStatic<?> mockKafkaPrincipal(String principalType, String name) {\n        KafkaPrincipalBuilder kafkaPrincipalBuilder = mock(KafkaPrincipalBuilder.class);\n        when(kafkaPrincipalBuilder.build(any())).thenReturn(new KafkaPrincipal(principalType, name));\n        MockedStatic<ChannelBuilders> channelBuilders = Mockito.mockStatic(ChannelBuilders.class, Answers.RETURNS_MOCKS);\n        channelBuilders.when(() ->\n                ChannelBuilders.createPrincipalBuilder(anyMap(), any(KerberosShortNamer.class), any(SslPrincipalMapper.class))\n        ).thenReturn(kafkaPrincipalBuilder);\n        return channelBuilders;\n    }\n\n    private void consumeSizeAndHeader(ByteBuffer responseBuffer) {\n        responseBuffer.getInt();\n        ResponseHeader.parse(responseBuffer, (short) 1);\n    }\n\n    private List<ByteBuffer> getResponses(TransportLayer transportLayer) throws IOException {\n        ArgumentCaptor<ByteBuffer[]> buffersCaptor = ArgumentCaptor.forClass(ByteBuffer[].class);\n        verify(transportLayer, times(2)).write(buffersCaptor.capture());\n        return buffersCaptor.getAllValues().stream()\n                .map(this::concatBuffers)\n                .collect(Collectors.toList());\n    }\n\n    private ByteBuffer concatBuffers(ByteBuffer[] buffers) {\n        int combinedCapacity = 0;\n        for (ByteBuffer buffer : buffers) {\n            combinedCapacity += buffer.capacity();\n        }\n        if (combinedCapacity > 0) {\n            ByteBuffer concat = ByteBuffer.allocate(combinedCapacity);\n            for (ByteBuffer buffer : buffers) {\n                concat.put(buffer);\n            }\n            return safeFlip(concat);\n        } else {\n            return ByteBuffer.allocate(0);\n        }"
}

org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.ScramLoginModule;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals.ScramMechanism;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\nimport org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n\nimport javax.net.ssl.SSLSession;\nimport javax.security.auth.Subject;\nimport javax.security.sasl.Sasl;\nimport javax.security.sasl.SaslException;\nimport javax.security.sasl.SaslServer;\nimport java.io.Closeable;\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.net.InetAddress;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.nio.channels.SelectionKey;\nimport java.security.PrivilegedActionException;\nimport java.security.PrivilegedExceptionAction;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Date;\nimport java.util.HashSet;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Objects;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.function.Supplier;\n\npublic class SaslServerAuthenticator implements Authenticator {\n    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(SaslServerAuthenticator.class);\n\n    /**\n     * The internal state transitions for initial authentication of a channel on the\n     * server side are declared in order, starting with {@link #INITIAL_REQUEST} and\n     * ending in either {@link #COMPLETE} or {@link #FAILED}.\n     * <p>\n     * Re-authentication of a channel on the server side starts with the state\n     * {@link #REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE}. It may then flow to\n     * {@link #REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM} before a transition to {@link #FAILED} if\n     * re-authentication is attempted with a mechanism different than the original\n     * one; otherwise it joins the authentication flow at the {@link #AUTHENTICATE}\n     * state and likewise ends at either {@link #COMPLETE} or {@link #FAILED}.\n     */\n    private enum SaslState {\n        INITIAL_REQUEST,               // May be GSSAPI token, SaslHandshake or ApiVersions for authentication\n        HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST, // May be SaslHandshake or ApiVersions\n        HANDSHAKE_REQUEST,             // After an ApiVersions request, next request must be SaslHandshake\n        AUTHENTICATE,                  // Authentication tokens (SaslHandshake v1 and above indicate SaslAuthenticate headers)\n        COMPLETE,                      // Authentication completed successfully\n        FAILED,                        // Authentication failed\n        REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE,      // Initial state for re-authentication, processes SASL handshake request\n        REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM,          // When re-authentication requested with wrong mechanism, generate exception\n    }\n\n    private final SecurityProtocol securityProtocol;\n    private final ListenerName listenerName;\n    private final String connectionId;\n    private final Map<String, Subject> subjects;\n    private final TransportLayer transportLayer;\n    private final List<String> enabledMechanisms;\n    private final Map<String, ?> configs;\n    private final KafkaPrincipalBuilder principalBuilder;\n    private final Map<String, AuthenticateCallbackHandler> callbackHandlers;\n    private final Map<String, Long> connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism;\n    private final Time time;\n    private final ReauthInfo reauthInfo;\n    private final ChannelMetadataRegistry metadataRegistry;\n    private final Supplier<ApiVersionsResponse> apiVersionSupplier;\n\n    // Current SASL state\n    private SaslState saslState = SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST;\n    // Next SASL state to be set when outgoing writes associated with the current SASL state complete\n    private SaslState pendingSaslState = null;\n    // Exception that will be thrown by `authenticate()` when SaslState is set to FAILED after outbound writes complete\n    private AuthenticationException pendingException = null;\n    private SaslServer saslServer;\n    private String saslMechanism;\n\n    // buffers used in `authenticate`\n    private Integer saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize;\n    private NetworkReceive netInBuffer;\n    private Send netOutBuffer;\n    private Send authenticationFailureSend = null;\n    // flag indicating if sasl tokens are sent as Kafka SaslAuthenticate request/responses\n    private boolean enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders;\n\n    public SaslServerAuthenticator(Map<String, ?> configs,\n                                   Map<String, AuthenticateCallbackHandler> callbackHandlers,\n                                   String connectionId,\n                                   Map<String, Subject> subjects,\n                                   KerberosShortNamer kerberosNameParser,\n                                   ListenerName listenerName,\n                                   SecurityProtocol securityProtocol,\n                                   TransportLayer transportLayer,\n                                   Map<String, Long> connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism,\n                                   ChannelMetadataRegistry metadataRegistry,\n                                   Time time,\n                                   Supplier<ApiVersionsResponse> apiVersionSupplier) {\n        this.callbackHandlers = callbackHandlers;\n        this.connectionId = connectionId;\n        this.subjects = subjects;\n        this.listenerName = listenerName;\n        this.securityProtocol = securityProtocol;\n        this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders = false;\n        this.transportLayer = transportLayer;\n        this.connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism = connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism;\n        this.time = time;\n        this.reauthInfo = new ReauthInfo();\n        this.metadataRegistry = metadataRegistry;\n        this.apiVersionSupplier = apiVersionSupplier;\n\n        this.configs = configs;\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        List<String> enabledMechanisms = (List<String>) this.configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG);\n        if (enabledMechanisms == null || enabledMechanisms.isEmpty())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"No SASL mechanisms are enabled\");\n        this.enabledMechanisms = new ArrayList<>(new HashSet<>(enabledMechanisms));\n        for (String mechanism : this.enabledMechanisms) {\n            if (!callbackHandlers.containsKey(mechanism))\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Callback handler not specified for SASL mechanism \" + mechanism);\n            if (!subjects.containsKey(mechanism))\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Subject cannot be null for SASL mechanism \" + mechanism);\n            LOG.trace(\"{} for mechanism={}: {}\", BrokerSecurityConfigs.CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_CONFIG, mechanism,\n                    connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism.get(mechanism));\n        }\n\n        // Note that the old principal builder does not support SASL, so we do not need to pass the\n        // authenticator or the transport layer\n        this.principalBuilder = ChannelBuilders.createPrincipalBuilder(configs, kerberosNameParser, null);\n\n        saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize = (Integer) configs.get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE_CONFIG);\n        if (saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize == null)\n            saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize = BrokerSecurityConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE;\n    }\n\n    private void createSaslServer(String mechanism) throws IOException {\n        this.saslMechanism = mechanism;\n        Subject subject = subjects.get(mechanism);"
}

Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/CredentialCache.java 2>/dev/null",
  "description": "Read CredentialCache.java HEAD version"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator;\n\nimport java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;\n\npublic class CredentialCache {\n\n    private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, Cache<?>> cacheMap = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n\n    public <C> Cache<C> createCache(String mechanism, Class<C> credentialClass) {\n        Cache<C> cache = new Cache<>(credentialClass);\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        Cache<C> oldCache = (Cache<C>) cacheMap.putIfAbsent(mechanism, cache);\n        return oldCache == null ? cache : oldCache;\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    public <C> Cache<C> cache(String mechanism, Class<C> credentialClass) {\n        Cache<?> cache = cacheMap.get(mechanism);\n        if (cache != null) {\n            if (cache.credentialClass() != credentialClass)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid credential class \" + credentialClass + \", expected \" + cache.credentialClass());\n            return (Cache<C>) cache;\n        } else\n            return null;\n    }\n\n    public static class Cache<C> {\n        private final Class<C> credentialClass;\n        private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, C> credentials;\n\n        public Cache(Class<C> credentialClass) {\n            this.credentialClass = credentialClass;\n            this.credentials = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n        }\n\n        public C get(String username) {\n            return credentials.get(username);\n        }\n\n        public C put(String username, C credential) {\n            return credentials.put(username, credential);\n        }\n\n        public C remove(String username) {\n            return credentials.remove(username);\n        }\n\n        public Class<C> credentialClass() {\n            return credentialClass;\n        }\n    }\n}"
}

License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage kafka.security.authorizer\n\nimport java.{lang, util}\nimport java.util.concurrent.{CompletableFuture, CompletionStage}\nimport com.typesafe.scalalogging.Logger\nimport kafka.server.{KafkaConfig, KafkaServer}\nimport kafka.utils._\nimport kafka.utils.Implicits._\nimport kafka.zk._\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.Endpoint\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.acl._\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.acl.AclOperation._\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.acl.AclPermissionType.{ALLOW, DENY}\nimport org.apache.kafka.security.authorizer.AclEntry.RESOURCE_SEPARATOR\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.{ApiException, InvalidRequestException, UnsupportedVersionException}\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.ApiKeys\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.resource._\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.KafkaPrincipal\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.{SecurityUtils, Time}\nimport org.apache.kafka.security.authorizer.AclEntry\nimport org.apache.kafka.server.authorizer.AclDeleteResult.AclBindingDeleteResult\nimport org.apache.kafka.server.authorizer._\nimport org.apache.kafka.server.config.ReplicationConfigs\nimport org.apache.kafka.server.common.MetadataVersion.IBP_2_0_IV1\nimport org.apache.kafka.server.config.ZkConfigs\nimport org.apache.zookeeper.client.ZKClientConfig\n\nimport scala.annotation.nowarn\nimport scala.collection.mutable.ArrayBuffer\nimport scala.collection.{Seq, immutable, mutable}\nimport scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\nimport scala.util.{Failure, Random, Success, Try}\n\nobject AclAuthorizer {\n  // Optional override zookeeper cluster configuration where acls will be stored. If not specified,\n  // acls will be stored in the same zookeeper where all other kafka broker metadata is stored.\n  val configPrefix: String = \"authorizer.\"\n  private val ZkUrlProp = s\"${configPrefix}zookeeper.url\"\n  private val ZkConnectionTimeOutProp = s\"${configPrefix}zookeeper.connection.timeout.ms\"\n  private val ZkSessionTimeOutProp = s\"${configPrefix}zookeeper.session.timeout.ms\"\n  private val ZkMaxInFlightRequests = s\"${configPrefix}zookeeper.max.in.flight.requests\"\n\n  // Semi-colon separated list of users that will be treated as super users and will have access to all the resources\n  // for all actions from all hosts, defaults to no super users.\n  val SuperUsersProp: String = \"super.users\"\n  // If set to true when no acls are found for a resource, authorizer allows access to everyone. Defaults to false.\n  val AllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFoundProp: String = \"allow.everyone.if.no.acl.found\"\n\n  case class VersionedAcls(acls: Set[AclEntry], zkVersion: Int) {\n    def exists: Boolean = zkVersion != ZkVersion.UnknownVersion\n  }\n\n  private class AclSeqs(seqs: Seq[AclEntry]*) {\n    def find(p: AclEntry => Boolean): Option[AclEntry] = {\n      // Lazily iterate through the inner `Seq` elements and stop as soon as we find a match\n      val it = seqs.iterator.flatMap(_.find(p))\n      if (it.hasNext) Some(it.next())\n      else None\n    }\n\n    def isEmpty: Boolean = !seqs.exists(_.nonEmpty)\n  }\n\n  val NoAcls: VersionedAcls = VersionedAcls(Set.empty, ZkVersion.UnknownVersion)\n  val WildcardHost: String = \"*\"\n\n  // Orders by resource type, then resource pattern type and finally reverse ordering by name.\n  class ResourceOrdering extends Ordering[ResourcePattern] {\n\n    def compare(a: ResourcePattern, b: ResourcePattern): Int = {\n      val rt = a.resourceType.compareTo(b.resourceType)\n      if (rt != 0)\n        rt\n      else {\n        val rnt = a.patternType.compareTo(b.patternType)\n        if (rnt != 0)\n          rnt\n        else\n          (a.name compare b.name) * -1\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  private[authorizer] def zkClientConfigFromKafkaConfigAndMap(kafkaConfig: KafkaConfig, configMap: mutable.Map[String, _<:Any]): ZKClientConfig = {\n    val zkSslClientEnable = configMap.get(AclAuthorizer.configPrefix + ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CLIENT_ENABLE_CONFIG).\n      map(_.toString.trim).getOrElse(kafkaConfig.zkSslClientEnable.toString).toBoolean\n    if (!zkSslClientEnable)\n      new ZKClientConfig\n    else {\n      // start with the base config from the Kafka configuration\n      // be sure to force creation since the zkSslClientEnable property in the kafkaConfig could be false\n      val zkClientConfig = KafkaServer.zkClientConfigFromKafkaConfig(kafkaConfig, forceZkSslClientEnable = true)\n      // add in any prefixed overlays\n      ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CONFIG_TO_SYSTEM_PROPERTY_MAP.asScala.forKeyValue { (kafkaProp, sysProp) =>\n        configMap.get(AclAuthorizer.configPrefix + kafkaProp).foreach { prefixedValue =>\n          zkClientConfig.setProperty(sysProp,\n            if (kafkaProp == ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_ENDPOINT_IDENTIFICATION_ALGORITHM_CONFIG)\n              (prefixedValue.toString.trim.toUpperCase == \"HTTPS\").toString\n            else\n              prefixedValue.toString.trim)\n        }\n      }\n      zkClientConfig\n    }\n  }\n\n  private def validateAclBinding(aclBinding: AclBinding): Unit = {\n    if (aclBinding.isUnknown)\n      throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"ACL binding contains unknown elements\")\n    if (aclBinding.pattern().name().contains(\"/\"))\n      throw new IllegalArgumentException(s\"ACL binding contains invalid resource name: ${aclBinding.pattern().name()}\")\n  }\n\n  def loadAllAcls(\n    zkClient: KafkaZkClient,\n    logger: Logging,\n    aclConsumer: (ResourcePattern, VersionedAcls) => Unit\n  ): Unit = {\n    ZkAclStore.stores.foreach { store =>\n      val resourceTypes = zkClient.getResourceTypes(store.patternType)\n      for (rType <- resourceTypes) {\n        val resourceType = Try(SecurityUtils.resourceType(rType))\n        resourceType match {\n          case Success(resourceTypeObj) =>\n            val resourceNames = zkClient.getResourceNames(store.patternType, resourceTypeObj)\n            for (resourceName <- resourceNames) {\n              val resource = new ResourcePattern(resourceTypeObj, resourceName, store.patternType)\n              val versionedAcls = getAclsFromZk(zkClient, resource)\n              aclConsumer.apply(resource, versionedAcls)\n            }\n          case Failure(_) => logger.warn(s\"Ignoring unknown ResourceType: $rType\")\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  private def getAclsFromZk(zkClient: KafkaZkClient, resource: ResourcePattern): VersionedAcls = {\n    zkClient.getVersionedAclsForResource(resource)\n  }\n}\n\nclass AclAuthorizer extends Authorizer with Logging {\n  import kafka.security.authorizer.AclAuthorizer._\n\n  private[security] val authorizerLogger = Logger(\"kafka.authorizer.logger\")\n  private var superUsers = Set.empty[KafkaPrincipal]\n  private var shouldAllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFound = false\n  private var zkClient: KafkaZkClient = _\n  private var aclChangeListeners: Iterable[AclChangeSubscription] = Iterable.empty\n  private var extendedAclSupport: Boolean = _\n\n  @volatile\n  private var aclCache = new scala.collection.immutable.TreeMap[ResourcePattern, VersionedAcls]()(new ResourceOrdering)\n\n  @volatile\n  private var resourceCache = new scala.collection.immutable.HashMap[ResourceTypeKey,\n    scala.collection.immutable.HashSet[String]]()\n\n  private val lock = new Object()\n\n  // The maximum number of times we should try to update the resource acls in zookeeper before failing;\n  // This should never occur, but is a safeguard just in case.\n  protected[security] var maxUpdateRetries = 10\n\n  private val retryBackoffMs = 100\n  private val retryBackoffJitterMs = 50\n\n  /**\n   * Guaranteed to be called before any authorize call is made.\n   */\n  override def configure(javaConfigs: util.Map[String, _]): Unit = {\n    val configs = javaConfigs.asScala\n    val props = new java.util.Properties()\n    configs.forKeyValue { (key, value) => props.put(key, value.toString.trim) }\n\n    superUsers = configs.get(AclAuthorizer.SuperUsersProp).collect {\n      case str: String if str.nonEmpty => str.split(\";\").map(s => SecurityUtils.parseKafkaPrincipal(s.trim)).toSet\n    }.getOrElse(Set.empty[KafkaPrincipal])\n\n    shouldAllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFound = configs.get(AclAuthorizer.AllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFoundProp).exists(_.toString.trim.toBoolean)\n\n    // Use `KafkaConfig` in order to get the default ZK config values if not present in `javaConfigs`. Note that this\n    // means that `KafkaConfig.zkConnect` must always be set by the user (even if `AclAuthorizer.ZkUrlProp` is also\n    // set)."
}

{
