Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "                    clientMechanism, previousSaslMechanism);\n            LOG.debug(badMechanismErrorMessage);\n            setSaslState(SaslState.REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM);\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        private long calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs() {\n            long retvalSessionLifetimeMs = 0L;\n            long authenticationEndMs = time.milliseconds();\n            authenticationEndNanos = time.nanoseconds();\n            Long credentialExpirationMs = (Long) saslServer\n                    .getNegotiatedProperty(SaslInternalConfigs.CREDENTIAL_LIFETIME_MS_SASL_NEGOTIATED_PROPERTY_KEY);\n            Long connectionsMaxReauthMs = connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism.get(saslMechanism);\n            boolean maxReauthSet = connectionsMaxReauthMs != null && connectionsMaxReauthMs > 0;\n\n            if (credentialExpirationMs != null || maxReauthSet) {\n                if (credentialExpirationMs == null)\n                    retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(connectionsMaxReauthMs);\n                else if (!maxReauthSet)\n                    retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs);\n                else\n                    retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(Math.min(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs, connectionsMaxReauthMs));\n\n                sessionExpirationTimeNanos = authenticationEndNanos + 1000 * 1000 * retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\n            }\n\n            if (credentialExpirationMs != null) {\n                LOG.debug(\n                        \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, credential expiration={} ({} ms); session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\",\n                        connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(credentialExpirationMs),\n                        credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs,\n                        new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs), retvalSessionLifetimeMs,\n                        retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\n            } else {\n                if (sessionExpirationTimeNanos != null)\n                    LOG.debug(\n                            \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\",\n                            connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs),\n                            retvalSessionLifetimeMs, retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\n                else\n                    LOG.debug(\n                            \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; no session expiration, sending 0 ms to client\",\n                            connectionsMaxReauthMs);\n            }\n            return retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\n        }\n\n        public Long reauthenticationLatencyMs() {\n            if (!reauthenticating())\n                return null;\n            // record at least 1 ms if there is some latency"
}
39. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:core/src/main/scala/kafka/security/authorizer/AclAuthorizer.scala 2>/dev/null | sed -n '475,580p'",
  "description": "Read authorizeAction and matchingAclExists methods in full"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "  private def authorizeAction(requestContext: AuthorizableRequestContext, action: Action): AuthorizationResult = {\n    val resource = action.resourcePattern\n    if (resource.patternType != PatternType.LITERAL) {\n      throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Only literal resources are supported. Got: \" + resource.patternType)\n    }\n\n    // ensure we compare identical classes\n    val sessionPrincipal = requestContext.principal\n    val principal = if (classOf[KafkaPrincipal] != sessionPrincipal.getClass)\n      new KafkaPrincipal(sessionPrincipal.getPrincipalType, sessionPrincipal.getName)\n    else\n      sessionPrincipal\n\n    val host = requestContext.clientAddress.getHostAddress\n    val operation = action.operation\n\n    def isEmptyAclAndAuthorized(acls: AclSeqs): Boolean = {\n      if (acls.isEmpty) {\n        // No ACLs found for this resource, permission is determined by value of config allow.everyone.if.no.acl.found\n        authorizerLogger.debug(s\"No acl found for resource $resource, authorized = $shouldAllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFound\")\n        shouldAllowEveryoneIfNoAclIsFound\n      } else false\n    }\n\n    def denyAclExists(acls: AclSeqs): Boolean = {\n      // Check if there are any Deny ACLs which would forbid this operation.\n      matchingAclExists(operation, resource, principal, host, DENY, acls)\n    }\n\n    def allowAclExists(acls: AclSeqs): Boolean = {\n      // Check if there are any Allow ACLs which would allow this operation.\n      // Allowing read, write, delete, or alter implies allowing describe.\n      // See #{org.apache.kafka.common.acl.AclOperation} for more details about ACL inheritance.\n      val allowOps = operation match {\n        case DESCRIBE => Set[AclOperation](DESCRIBE, READ, WRITE, DELETE, ALTER)\n        case DESCRIBE_CONFIGS => Set[AclOperation](DESCRIBE_CONFIGS, ALTER_CONFIGS)\n        case _ => Set[AclOperation](operation)\n      }\n      allowOps.exists(operation => matchingAclExists(operation, resource, principal, host, ALLOW, acls))\n    }\n\n    def aclsAllowAccess = {\n      // we allow an operation if no acls are found and user has configured to allow all users\n      // when no acls are found or if no deny acls are found and at least one allow acls matches.\n      val acls = matchingAcls(resource.resourceType, resource.name)\n      isEmptyAclAndAuthorized(acls) || (!denyAclExists(acls) && allowAclExists(acls))\n    }\n\n    // Evaluate if operation is allowed\n    val authorized = isSuperUser(principal) || aclsAllowAccess\n\n    logAuditMessage(requestContext, action, authorized)\n    if (authorized) AuthorizationResult.ALLOWED else AuthorizationResult.DENIED\n  }\n\n  private def isSuperUser(principal: KafkaPrincipal): Boolean = {\n    if (superUsers.contains(principal)) {\n      authorizerLogger.debug(s\"principal = $principal is a super user, allowing operation without checking acls.\")\n      true\n    } else false\n  }\n\n  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n  private def matchingAcls(resourceType: ResourceType, resourceName: String): AclSeqs = {\n    // this code is performance sensitive, make sure to run AclAuthorizerBenchmark after any changes\n\n    // save aclCache reference to a local val to get a consistent view of the cache during acl updates.\n    val aclCacheSnapshot = aclCache\n    val wildcard = aclCacheSnapshot.get(new ResourcePattern(resourceType, ResourcePattern.WILDCARD_RESOURCE, PatternType.LITERAL))\n      .map(_.acls.toBuffer)\n      .getOrElse(mutable.Buffer.empty)\n\n    val literal = aclCacheSnapshot.get(new ResourcePattern(resourceType, resourceName, PatternType.LITERAL))\n      .map(_.acls.toBuffer)\n      .getOrElse(mutable.Buffer.empty)\n\n    val prefixed = new ArrayBuffer[AclEntry]\n    aclCacheSnapshot\n      .from(new ResourcePattern(resourceType, resourceName, PatternType.PREFIXED))\n      .to(new ResourcePattern(resourceType, resourceName.take(1), PatternType.PREFIXED))\n      .forKeyValue { (resource, acls) =>\n        if (resourceName.startsWith(resource.name)) prefixed ++= acls.acls\n      }\n\n    new AclSeqs(prefixed, wildcard, literal)\n  }\n\n  private def matchingAclExists(operation: AclOperation,\n                                resource: ResourcePattern,\n                                principal: KafkaPrincipal,\n                                host: String,\n                                permissionType: AclPermissionType,\n                                acls: AclSeqs): Boolean = {\n    acls.find { acl =>\n      acl.permissionType == permissionType &&\n        (acl.kafkaPrincipal == principal || acl.kafkaPrincipal == AclEntry.WILDCARD_PRINCIPAL) &&\n        (operation == acl.operation || acl.operation == AclOperation.ALL) &&\n        (acl.host == host || acl.host == AclEntry.WILDCARD_HOST)\n    }.exists { acl =>\n      authorizerLogger.debug(s\"operation = $operation on resource = $resource from host = $host is $permissionType based on acl = $acl\")\n      true\n    }\n  }\n\n  private def loadCache(): Unit = {\n    lock synchronized  {"
}

Output
{
  "interrupted": false,

  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    private void sendKafkaResponse(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) throws IOException {\n        sendKafkaResponse(context.buildResponseSend(response));\n    }\n\n    private void sendKafkaResponse(Send send) throws IOException {\n        netOutBuffer = send;\n        flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Information related to re-authentication\n     */\n    private class ReauthInfo {\n        public String previousSaslMechanism;\n        public KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal;\n        public long reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n        public Long sessionExpirationTimeNanos;\n        public boolean connectedClientSupportsReauthentication;\n        public long authenticationEndNanos;\n        public String badMechanismErrorMessage;\n\n        public void reauthenticating(String previousSaslMechanism, KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal,\n                long reauthenticationBeginNanos) {\n            this.previousSaslMechanism = Objects.requireNonNull(previousSaslMechanism);\n            this.previousKafkaPrincipal = Objects.requireNonNull(previousKafkaPrincipal);\n            this.reauthenticationBeginNanos = reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n        }\n\n        public boolean reauthenticating() {\n            return previousSaslMechanism != null;\n        }\n\n        public String authenticationOrReauthenticationText() {\n            return reauthenticating() ? \"re-authentication\" : \"authentication\";\n        }\n\n        public void ensurePrincipalUnchanged(KafkaPrincipal reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal) throws SaslAuthenticationException {\n            if (!previousKafkaPrincipal.equals(reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal)) {\n                throw new SaslAuthenticationException(String.format(\n                        \"Cannot change principals during re-authentication from %s.%s: %s.%s\",\n                        previousKafkaPrincipal.getPrincipalType(), previousKafkaPrincipal.getName(),\n                        reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal.getPrincipalType(), reauthenticatedKafkaPrincipal.getName()));\n            }\n        }\n\n        /*\n         * We define the REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM state because the failed re-authentication\n         * metric does not get updated if we send back an error immediately upon the\n         * start of re-authentication.\n         */\n        public boolean saslMechanismUnchanged(String clientMechanism) {\n            if (previousSaslMechanism.equals(clientMechanism))\n                return true;\n            badMechanismErrorMessage = String.format(\n                    \"SASL mechanism '%s' requested by client is not supported for re-authentication of mechanism '%s'\",\n                    clientMechanism, previousSaslMechanism);\n            LOG.debug(badMechanismErrorMessage);\n            setSaslState(SaslState.REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM);\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        private long calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs() {\n            long retvalSessionLifetimeMs = 0L;\n            long authenticationEndMs = time.milliseconds();\n            authenticationEndNanos = time.nanoseconds();\n            Long credentialExpirationMs = (Long) saslServer\n                    .getNegotiatedProperty(SaslInternalConfigs.CREDENTIAL_LIFETIME_MS_SASL_NEGOTIATED_PROPERTY_KEY);\n            Long connectionsMaxReauthMs = connectionsMaxReauthMsByMechanism.get(saslMechanism);\n            boolean maxReauthSet = connectionsMaxReauthMs != null && connectionsMaxReauthMs > 0;\n\n            if (credentialExpirationMs != null || maxReauthSet) {\n                if (credentialExpirationMs == null)\n                    retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(connectionsMaxReauthMs);\n                else if (!maxReauthSet)\n                    retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs);\n                else\n                    retvalSessionLifetimeMs = zeroIfNegative(Math.min(credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs, connectionsMaxReauthMs));\n\n                sessionExpirationTimeNanos = authenticationEndNanos + 1000 * 1000 * retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\n            }\n\n            if (credentialExpirationMs != null) {\n                LOG.debug(\n                        \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, credential expiration={} ({} ms); session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\",\n                        connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(credentialExpirationMs),\n                        credentialExpirationMs - authenticationEndMs,\n                        new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs), retvalSessionLifetimeMs,\n                        retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\n            } else {\n                if (sessionExpirationTimeNanos != null)\n                    LOG.debug(\n                            \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; session expiration = {} ({} ms), sending {} ms to client\",\n                            connectionsMaxReauthMs, new Date(authenticationEndMs + retvalSessionLifetimeMs),\n                            retvalSessionLifetimeMs, retvalSessionLifetimeMs);\n                else\n                    LOG.debug(\n                            \"Authentication complete; session max lifetime from broker config={} ms, no credential expiration; no session expiration, sending 0 ms to client\",\n                            connectionsMaxReauthMs);\n            }\n            return retvalSessionLifetimeMs;\n        }\n\n        public Long reauthenticationLatencyMs() {\n            if (!reauthenticating())\n                return null;\n            // record at least 1 ms if there is some latency\n            long latencyNanos = authenticationEndNanos - reauthenticationBeginNanos;\n            return latencyNanos == 0L ? 0L : Math.max(1L, Math.round(latencyNanos / 1000.0 / 1000.0));\n        }\n\n        private long zeroIfNegative(long value) {\n            return Math.max(0L, value);\n        }        \n    }\n}"
}

  "stdout": "        Subject subject = subjects.get(mechanism);\n        final AuthenticateCallbackHandler callbackHandler = callbackHandlers.get(mechanism);\n        if (mechanism.equals(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM)) {\n            saslServer = createSaslKerberosServer(callbackHandler, configs, subject);\n        } else {\n            try {\n                saslServer = Subject.doAs(subject, (PrivilegedExceptionAction<SaslServer>) () ->\n                    Sasl.createSaslServer(saslMechanism, \"kafka\", serverAddress().getHostName(), configs, callbackHandler));\n                if (saslServer == null) {\n                    throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication with server mechanism \" + saslMechanism);\n                }\n            } catch (PrivilegedActionException e) {\n                throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication with server mechanism \" + saslMechanism, e.getCause());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private SaslServer createSaslKerberosServer(final AuthenticateCallbackHandler saslServerCallbackHandler, final Map<String, ?> configs, Subject subject) throws IOException {\n        // server is using a JAAS-authenticated subject: determine service principal name and hostname from kafka server's subject.\n        final String servicePrincipal = SaslClientAuthenticator.firstPrincipal(subject);\n        KerberosName kerberosName;\n        try {\n            kerberosName = KerberosName.parse(servicePrincipal);\n        } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\n            throw new KafkaException(\"Principal has name with unexpected format \" + servicePrincipal);\n        }\n        final String servicePrincipalName = kerberosName.serviceName();\n        final String serviceHostname = kerberosName.hostName();\n\n        LOG.debug(\"Creating SaslServer for {} with mechanism {}\", kerberosName, saslMechanism);\n\n        try {\n            return Subject.doAs(subject, (PrivilegedExceptionAction<SaslServer>) () ->\n                    Sasl.createSaslServer(saslMechanism, servicePrincipalName, serviceHostname, configs, saslServerCallbackHandler));\n        } catch (PrivilegedActionException e) {\n            throw new SaslException(\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication\", e.getCause());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Evaluates client responses via `SaslServer.evaluateResponse` and returns the issued challenge to the client until\n     * authentication succeeds or fails.\n     *\n     * The messages are sent and received as size delimited bytes that consists of a 4 byte network-ordered size N\n     * followed by N bytes representing the opaque payload.\n     */\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"fallthrough\")\n    @Override\n    public void authenticate() throws IOException {\n        if (saslState != SaslState.REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE) {\n            if (netOutBuffer != null && !flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps())\n                return;\n\n            if (saslServer != null && saslServer.isComplete()) {\n                setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // allocate on heap (as opposed to any socket server memory pool)\n            if (netInBuffer == null) netInBuffer = new NetworkReceive(saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize, connectionId);\n\n            try {\n                netInBuffer.readFrom(transportLayer);\n            } catch (InvalidReceiveException e) {\n                throw new SaslAuthenticationException(\"Failing SASL authentication due to invalid receive size\", e);\n            }\n            if (!netInBuffer.complete())\n                return;\n            netInBuffer.payload().rewind();\n        }\n        byte[] clientToken = new byte[netInBuffer.payload().remaining()];\n        netInBuffer.payload().get(clientToken, 0, clientToken.length);\n        netInBuffer = null; // reset the networkReceive as we read all the data.\n        try {\n            switch (saslState) {\n                case REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE:\n                case HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST:\n                case HANDSHAKE_REQUEST:\n                    handleKafkaRequest(clientToken);\n                    break;\n                case REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM:\n                    throw new SaslAuthenticationException(reauthInfo.badMechanismErrorMessage);\n                case INITIAL_REQUEST:\n                    if (handleKafkaRequest(clientToken))\n                        break;\n                    // For default GSSAPI, fall through to authenticate using the client token as the first GSSAPI packet.\n                    // This is required for interoperability with 0.9.0.x clients which do not send handshake request\n                case AUTHENTICATE:\n                    handleSaslToken(clientToken);\n                    // When the authentication exchange is complete and no more tokens are expected from the client,\n                    // update SASL state. Current SASL state will be updated when outgoing writes to the client complete.\n                    if (saslServer.isComplete())\n                        setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\n                    break;\n                default:\n                    break;\n            }\n        } catch (AuthenticationException e) {\n            // Exception will be propagated after response is sent to client\n            setSaslState(SaslState.FAILED, e);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            // In the case of IOExceptions and other unexpected exceptions, fail immediately\n            saslState = SaslState.FAILED;\n            LOG.debug(\"Failed during {}: {}\", reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText(), e.getMessage());\n            throw e;\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KafkaPrincipal principal() {\n        Optional<SSLSession> sslSession = transportLayer instanceof SslTransportLayer ?\n                Optional.of(((SslTransportLayer) transportLayer).sslSession()) : Optional.empty();\n        SaslAuthenticationContext context = new SaslAuthenticationContext(saslServer, securityProtocol,\n                clientAddress(), listenerName.value(), sslSession);\n        KafkaPrincipal principal = principalBuilder.build(context);\n        if (ScramMechanism.isScram(saslMechanism) && Boolean.parseBoolean((String) saslServer.getNegotiatedProperty(ScramLoginModule.TOKEN_AUTH_CONFIG))) {\n            principal.tokenAuthenticated(true);\n        }\n        return principal;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Optional<KafkaPrincipalSerde> principalSerde() {\n        return principalBuilder instanceof KafkaPrincipalSerde ? Optional.of((KafkaPrincipalSerde) principalBuilder) : Optional.empty();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean complete() {\n        return saslState == SaslState.COMPLETE;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void handleAuthenticationFailure() throws IOException {\n        sendAuthenticationFailureResponse();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws IOException {\n        if (principalBuilder instanceof Closeable)\n            Utils.closeQuietly((Closeable) principalBuilder, \"principal builder\");\n        if (saslServer != null)\n            saslServer.dispose();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void reauthenticate(ReauthenticationContext reauthenticationContext) throws IOException {\n        NetworkReceive saslHandshakeReceive = reauthenticationContext.networkReceive();\n        if (saslHandshakeReceive == null)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n                    \"Invalid saslHandshakeReceive in server-side re-authentication context: null\");\n        SaslServerAuthenticator previousSaslServerAuthenticator = (SaslServerAuthenticator) reauthenticationContext.previousAuthenticator();\n        reauthInfo.reauthenticating(previousSaslServerAuthenticator.saslMechanism,\n                previousSaslServerAuthenticator.principal(), reauthenticationContext.reauthenticationBeginNanos());\n        previousSaslServerAuthenticator.close();\n        netInBuffer = saslHandshakeReceive;\n        LOG.debug(\"Beginning re-authentication: {}\", this);\n        netInBuffer.payload().rewind();\n        setSaslState(SaslState.REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE);\n        authenticate();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Long serverSessionExpirationTimeNanos() {\n        return reauthInfo.sessionExpirationTimeNanos;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Long reauthenticationLatencyMs() {\n        return reauthInfo.reauthenticationLatencyMs();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean connectedClientSupportsReauthentication() {\n        return reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication;\n    }\n\n    private void setSaslState(SaslState saslState) {\n        setSaslState(saslState, null);\n    }\n\n    private void setSaslState(SaslState saslState, AuthenticationException exception) {\n        if (netOutBuffer != null && !netOutBuffer.completed()) {\n            pendingSaslState = saslState;\n            pendingException = exception;\n        } else {\n            this.saslState = saslState;\n            LOG.debug(\"Set SASL server state to {} during {}\", saslState, reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText());\n            this.pendingSaslState = null;\n            this.pendingException = null;\n            if (exception != null)\n                throw exception;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private boolean flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps() throws IOException {\n        boolean flushedCompletely = flushNetOutBuffer();\n        if (flushedCompletely) {\n            transportLayer.removeInterestOps(SelectionKey.OP_WRITE);\n            if (pendingSaslState != null)\n                setSaslState(pendingSaslState, pendingException);\n        } else\n            transportLayer.addInterestOps(SelectionKey.OP_WRITE);\n        return flushedCompletely;\n    }\n\n    private boolean flushNetOutBuffer() throws IOException {\n        if (!netOutBuffer.completed())\n            netOutBuffer.writeTo(transportLayer);\n        return netOutBuffer.completed();\n    }\n\n    private InetAddress serverAddress() {\n        return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getLocalAddress();\n    }\n\n    private InetAddress clientAddress() {\n        return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getInetAddress();\n    }\n\n    private int clientPort() {\n        return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getPort();"
}

            saslServer = createSaslKerberosServer(callbackHandler, configs, subject);\\n206:         } else {\\n207:             try {\\n208:                 saslServer = }\\n216:         }\\n217:     }\\n218: \\n219:     private SaslServer createSaslKerberosServer(final AuthenticateCallbackHandler saslServerCallbackHandler, final Map<String, ?> configs, Subject subject) throws IOException {\\n220:         // server is using a JAAS-authenticated subject: determine service principal name and hostname from kafka server's subject.\\n221:         final String servicePrincipal = SaslClientAuthenticator.firstPrincipal(subject);\\n222:         KerberosName kerberosName;\\n223:         try {\\n224:             kerberosName = KerberosName.parse(servicePrincipal);\\n225:         } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\\n226:             throw new KafkaException(\\\"Principal has name with unexpected format \\\" + servicePrincipal);\\n227:         }\\n228:         final String servicePrincipalName = kerberosName.serviceName();\\n229:         final String serviceHostname = kerberosName.hostName();\\n230: \\n231:         LOG.debug(\\\"Creating SaslServer for {} with mechanism {}\\\", kerberosName, saslMechanism);\\n232: \\n233:         try {\\n234:             return SecurityManagerCompatibility.get().callAs(subject, () ->\\n235:                     Sasl.createSaslServer(saslMechanism, servicePrincipalName, serviceHostname, configs, saslServerCallbackHandler));\\n236:         } catch (CompletionException e) {\\n237:             throw new SaslException(\\\"Kafka Server failed to create a SaslServer to interact with a client during session authentication\\\", e.getCause());\\n238:         }\\n239:     }\\n240: \\n241:     /**\\n242:      * Evaluates client responses via `SaslServer.evaluateResponse` and returns the issued challenge to the client until\\n243:      * authentication succeeds or fails.\\n244:      *\\n245:      * The messages are sent and received as size delimited bytes that consists of a 4 byte network-ordered size N\\n246:      * followed by N bytes representing the opaque payload.\\n247:      */\\n248:     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"fallthrough\\\")\\n249:     @Override\\n250:     public void authenticate() throws IOException {\\n251:         if (saslState != SaslState.REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE) {\\n252:             if (netOutBuffer != null && !flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps())\\n253:                 return;\\n254: \\n255:             if (saslServer != null && saslServer.isComplete()) {\\n256:                 setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\\n257:                 return;\\n258:             }\\n259: \\n260:             // allocate on heap (as opposed to any socket server memory pool)\\n261:             if (netInBuffer == null) netInBuffer = new NetworkReceive(saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize, connectionId);\\n262: \\n263:             try {\\n264:                 netInBuffer.readFrom(transportLayer);\\n265:             } catch (InvalidReceiveException e) {\\n266:                 throw new SaslAuthenticationException(\\\"Failing SASL authentication due to invalid receive size\\\", e);\\n267:             }\\n268:             if (!netInBuffer.complete())\\n269:                 return;\\n270:             netInBuffer.payload().rewind();\\n271: reset the networkReceive as we read all the data.\\n275:         try {\\n276:             switch (saslState) {\\n277:                 case REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE:\\n278:                 case HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST:\\n279:                 case HANDSHAKE_REQUEST:\\n280:                 case INITIAL_REQUEST:\\n281:                     handleKafkaRequest(clientToken);\\n282:                     break;\\n283:                 case REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM:\\n284:                     throw new SaslAuthenticationException(reauthInfo.badMechanismErrorMessage);\\n285:                 case AUTHENTICATE:\\n286:                     handleSaslToken(clientToken);\\n287:                     // When the authentication exchange is complete and no more tokens are expected from the client,\\n288:                     // update SASL state. Current SASL state will be updated when outgoing writes to the client complete.\\n289:                     if (saslServer.isComplete())\\n290:                         setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\\n291:                     break;\\n292:                 default:\\n293:                     break;\\n294:             }\\n295:         } catch (AuthenticationException e) {\\n296:             // Exception will be propagated after response is sent to client\\n297:             setSaslState(SaslState.FAILED, e);\\n298:         } catch (Exception e) {\\n299:             // In the case of IOExceptions and other unexpected exceptions, fail immediately\\n300:             saslState = SaslState.FAILED;\\n301:             LOG.debug(\\\"Failed during {}: {}\\\", reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText(), e.getMessage());\\n302:             throw e;\\n303:         }\\n304:     }\\n305: \\n306:     @Override\\n307:     public KafkaPrincipal principal() {\\n308:         Optional<SSLSession> sslSession = transportLayer instanceof SslTransportLayer ?\\n309:                 Optional.of(((SslTransportLayer) transportLayer).sslSession()) : Optional.empty();\\n310:         SaslAuthenticationContext context = new SaslAuthenticationContext(saslServer, securityProtocol,\\n311:                 clientAddress(), listenerName.value(), sslSession);\\n312:         KafkaPrincipal principal = principalBuilder.build(context);\\n313:         if (ScramMechanism.isScram(saslMechanism) && Boolean.parseBoolean((String) saslServer.getNegotiatedProperty(ScramLoginModule.TOKEN_AUTH_CONFIG))) {\\n314:             principal.tokenAuthenticated(true);\\n315:         }\\n316:         return principal;\\n317:     }\\n318: \\n319:     @Override\\n320:     public Optional<KafkaPrincipalSerde> principalSerde() {\\n321:         return principalBuilder instanceof KafkaPrincipalSerde ? Optional.of((KafkaPrincipalSerde) principalBuilder) : Optional.empty();\\n322:     }\\n323: \\n324:     @Override\\n325:     public boolean complete() {\\n326:         return saslState == SaslState.COMPLETE;\\n327:     }\\n328: \\n329:     @Override\\n330:     public void handleAuthenticationFailure() throws IOException {\\n331:         sendAuthenticationFailureResponse();\\n332:     }\\n333: \\n334:     @Override\\n335:     public void close() throws IOException {\\n336:         if (principalBuilder instanceof Closeable)\\n337:             Utils.closeQuietly((Closeable) principalBuilder, \\\"principal builder\\\");\\n338:         if (saslServer != null)\\n339:             saslServer.dispose();\\n340:     }\\n341: \\n342:     @Override\\n343:     public void reauthenticate(ReauthenticationContext reauthenticationContext) throws IOException {\\n344:         NetworkReceive saslHandshakeReceive = reauthenticationContext.networkReceive();\\n345:         if null\\\");\\n348:         SaslServerAuthenticator previousSaslServerAuthenticator = (SaslServerAuthenticator) reauthenticationContext.previousAuthenticator();\\n349: reauthenticationContext.reauthenticationBeginNanos());\\n351:         previousSaslServerAuthenticator.close();\\n352:         netInBuffer = saslHandshakeReceive;\\n353:         LOG.debug(\\\"Beginning createSaslKerberosServer(final AuthenticateCallbackHandler saslServerCallbackHandler, final Map<String, ?> configs, Subject subject) throws IOException {\n220:         // server is using a JAAS-authenticated subject: determine service principal name and hostname from kafka server's subject.\n221:         final String servicePrincipal = (netInBuffer == null) netInBuffer = new NetworkReceive(saslAuthRequestMaxReceiveSize, connectionId);\n262: \n263:             try {\n264:                 netInBuffer.readFrom(transportLayer);\n265: data.\n275:         try {\n276:             switch (saslState) {\n277:                 case REAUTH_PROCESS_HANDSHAKE:\n278:                 case HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST:\n279: REAUTH_BAD_MECHANISM:\n284:                     throw new SaslAuthenticationException(reauthInfo.badMechanismErrorMessage);\n285:                 case AUTHENTICATE:\n286:                     handleSaslToken(clientToken);\n287:                     // When the authentication exchange is complete and no more tokens are expected from the client,\n288:                     // update SASL state. Current SASL state will be updated when outgoing writes to the client complete.\n289:                     if (saslServer.isComplete())\n290:                         setSaslState(SaslState.COMPLETE);\n291:                     break;\n292:                 default:\n293:                     break;\n294:             }\n295:         } catch (AuthenticationException e) {\n296:             // Exception will be propagated after response is sent to client\n297:             setSaslState(SaslState.FAILED, e);\n298:         } catch (Exception e) {\n299:             // In the case of IOExceptions and other unexpected exceptions, fail immediately\n300:             saslState = SaslState.FAILED;\n301:             LOG.debug(\"Failed during {}: {}\", reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText(), e.getMessage());\n302:             throw e;\n303:         }\n304:     }\n305: \n306:     @Override\n307:     public KafkaPrincipal principal() {\n308:         Optional<SSLSession> sslSession = transportLayer instanceof SslTransportLayer ?\n309:                 Optional.of(((SslTransportLayer) transportLayer).sslSession()) : Optional.empty();\n310:         SaslAuthenticationContext context = new SaslAuthenticationContext(saslServer, securityProtocol,\n311:                 clientAddress(), listenerName.value(), sslSession);\n312:         KafkaPrincipal principal = principalBuilder.build(context);\n313:         if (ScramMechanism.isScram(saslMechanism) && Boolean.parseBoolean((String) saslServer.getNegotiatedProperty(ScramLoginModule.TOKEN_AUTH_CONFIG))) {\n314:             principal.tokenAuthenticated(true);\n315:         }\n316:         return principal;\n317:     }\n318: \n319:     @Override\n320:     public Optional<KafkaPrincipalSerde> principalSerde() {\n321:         return principalBuilder instanceof KafkaPrincipalSerde ?   "stdout": "        return transportLayer.socketChannel().socket().getPort();\n    }\n\n    private void handleSaslToken(byte[] clientToken) throws IOException {\n        if (!enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders) {\n            byte[] response = saslServer.evaluateResponse(clientToken);\n            if (saslServer.isComplete()) {\n                reauthInfo.calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs();\n                if (reauthInfo.reauthenticating())\n                    reauthInfo.ensurePrincipalUnchanged(principal());\n            }\n            if (response != null) {\n                netOutBuffer = ByteBufferSend.sizePrefixed(ByteBuffer.wrap(response));\n                flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\n            }\n        } else {\n            ByteBuffer requestBuffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(clientToken);\n            RequestHeader header = RequestHeader.parse(requestBuffer);\n            ApiKeys apiKey = header.apiKey();\n            short version = header.apiVersion();\n            RequestContext requestContext = new RequestContext(header, connectionId, clientAddress(), Optional.of(clientPort()),\n                    KafkaPrincipal.ANONYMOUS, listenerName, securityProtocol, ClientInformation.EMPTY, false);\n            RequestAndSize requestAndSize = requestContext.parseRequest(requestBuffer);\n            if (apiKey != ApiKeys.SASL_AUTHENTICATE) {\n                IllegalSaslStateException e = new IllegalSaslStateException(\"Unexpected Kafka request of type \" + apiKey + \" during SASL authentication.\");\n                buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext, requestAndSize.request.getErrorResponse(e));\n                throw e;\n            }\n            if (!apiKey.isVersionSupported(version)) {\n                // We cannot create an error response if the request version of SaslAuthenticate is not supported\n                // This should not normally occur since clients typically check supported versions using ApiVersionsRequest\n                throw new UnsupportedVersionException(\"Version \" + version + \" is not supported for apiKey \" + apiKey);\n            }\n            /*\n             * The client sends multiple SASL_AUTHENTICATE requests, and the client is known\n             * to support the required version if any one of them indicates it supports that\n             * version.\n             */\n            if (!reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication)\n                reauthInfo.connectedClientSupportsReauthentication = version > 0;\n            SaslAuthenticateRequest saslAuthenticateRequest = (SaslAuthenticateRequest) requestAndSize.request;\n\n            try {\n                byte[] responseToken = saslServer.evaluateResponse(\n                        Utils.copyArray(saslAuthenticateRequest.data().authBytes()));\n                if (reauthInfo.reauthenticating() && saslServer.isComplete())\n                    reauthInfo.ensurePrincipalUnchanged(principal());\n                // For versions with SASL_AUTHENTICATE header, send a response to SASL_AUTHENTICATE request even if token is empty.\n                byte[] responseBytes = responseToken == null ? new byte[0] : responseToken;\n                long sessionLifetimeMs = !saslServer.isComplete() ? 0L\n                        : reauthInfo.calcCompletionTimesAndReturnSessionLifetimeMs();\n                sendKafkaResponse(requestContext, new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n                        new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n                        .setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code())\n                        .setAuthBytes(responseBytes)\n                        .setSessionLifetimeMs(sessionLifetimeMs)));\n            } catch (SaslAuthenticationException e) {\n                buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext,\n                        new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n                                new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n                                .setErrorCode(Errors.SASL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.code())\n                                .setErrorMessage(e.getMessage())));\n                throw e;\n            } catch (SaslException e) {\n                KerberosError kerberosError = KerberosError.fromException(e);\n                if (kerberosError != null && kerberosError.retriable()) {\n                    // Handle retriable Kerberos exceptions as I/O exceptions rather than authentication exceptions\n                    throw e;\n                } else {\n                    // DO NOT include error message from the `SaslException` in the client response since it may\n                    // contain sensitive data like the existence of the user.\n                    String errorMessage = \"Authentication failed during \"\n                            + reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText()\n                            + \" due to invalid credentials with SASL mechanism \" + saslMechanism;\n                    buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(requestContext, new SaslAuthenticateResponse(\n                            new SaslAuthenticateResponseData()\n                            .setErrorCode(Errors.SASL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.code())\n                            .setErrorMessage(errorMessage)));\n                    throw new SaslAuthenticationException(errorMessage, e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private boolean handleKafkaRequest(byte[] requestBytes) throws IOException, AuthenticationException {\n        boolean isKafkaRequest = false;\n        String clientMechanism = null;\n        try {\n            ByteBuffer requestBuffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(requestBytes);\n            RequestHeader header = RequestHeader.parse(requestBuffer);\n            ApiKeys apiKey = header.apiKey();\n\n            // A valid Kafka request header was received. SASL authentication tokens are now expected only\n            // following a SaslHandshakeRequest since this is not a GSSAPI client token from a Kafka 0.9.0.x client.\n            if (saslState == SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST)\n                setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST);\n            isKafkaRequest = true;\n\n            // Raise an error prior to parsing if the api cannot be handled at this layer. This avoids\n            // unnecessary exposure to some of the more complex schema types.\n            if (apiKey != ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS && apiKey != ApiKeys.SASL_HANDSHAKE)\n                throw new IllegalSaslStateException(\"Unexpected Kafka request of type \" + apiKey + \" during SASL handshake.\");\n\n            LOG.debug(\"Handling Kafka request {} during {}\", apiKey, reauthInfo.authenticationOrReauthenticationText());\n\n\n            RequestContext requestContext = new RequestContext(header, connectionId, clientAddress(), Optional.of(clientPort()),\n                    KafkaPrincipal.ANONYMOUS, listenerName, securityProtocol, ClientInformation.EMPTY, false);\n            RequestAndSize requestAndSize = requestContext.parseRequest(requestBuffer);\n            if (apiKey == ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS)\n                handleApiVersionsRequest(requestContext, (ApiVersionsRequest) requestAndSize.request);\n            else\n                clientMechanism = handleHandshakeRequest(requestContext, (SaslHandshakeRequest) requestAndSize.request);\n        } catch (InvalidRequestException e) {\n            if (saslState == SaslState.INITIAL_REQUEST) {\n                // InvalidRequestException is thrown if the request is not in Kafka format or if the API key\n                // is invalid. For compatibility with 0.9.0.x where the first packet is a GSSAPI token\n                // starting with 0x60, revert to GSSAPI for both these exceptions.\n                if (LOG.isDebugEnabled()) {\n                    StringBuilder tokenBuilder = new StringBuilder();\n                    for (byte b : requestBytes) {\n                        tokenBuilder.append(String.format(\"%02x\", b));\n                        if (tokenBuilder.length() >= 20)\n                             break;\n                    }\n                    LOG.debug(\"Received client packet of length {} starting with bytes 0x{}, process as GSSAPI packet\", requestBytes.length, tokenBuilder);\n                }\n                if (enabledMechanisms.contains(SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM)) {\n                    LOG.debug(\"First client packet is not a SASL mechanism request, using default mechanism GSSAPI\");\n                    clientMechanism = SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM;\n                } else\n                    throw new UnsupportedSaslMechanismException(\"Exception handling first SASL packet from client, GSSAPI is not supported by server\", e);\n            } else\n                throw e;\n        }\n        if (clientMechanism != null && (!reauthInfo.reauthenticating()\n                || reauthInfo.saslMechanismUnchanged(clientMechanism))) {\n            createSaslServer(clientMechanism);\n            setSaslState(SaslState.AUTHENTICATE);\n        }\n        return isKafkaRequest;\n    }\n\n    private String handleHandshakeRequest(RequestContext context, SaslHandshakeRequest handshakeRequest) throws IOException, UnsupportedSaslMechanismException {\n        String clientMechanism = handshakeRequest.data().mechanism();\n        short version = context.header.apiVersion();\n        if (version >= 1)\n            this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(true);\n        if (enabledMechanisms.contains(clientMechanism)) {\n            LOG.debug(\"Using SASL mechanism '{}' provided by client\", clientMechanism);\n            sendKafkaResponse(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\n                    new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\n            return clientMechanism;\n        } else {\n            LOG.debug(\"SASL mechanism '{}' requested by client is not supported\", clientMechanism);\n            buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\n                    new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.UNSUPPORTED_SASL_MECHANISM.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\n            throw new UnsupportedSaslMechanismException(\"Unsupported SASL mechanism \" + clientMechanism);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Visible to override for testing\n    protected void enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(boolean flag) {\n        this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders = flag;\n    }\n\n    private void handleApiVersionsRequest(RequestContext context, ApiVersionsRequest apiVersionsRequest) throws IOException {\n        if (saslState != SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST)\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Unexpected ApiVersions request received during SASL authentication state \" + saslState);\n\n        if (apiVersionsRequest.hasUnsupportedRequestVersion())\n            sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.UNSUPPORTED_VERSION.exception()));\n        else if (!apiVersionsRequest.isValid())\n            sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.INVALID_REQUEST.exception()));\n        else {\n            metadataRegistry.registerClientInformation(new ClientInformation(apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareName(),\n                apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareVersion()));\n            sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionSupplier.get());\n            setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_REQUEST);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Build a {@link Send} response on {@link #authenticate()} failure. The actual response is sent out when\n     * {@link #sendAuthenticationFailureResponse()} is called.\n     */\n    private void buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) {\n        authenticationFailureSend = context.buildResponseSend(response);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Send any authentication failure response that may have been previously built.\n     */\n    private void sendAuthenticationFailureResponse() throws IOException {\n        if (authenticationFailureSend == null)\n            return;\n        sendKafkaResponse(authenticationFailureSend);\n        authenticationFailureSend = null;\n    }\n\n    private void sendKafkaResponse(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) throws IOException {"
}

clientMechanism;\n559:         } else {\n560:             LOG.debug(\"SASL mechanism '{}' requested by client is not supported\", clientMechanism);\n561:             buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(context, new SaslHandshakeResponse(\n562:                     new SaslHandshakeResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.UNSUPPORTED_SASL_MECHANISM.code()).setMechanisms(enabledMechanisms)));\n563:             throw new UnsupportedSaslMechanismException(\"Unsupported SASL mechanism \" + clientMechanism);\n564:         }\n565:     }\n566: \n567:     // Visible to override for testing\n568:     protected void enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders(boolean flag) {\n569:         this.enableKafkaSaslAuthenticateHeaders = flag;\n570:     }\n571: \n572:     private void handleApiVersionsRequest(RequestContext context, ApiVersionsRequest apiVersionsRequest) throws IOException {\n573:         if (saslState != SaslState.HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST)\n574:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Unexpected ApiVersions request received during SASL authentication state \" + saslState);\n575: \n576:         if (apiVersionsRequest.hasUnsupportedRequestVersion())\n577:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.UNSUPPORTED_VERSION.exception()));\n578:         else if (!apiVersionsRequest.isValid())\n579:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionsRequest.getErrorResponse(0, Errors.INVALID_REQUEST.exception()));\n580:         else {\n581:             metadataRegistry.registerClientInformation(new ClientInformation(apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareName(),\n582:                 apiVersionsRequest.data().clientSoftwareVersion()));\n583:             sendKafkaResponse(context, apiVersionSupplier.apply(apiVersionsRequest.version()));\n584:             setSaslState(SaslState.HANDSHAKE_REQUEST);\n585:         }\n586:     }\n587: \n588:     /**\n589:      * Build a {@link Send} response on {@link #authenticate()} failure. The actual response is sent out when\n590:      * {@link #sendAuthenticationFailureResponse()} is called.\n591:      */\n592:     private void buildResponseOnAuthenticateFailure(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) {\n593:         authenticationFailureSend = context.buildResponseSend(response);\n594:     }\n595: \n596:     /**\n597:      * Send any authentication failure response that may have been previously built.\n598:      */\n599:     private void sendAuthenticationFailureResponse() throws IOException {\n600:         if (authenticationFailureSend == null)\n601:             return;\n602:         sendKafkaResponse(authenticationFailureSend);\n603:         authenticationFailureSend = null;\n604:     }\n605: \n606:     private void sendKafkaResponse(RequestContext context, AbstractResponse response) throws IOException {\n607:         sendKafkaResponse(context.buildResponseSend(response));\n608:     }\n609: \n610:     private void sendKafkaResponse(Send send) throws IOException {\n611:         netOutBuffer = send;\n612:         flushNetOutBufferAndUpdateInterestOps();\n613:     }\n614: \n615:     /**\n616:      * Information related to re-authentication\n617:      */\n618:     private class ReauthInfo {\n619:         public String previousSaslMechanism;\n620:         public KafkaPrincipal previousKafkaPrincipal;\n621: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--be816b82@7bb066906e7979c9d10cdc924186dfe3f49a9428/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java%3FL420-620"
  }
}

org.mockito.Mockito.verify;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\n\npublic class SaslServerAuthenticatorTest {\n\n    private final String clientId = \"clientId\";\n    \n    @Test\n    public void testOversizeRequest() throws IOException {\n        TransportLayer transportLayer = mock(TransportLayer.class);\n        Map<String, ?> configs = Collections.singletonMap(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG,\n                Collections.singletonList(SCRAM_SHA_256.mechanismName()));\n        SaslServerAuthenticator authenticator = setupAuthenticator(configs, transportLayer,\n                SCRAM_SHA_256.mechanismName(), new DefaultChannelMetadataRegistry());\n\n        when(transportLayer.read(any(ByteBuffer.class))).then(invocation -> {\n            invocation.<ByteBuffer>getArgument(0).putInt(BrokerSecurityConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_SERVER_MAX_RECEIVE_SIZE + 1);\n            return 4;\n        });\n        assertThrows(SaslAuthenticationException.class, authenticator::authenticate);\n        verify(transportLayer).read(any(ByteBuffer.class));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testUnexpectedRequestType() throws IOException {\n        TransportLayer transportLayer = mock(TransportLayer.class);\n        Map<String, ?> configs = Collections.singletonMap(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG,\n                Collections.singletonList(SCRAM_SHA_256.mechanismName()));\n        SaslServerAuthenticator authenticator = setupAuthenticator(configs, transportLayer,\n                SCRAM_SHA_256.mechanismName(), new DefaultChannelMetadataRegistry());\n\n        RequestHeader header = new RequestHeader(ApiKeys.METADATA, (short) 0, clientId, 13243);\n        ByteBuffer headerBuffer = RequestTestUtils.serializeRequestHeader(header);\n\n        when(transportLayer.read(any(ByteBuffer.class))).then(invocation -> {\n            invocation.<ByteBuffer>getArgument(0).putInt(headerBuffer.remaining());\n            return 4;\n        }).then(invocation -> {\n            // serialize only the request header. the authenticator should not parse beyond this\n            invocation.<ByteBuffer>getArgument(0).put(headerBuffer.duplicate());\n            return headerBuffer.remaining();\n        });\n\n        try {\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n            fail(\"Expected authenticate() to raise an exception\");\n        } catch (IllegalSaslStateException e) {\n            // expected exception\n        }\n\n        verify(transportLayer, times(2)).read(any(ByteBuffer.class));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testOldestApiVersionsRequest() throws IOException {\n        testApiVersionsRequest(ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS.oldestVersion(),\n                ClientInformation.UNKNOWN_NAME_OR_VERSION, ClientInformation.UNKNOWN_NAME_OR_VERSION);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testLatestApiVersionsRequest() throws IOException {\n        testApiVersionsRequest(ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS.latestVersion(),\n                \"apache-kafka-java\", AppInfoParser.getVersion());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testSessionExpiresAtTokenExpiryDespiteNoReauthIsSet() throws IOException {\n        String mechanism = OAuthBearerLoginModule.OAUTHBEARER_MECHANISM;\n        Duration tokenExpirationDuration = Duration.ofSeconds(1);\n        SaslServer saslServer = mock(SaslServer.class);\n\n        MockTime time = new MockTime();\n        try (\n                MockedStatic<?> ignored = mockSaslServer(saslServer, mechanism, time, tokenExpirationDuration);\n                MockedStatic<?> ignored2 = mockKafkaPrincipal(\"[principal-type]\", \"[principal-name\");\n                TransportLayer transportLayer = mockTransportLayer()\n        ) {\n\n            SaslServerAuthenticator authenticator = getSaslServerAuthenticatorForOAuth(mechanism, transportLayer, time, 0L);\n\n            mockRequest(saslHandshakeRequest(mechanism), transportLayer);\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n\n            when(saslServer.isComplete()).thenReturn(false).thenReturn(true);\n            mockRequest(saslAuthenticateRequest(), transportLayer);\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n\n            long atTokenExpiryNanos = time.nanoseconds() + tokenExpirationDuration.toNanos();\n            assertEquals(atTokenExpiryNanos, authenticator.serverSessionExpirationTimeNanos());\n\n            ByteBuffer secondResponseSent = getResponses(transportLayer).get(1);\n            consumeSizeAndHeader(secondResponseSent);\n            SaslAuthenticateResponse response = SaslAuthenticateResponse.parse(secondResponseSent, (short) 2);\n            assertEquals(tokenExpirationDuration.toMillis(), response.sessionLifetimeMs());\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    public void testSessionExpiresAtMaxReauthTime() throws IOException {\n        String mechanism = OAuthBearerLoginModule.OAUTHBEARER_MECHANISM;\n        SaslServer saslServer = mock(SaslServer.class);\n        MockTime time = new MockTime(0, 1, 1000);\n        long maxReauthMs = 100L;\n        Duration tokenExpiryGreaterThanMaxReauth = Duration.ofMillis(maxReauthMs).multipliedBy(10);\n\n        try (\n                MockedStatic<?> ignored = mockSaslServer(saslServer, mechanism, time, tokenExpiryGreaterThanMaxReauth);\n                MockedStatic<?> ignored2 = mockKafkaPrincipal(\"[principal-type]\", \"[principal-name\");\n                TransportLayer transportLayer = mockTransportLayer()\n        ) {\n\n            SaslServerAuthenticator authenticator = getSaslServerAuthenticatorForOAuth(mechanism, transportLayer, time, maxReauthMs);\n\n            mockRequest(saslHandshakeRequest(mechanism), transportLayer);\n            authenticator.authenticate();\n\n            when(saslServer.isComplete()).thenReturn(false).thenReturn(true);"
}

limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals;\n\nimport java.security.InvalidKeyException;\nimport java.security.MessageDigest;\nimport java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;\nimport java.util.Arrays;\nimport java.util.Collection;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Set;\n\nimport javax.security.auth.callback.Callback;\nimport javax.security.auth.callback.CallbackHandler;\nimport javax.security.auth.callback.NameCallback;\nimport javax.security.sasl.SaslException;\nimport javax.security.sasl.SaslServer;\nimport javax.security.sasl.SaslServerFactory;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.IllegalSaslStateException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.SaslAuthenticationException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator.SaslInternalConfigs;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.ScramCredential;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.ScramCredentialCallback;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.ScramLoginModule;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals.ScramMessages.ClientFinalMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals.ScramMessages.ClientFirstMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals.ScramMessages.ServerFinalMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.internals.ScramMessages.ServerFirstMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.security.token.delegation.internals.DelegationTokenCredentialCallback;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\nimport org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n\n/**\n * SaslServer implementation for SASL/SCRAM. This server is configured with a callback\n * handler for integration with a credential manager. Kafka brokers provide callbacks\n * based on a Zookeeper-based password store.\n *\n * @see <a href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5802\">RFC 5802</a>\n */\npublic class ScramSaslServer implements SaslServer {\n\n    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ScramSaslServer.class);\n    private static final Set<String> SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS = Utils.mkSet(ScramLoginModule.TOKEN_AUTH_CONFIG);\n\n    enum State {\n        RECEIVE_CLIENT_FIRST_MESSAGE,\n        RECEIVE_CLIENT_FINAL_MESSAGE,\n        COMPLETE,\n        FAILED\n    }\n\n    private final ScramMechanism mechanism;\n    private final ScramFormatter formatter;\n    private final CallbackHandler callbackHandler;\n    private State state;\n    private String username;\n    private ClientFirstMessage clientFirstMessage;\n    private ServerFirstMessage serverFirstMessage;\n    private ScramExtensions scramExtensions;\n    private ScramCredential scramCredential;\n    private String authorizationId;\n    private Long tokenExpiryTimestamp;\n\n    public ScramSaslServer(ScramMechanism mechanism, Map<String, ?> props, CallbackHandler callbackHandler) throws NoSuchAlgorithmException {\n        this.mechanism = mechanism;\n        this.formatter = new ScramFormatter(mechanism);\n        this.callbackHandler = callbackHandler;\n        setState(State.RECEIVE_CLIENT_FIRST_MESSAGE);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @throws SaslAuthenticationException if the requested authorization id is not the same as username.\n     * <p>\n     * <b>Note:</b> This method may throw {@link SaslAuthenticationException} to provide custom error messages\n     * to clients. But care should be taken to avoid including any information in the exception message that\n     * should not be leaked to unauthenticated clients. It may be safer to throw {@link SaslException} in\n     * most cases so that a standard error message is returned to clients.\n     * </p>\n     */\n    @Override\n    public byte[] evaluateResponse(byte[] response) throws SaslException, SaslAuthenticationException {\n        try {\n            switch (state) {\n                case RECEIVE_CLIENT_FIRST_MESSAGE:\n                    this.clientFirstMessage = new ClientFirstMessage(response);\n                    this.scramExtensions = clientFirstMessage.extensions();\n                    if (!SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS.containsAll(scramExtensions.map().keySet())) {\n                        log.debug(\"Unsupported extensions will be ignored, supported {}, provided {}\",\n                                SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS, scramExtensions.map().keySet());\n                    }\n                    String serverNonce = formatter.secureRandomString();\n                    try {\n                        String saslName = clientFirstMessage.saslName();\n                        this.username = ScramFormatter.username(saslName);\n                        NameCallback nameCallback = new NameCallback(\"username\", username);\n                        ScramCredentialCallback credentialCallback;\n                        if (scramExtensions.tokenAuthenticated()) {\n                            DelegationTokenCredentialCallback tokenCallback = new DelegationTokenCredentialCallback();\n                            credentialCallback = tokenCallback;\n                            callbackHandler.handle(new Callback[]{nameCallback, tokenCallback});\n                            if (tokenCallback.tokenOwner() == null)\n                                throw new SaslException(\"Token Authentication failed: Invalid tokenId : \" + username);\n                            this.authorizationId = tokenCallback.tokenOwner();\n                            this.tokenExpiryTimestamp = tokenCallback.tokenExpiryTimestamp();\n                        } else {\n                            credentialCallback = new ScramCredentialCallback();\n                            callbackHandler.handle(new Callback[]{nameCallback, credentialCallback});\n                            this.authorizationId = username;\n                            this.tokenExpiryTimestamp = null;\n                        }\n                        this.scramCredential = credentialCallback.scramCredential();\n                        if (scramCredential == null)\n                            throw new SaslException(\"Authentication failed: Invalid user credentials\");\n                        String authorizationIdFromClient = clientFirstMessage.authorizationId();\n                        if (!authorizationIdFromClient.isEmpty() && !authorizationIdFromClient.equals(username))\n                            throw new SaslAuthenticationException(\"Authentication failed: Client requested an authorization id that is different from username\");\n\n                        if (scramCredential.iterations() < mechanism.minIterations())\n                            throw new SaslException(\"Iterations \" + scramCredential.iterations() +  \" is less than the minimum \" + mechanism.minIterations() + \" for \" + mechanism);\n                        this.serverFirstMessage = new ServerFirstMessage(clientFirstMessage.nonce(),\n                                serverNonce,\n                                scramCredential.salt(),\n                                scramCredential.iterations());\n                        setState(State.RECEIVE_CLIENT_FINAL_MESSAGE);\n                        return serverFirstMessage.toBytes();\n                    } catch (SaslException | AuthenticationException e) {\n                        throw e;\n                    } catch (Throwable e) {\n                        throw new SaslException(\"Authentication failed: Credentials could not be obtained\", e);\n                    }\n\n                case RECEIVE_CLIENT_FINAL_MESSAGE:\n                    try {\n                        ClientFinalMessage clientFinalMessage = new ClientFinalMessage(response);\n                        verifyClientProof(clientFinalMessage);\n                        byte[] serverKey = scramCredential.serverKey();\n                        byte[] serverSignature = formatter.serverSignature(serverKey, clientFirstMessage, serverFirstMessage, clientFinalMessage);\n                        ServerFinalMessage serverFinalMessage = new ServerFinalMessage(null, serverSignature);\n                        clearCredentials();\n                        setState(State.COMPLETE);\n                        return serverFinalMessage.toBytes();\n                    } catch (InvalidKeyException e) {\n                        throw new SaslException(\"Authentication failed: Invalid client final message\", e);\n                    }\n\n                default:\n                    throw new IllegalSaslStateException(\"Unexpected challenge in Sasl server state \" + state);\n            }\n        } catch (SaslException | AuthenticationException e) {\n            clearCredentials();\n            setState(State.FAILED);\n            throw e;\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String getAuthorizationID() {\n        if (!isComplete())\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Authentication exchange has not completed\");\n        return authorizationId;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String getMechanismName() {\n        return mechanism.mechanismName();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Object getNegotiatedProperty(String propName) {\n        if (!isComplete())\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Authentication exchange has not completed\");\n        if (SaslInternalConfigs.CREDENTIAL_LIFETIME_MS_SASL_NEGOTIATED_PROPERTY_KEY.equals(propName))\n            return tokenExpiryTimestamp; // will be null if token not used\n        if (SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS.contains(propName))\n            return scramExtensions.map().get(propName);\n        else\n            return null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isComplete() {\n        return state == State.COMPLETE;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte[] unwrap(byte[] incoming, int offset, int len) {\n        if (!isComplete())\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Authentication exchange has not completed\");\n        return Arrays.copyOfRange(incoming, offset, offset + len);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte[] wrap(byte[] outgoing, int offset, int len) {\n        if (!isComplete())\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Authentication exchange has not completed\");\n        return Arrays.copyOfRange(outgoing, offset, offset + len);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void dispose() {\n    }\n\n    private void setState(State state) {\n        log.debug(\"Setting SASL/{} server state to {}\", mechanism, state);\n        this.state = state;\n    }\n\n    private void verifyClientProof(ClientFinalMessage clientFinalMessage) throws SaslException {\n        try {\n            byte[] expectedStoredKey = scramCredential.storedKey();\n            byte[] clientSignature = formatter.clientSignature(expectedStoredKey, clientFirstMessage, serverFirstMessage, clientFinalMessage);\n            byte[] computedStoredKey = formatter.storedKey(clientSignature, clientFinalMessage.proof());\n            if (!MessageDigest.isEqual(computedStoredKey, expectedStoredKey))\n                throw new SaslException(\"Invalid client credentials\");\n        } catch (InvalidKeyException e) {\n            throw new SaslException(\"Sasl client verification failed\", e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void clearCredentials() {\n        scramCredential = null;\n        clientFirstMessage = null;\n        serverFirstMessage = null;\n    }\n\n    public static class ScramSaslServerFactory implements SaslServerFactory {\n\n        @Override\n        public SaslServer createSaslServer(String mechanism, String protocol, String serverName, Map<String, ?> props, CallbackHandler cbh)\n            throws SaslException {\n\n            if (!ScramMechanism.isScram(mechanism)) {\n                throw new SaslException(String.format(\"Requested mechanism '%s' is not supported. Supported mechanisms are '%s'.\",\n                        mechanism, ScramMechanism.mechanismNames()));\n            }\n            try {\n                return new ScramSaslServer(ScramMechanism.forMechanismName(mechanism), props, cbh);\n            } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {\n                throw new SaslException(\"Hash algorithm not supported for mechanism \" + mechanism, e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String[] getMechanismNames(Map<String, ?> props) {\n            Collection<String> mechanisms = ScramMechanism.mechanismNames();\n            return mechanisms.toArray(new String[0]);\n        }\n    }\n}"
}

cache : oldCache;\\n30:     }\\n31: \\n32:     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n33:     public <C> Cache<C> cache(String mechanism, Class<C> credentialClass) {\\n34:         Cache<?> cache = cacheMap.get(mechanism);\\n35:         if (cache != null) {\\n36:             if (cache.credentialClass() != credentialClass)\\n37:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Invalid credential class \\\" + credentialClass + \\\", expected \\\" + cache.credentialClass());\\n38:             return (Cache<C>) cache;\\n39:         } else\\n40:             return null;\\n41:     }\\n42: \\n43:     public static class Cache<C> {\\n44:         private final Class<C> credentialClass;\\n45:         private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, C> credentials;\\n46: \\n47:         public Cache(Class<C> credentialClass) {\\n48:             this.credentialClass = credentialClass;\\n49:             this.credentials = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\\n50:         }\\n51: \\n52:         public C get(String username) {\\n53:             return credentials.get(username);\\n54:         }\\n55: \\n56:         public C put(String username, C credential) {\\n57:             return credentials.put(username, credential);\\n58:         }\\n59: \\n60:         public C remove(String username) {\\n61:             return credentials.remove(username);\\n62:         }\\n63: \\n64:         public Class<C> credentialClass() {\\n65:             return credentialClass;\\n66:         }\\n67:     }\\n68: }\\n69: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--be816b82@7bb066906e7979c9d10cdc924186dfe3f49a9428/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/CredentialCache.java\"}",
